math-cam-al-roots-poly.name: 多项式根
math-cam-al-roots-poly.subject: math
curriculum_points: use the relations between the symmetric functions of the roots of polynomial equations and the coefficients (for polynomial equations of degree 2, 3 and 4 only)

math-cam-al-rational-fn.name: 有理函数
math-cam-al-rational-fn.subject: math
curriculum_points: sketch graphs of rational functions of the form (ax+b)/(cx+d) and (ax^2+bx+c)/(dx^2+ex+f) (including the determination of oblique asymptotes)

math-cam-al-summation.name: 级数求和
math-cam-al-summation.subject: math
curriculum_points: use the method of differences to sum series, and use the formula for the sum of the first n natural numbers and the first n squares, and establish similar results for other series

math-cam-al-matrices-fm.name: 矩阵
math-cam-al-matrices-fm.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand the terms singular and non-singular as applied to square matrices
find the inverse of a non-singular 3 × 3 matrix by the method of adjoints
solve a system of three linear equations in three unknowns by matrix methods
understand the meaning of the terms eigenvalue and eigenvector, as applied to square matrices
find eigenvalues and eigenvectors of 2 × 2 and 3 × 3 matrices (restricted to cases where the eigenvalues are real and distinct)

math-cam-al-polar-coord.name: 极坐标
math-cam-al-polar-coord.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand the relations between cartesian and polar coordinates (using the convention r ≥ 0)
sketch curves with r given as a function of θ, including the use of trigonometric functions
find the area enclosed by a polar curve using the formula for the area of a sector

math-cam-al-vectors-fm.name: 向量进阶
math-cam-al-vectors-fm.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand the significance of all the symbols used when the equation of a straight line is expressed in the form r = a + tb
determine whether two lines are parallel, intersect or are skew
find the perpendicular distance from a point to a line, from a point to a plane, and between two skew lines
find the angle between two lines, between a line and a plane, and between two planes
find the intersection of a line and a plane, and of two or three planes

math-cam-al-proof-induction.name: 归纳证明
math-cam-al-proof-induction.subject: math
curriculum_points: use the method of mathematical induction to establish a given result (questions may involve divisibility tests, inequalities, sequences, recurrence relations, matrices, derivatives, integrals, etc.)

math-cam-al-complex-numbers.name: 复数
math-cam-al-complex-numbers.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand de Moivre's theorem, for a positive integer exponent, in terms of the geometrical effect of multiplication of complex numbers
use de Moivre's theorem for positive integer exponent
find and use the complex roots of unity
understand the geometrical effect of conjugating a complex number and of adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing two complex numbers
represent complex numbers geometrically by means of an Argand diagram
carry out operations of multiplication and division of two complex numbers expressed in polar form r(cos θ + i sin θ) ≡ re^(iθ)
find the two square roots of a complex number
understand in simple terms the geometrical effects of conjugating a complex number and of adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing two complex numbers
illustrate simple equations and inequalities involving complex numbers by means of loci in an Argand diagram (e.g. |z – a| < k, |z – a| = |z – b|, arg(z – a) = α)

math-cam-al-de-moivre.name: 棣莫弗定理
math-cam-al-de-moivre.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand de Moivre's theorem, for a positive integer exponent, in terms of the geometrical effect of multiplication of complex numbers
use de Moivre's theorem for positive integer exponent
find and use the complex roots of unity
carry out operations of multiplication and division of two complex numbers expressed in polar form r(cos θ + i sin θ) ≡ re^(iθ)

math-cam-al-diff-eq-fm.name: 微分方程
math-cam-al-diff-eq-fm.subject: math
curriculum_points: find an integrating factor for a first-order linear differential equation, and use an integrating factor to find the general solution
use a substitution to reduce a differential equation to a first order or to a linear form
find the general solution of a homogeneous second order linear differential equation with constant coefficients
find the general solution of a non-homogeneous second order linear differential equation with constant coefficients using an auxiliary equation and a particular integral
use initial conditions to find a particular solution to a differential equation, and interpret the solution in the context of a problem

math-cam-al-hyperbolic.name: 双曲函数
math-cam-al-hyperbolic.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand the definitions of hyperbolic functions sinh x, cosh x and tanh x, including their domains and ranges, and be able to sketch their graphs
differentiate and integrate hyperbolic functions
understand and be able to use the definitions of the inverse hyperbolic functions and their domains and ranges
derive and use the logarithmic forms of the inverse hyperbolic functions
integrate functions of the form (x^2 ± a^2)^(–1/2) and (a^2 – x^2)^(–1/2) and recognise the integrals of sinh^(–1)(x/a), cosh^(–1)(x/a) and tanh^(–1)(x/a)

math-cam-al-further-calc.name: 高级微积分
math-cam-al-further-calc.subject: math
curriculum_points: derive and use reduction formulae for the evaluation of definite integrals in simple cases
understand how to find the Maclaurin series of a function including the general term
use Maclaurin series to find limits, approximate values and solutions to equations
differentiate and integrate inverse trigonometric functions
integrate using partial fractions (extending to quadratic factors of the form ax^2 + c in the denominator, with b = 0)

math-cam-al-motion-plane.name: 平面运动
math-cam-al-motion-plane.subject: math
curriculum_points: use vector methods to model motion in a plane with constant velocity or constant acceleration
use Newton's second law in vector form, F = ma, to solve problems for motion in a plane
use the equation F = μR for friction, where μ is the coefficient of friction, including motion on an inclined plane

math-cam-al-circular-motion.name: 圆周运动
math-cam-al-circular-motion.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand the concept of angular speed for a particle moving in a circle, and use the relations v = rω
understand that the acceleration of a particle moving in a circle with constant speed is directed towards the centre of the circle, and use the formulae rω^2 and v^2/r
solve problems which can be modelled by the motion of a particle moving in a horizontal circle with constant speed

math-cam-al-elastic-collisions.name: 弹性碰撞
math-cam-al-elastic-collisions.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand the concept of the coefficient of restitution, and use it in solving problems involving direct impact of two smooth spheres or a smooth sphere with a fixed surface
use conservation of linear momentum and Newton's experimental law to solve problems involving the direct impact of two smooth spheres or a smooth sphere with a fixed surface

math-cam-al-momentum-fm.name: 动量与冲量
math-cam-al-momentum-fm.subject: math
curriculum_points: use vector methods to calculate impulse and momentum in two dimensions
use conservation of linear momentum and the principle of impulse and momentum in vector form to solve problems involving two particles colliding or a particle exploding into two parts

math-cam-al-shm.name: 简谐运动
math-cam-al-shm.subject: math
curriculum_points: understand simple harmonic motion and use the equations for displacement, velocity and acceleration in solving problems
use the formula ω^2 = n for SHM, where n is a constant
solve problems involving the simple pendulum or other systems that can be modelled as undergoing SHM

math-cam-al-continuous-dist.name: 连续分布
math-cam-al-continuous-dist.subject: math
math-cam-al-continuous-dist.domain: Further Prob & Stats / Further Probability & Statistics
curriculum_points: understand the concept of a continuous random variable and its probability density function, and use it to calculate probabilities
understand the meaning of the mean and variance of a continuous random variable, and use them to solve problems
use the probability density function of a continuous random variable to find its mean and variance
understand the concept of the cumulative distribution function and use it to find probabilities
use the cumulative distribution function to find the median, quartiles and other percentiles

math-cam-al-inference-fm.name: 假设检验与置信区间
math-cam-al-inference-fm.subject: math
math-cam-al-inference-fm.domain: Further Prob & Stats / Further Probability & Statistics
curriculum_points: understand the concept of a confidence interval for a population mean or proportion, and be able to calculate and interpret such intervals
understand the concept of a hypothesis test for a population mean or proportion, and be able to carry out such tests and interpret the results
understand the difference between one-tailed and two-tailed tests
understand the meaning of significance level and p-value
use the normal distribution or t-distribution as appropriate in hypothesis tests and confidence intervals

math-cam-al-chi-squared-fm.name: 卡方检验
math-cam-al-chi-squared-fm.subject: math
math-cam-al-chi-squared-fm.domain: Further Prob & Stats / Further Probability & Statistics
curriculum_points: understand the concept of a chi-squared test for goodness of fit and for independence in a contingency table
calculate expected frequencies for goodness of fit and for independence
calculate the chi-squared statistic and the appropriate degrees of freedom
use the chi-squared distribution tables to determine whether to accept or reject the null hypothesis
understand the conditions for the validity of chi-squared tests

math-cam-al-non-param-fm.name: 非参数检验
math-cam-al-non-param-fm.subject: math
math-cam-al-non-param-fm.domain: Further Prob & Stats / Further Probability & Statistics
curriculum_points: understand the concept of a non-parametric test and when it might be used
use the sign test for a median
use the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for a median or for paired data
use the Wilcoxon rank-sum test (Mann-Whitney U test) for two independent samples
use the Spearman's rank correlation coefficient to test for association between two variables
understand the conditions for the validity of non-parametric tests

math-cam-al-quadratics.name: P1.1 二次函数（判别式/顶点式/完全平方）
math-cam-al-quadratics.subject: math
curriculum_points: • use the derivatives of ex, ln x, sin x, cos x, tan x, together with constant multiples, sums, differences and composites
• differentiate products and quotients
• find and use the first derivative of a function which is defined parametrically or implicitly.

math-cam-al-functions.name: P1.2 函数（复合/反函数/变换）
math-cam-al-functions.subject: math
curriculum_points: • use the expansion of (a + b)n, where n is a positive integer
• recognise arithmetic and geometric progressions
• use the formulae for the nth term and for the sum of the first n terms to solve problems involving arithmetic or geometric progressions
• use the condition for the convergence of a geometric progression, and the formula for the sum to infinity of a convergent geometric progression.

math-cam-al-coordinate.name: P1.3 坐标几何（直线/圆/轨迹）
math-cam-al-coordinate.subject: math
curriculum_points: 1.3 Understand and use the coordinate geometry of the straight line, including the forms y = mx + c and ax + by + c = 0.
1.3 Understand and use the coordinate geometry of the circle, including the forms (x – a)² + (y – b)² = r² and x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0.

1.3 Use the following circle properties in problems:
• the perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects the chord
• the tangent at any point on a circle is perpendicular to the radius at that point
• the angle in a semicircle is a right angle.

math-cam-al-circular-trig.name: P1.4 圆周三角函数（弧度/正余弦定律）
math-cam-al-circular-trig.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the terms function, domain, range, one-one function, inverse function and composition of functions
• identify the range of a given function in simple cases, and find the composition of two given functions
• determine whether or not a given function is one-one, and find the inverse of a one-one function in simple cases
• illustrate in graphical terms the relation between a one-one function and its inverse

math-cam-al-series.name: P1.5 级数（二项展开/AP/GP）
math-cam-al-series.subject: math
curriculum_points: 1.5 Understand and use the expansion of (a + b)^n, where n is a positive integer.
1.5 Recognise arithmetic and geometric progressions.
1.5 Use the formulae for the nth term and for the sum of the first n terms to solve problems involving arithmetic or geometric progressions.
1.5 Use the condition for the convergence of a geometric progression, and use the formula for the sum to infinity of a convergent geometric progression.

math-cam-al-differentiation-p1.name: P1.6 微分（多项式/切线法线/极值）
math-cam-al-differentiation-p1.subject: math

math-cam-al-integration-p1.name: P1.7 积分（不定/定积分/面积体积）
math-cam-al-integration-p1.subject: math
curriculum_points: • use the derivatives of ex, ln x, sin x, cos x, tan x, together with constant multiples, sums, differences and composites
• differentiate products and quotients
• find and use the first derivative of a function which is defined parametrically or implicitly.

math-cam-al-algebra.name: P3.1 代数（模不等式/部分分式/多项式除法）
math-cam-al-algebra.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the definition of a radian, and use the relationship between radians and degrees
• use the formulae s = rθ and A = ½r²θ in solving problems concerning the arc length and sector area of a circle.

math-cam-al-log-exp.name: P3.2 对数与指数函数
math-cam-al-log-exp.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the relationship between logarithms and indices, and use the laws of logarithms (excluding change of base)
• understand the definition and properties of e^x and ln x, including their relationship as inverse functions and their graphs
• use logarithms to solve equations and inequalities in which the unknown appears in indices
• use logarithms to transform a given relationship to linear form, and hence determine unknown constants by considering the gradient and/or intercept.

math-cam-al-advanced-trig.name: P3.3 三角学进阶（恒等式/R-形式）
math-cam-al-advanced-trig.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the definition of a radian, and use the relationship between radians and degrees
• use the formulae s = rθ and A = ½r²θ in solving problems concerning the arc length and sector area of a circle.

math-cam-al-differentiation-p3.name: P3.4 微分进阶（链/乘/商/隐/参数）
math-cam-al-differentiation-p3.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the definition of a radian, and use the relationship between radians and degrees
• use the formulae s = rθ and A = ½r²θ in solving problems concerning the arc length and sector area of a circle.
Including calculation of lengths and angles in triangles and areas of triangles.

math-cam-al-integration-p3.name: P3.5 积分进阶（换元/分部/部分分式）
math-cam-al-integration-p3.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the definition of a radian, and use the relationship between radians and degrees
• use the formulae s = rθ and A = ½r²θ in solving problems concerning the arc length and sector area of a circle.
Including calculation of lengths and angles in triangles and areas of triangles.

math-cam-al-numerical.name: P3.6 数值方法（牛顿-拉弗森/梯形法则）
math-cam-al-numerical.subject: math
curriculum_points: 3.6 Locate approximately a root of an equation, by means of graphical considerations and/or searching for a sign change.
3.6 Understand the idea of, and use the notation for, a sequence of approximations which converges to a root of an equation.

3.6 Understand how a given simple iterative formula of the form x_{n+1} = F(x_n) relates to the equation being solved, and use a given iteration, or an iteration based on a given rearrangement of an equation, to determine a root to a prescribed degree of accuracy.

math-cam-al-vectors.name: P3.7 向量（直线、平面、夹角）
math-cam-al-vectors.subject: math
curriculum_points: 3.7 Use standard notations for vectors, i.e. x\mathbf{i} + y\mathbf{j} + z\mathbf{k}, \begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \\ z \end{pmatrix}, \overrightarrow{AB}, \mathbf{a}.
3.7 Carry out addition and subtraction of vectors and multiplication of a vector by a scalar, and interpret these operations geometrically.
3.7 Calculate the magnitude of a vector, and use unit vectors, displacement vectors and position vectors.
3.7 Understand the significance of all the symbols used when the equation of a straight line is expressed in the form \mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a} + t\mathbf{b}.
3.7 Determine whether two lines are parallel, intersect or are skew.
3.7 Find the angle between two lines, and the point of intersection of two lines when it exists.
3.7 Use the scalar product to find the angle between two lines, and use the perpendicular property where appropriate.

math-cam-al-diff-eqs.name: P3.8 微分方程（可分离变量/一阶线性）
math-cam-al-diff-eqs.subject: math
curriculum_points: • understand the definition of a radian, and use the relationship between radians and degrees
• use the formulae s = rθ and A = ½r²θ in solving problems concerning the arc length and sector area of a circle.

math-cam-al-momentum.subject: math
curriculum_points: • carry out the process of completing the square for a quadratic polynomial ax2 + bx + c and use a completed square form
• find the discriminant of a quadratic polynomial ax2 + bx + c and use the discriminant
• solve quadratic equations, and quadratic inequalities, in one unknown

phy-cam-al-quantities-units.name: 1 物理量与单位（SI/不确定度）
phy-cam-al-quantities-units.subject: physics
phy-cam-al-quantities-units.domain: AS · 物理量与力学 Physical Quantities & Mechanics (Topics 1-6)
curriculum_points: 1 understand that a magnetic field is an example of a field of force produced either by moving charges or by permanent magnets
2 represent a magnetic field by field lines

phy-cam-al-kinematics.name: 2 运动学（直线/抛体）
phy-cam-al-kinematics.subject: physics
phy-cam-al-kinematics.domain: AS · 物理量与力学 Physical Quantities & Mechanics (Topics 1-6)
curriculum_points: 1 define and use the terms displacement, amplitude, phase difference, period, frequency, wavelength and speed
4 derive, using the definitions of speed, frequency and wavelength, the wave equation v = f λ
5 recall and use v = f λ
6 understand that energy is transferred by a progressive wave

phy-cam-al-dynamics.name: 3 动力学（牛顿定律/动量）
phy-cam-al-dynamics.subject: physics
phy-cam-al-dynamics.domain: AS · 物理量与力学 Physical Quantities & Mechanics (Topics 1-6)
curriculum_points: define and use linear momentum as the product of mass and velocity
state the principle of conservation of momentum

apply the principle of conservation of momentum to solve simple problems, including elastic and inelastic interactions between objects in both one and two dimensions (knowledge of the concept of coefficient of restitution is not required)
recall that, for an elastic collision, total kinetic energy is conserved and the relative speed of approach is equal to the relative speed of separation

phy-cam-al-forces-density.name: 4 力、密度、压强
phy-cam-al-forces-density.subject: physics
phy-cam-al-forces-density.domain: AS · 物理量与力学 Physical Quantities & Mechanics (Topics 1-6)
curriculum_points: define and use linear momentum as the product of mass and velocity
state the principle of conservation of momentum

apply the principle of conservation of momentum to solve simple problems, including elastic and inelastic interactions between objects in both one and two dimensions (knowledge of the concept of coefficient of restitution is not required)
recall that, for an elastic collision, total kinetic energy is conserved and the relative speed of approach is equal to the relative speed of separation

phy-cam-al-work-energy-power.name: 5 功、能量、功率
phy-cam-al-work-energy-power.subject: physics
phy-cam-al-work-energy-power.domain: AS · 物理量与力学 Physical Quantities & Mechanics (Topics 1-6)
curriculum_points:
7.1 Progressive waves
Candidates should be able to:
1 describe what is meant by wave motion as illustrated by vibration in ropes, springs and ripple tanks
2 understand and use the terms displacement, amplitude, phase difference, period, frequency,
wavelength and speed
4 derive, using the definitions of speed, frequency and wavelength, the wave equation v = f λ
5 recall and use v = f λ
6 understand that energy is transferred by a progressive wave

7.2 Transverse and longitudinal waves
Candidates should be able to:
1 compare transverse and longitudinal waves
2 analyse and interpret graphical representations of transverse and longitudinal waves

phy-cam-al-deformation-of-solids.name: 6 固体的形变与弹性
phy-cam-al-deformation-of-solids.subject: physics
phy-cam-al-deformation-of-solids.domain: AS · 物理量与力学 Physical Quantities & Mechanics (Topics 1-6)
curriculum_points:
Candidates should be able to:
1 define and use linear momentum as the product of mass and velocity
4 define and use force as rate of change of momentum

Candidates should be able to:
1 state the principle of conservation of momentum
2 apply the principle of conservation of momentum to solve simple problems, including elastic and inelastic interactions between objects in both one and two dimensions (knowledge of the concept of coefficient of restitution is not required)
3 recall that, for an elastic collision, total kinetic energy is conserved and the relative speed of approach is equal to the relative speed of separation
4 understand that, while momentum of a system is always conserved in interactions between objects, some change in kinetic energy may take place

phy-cam-al-waves-basics.name: 7 波的基本性质
phy-cam-al-waves-basics.subject: physics

state the principle of conservation of momentum

1 understand that a force might act on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field

3 define magnetic flux density as the force acting per unit current per unit length on a wire placed at right-angles to the magnetic field

curriculum_points: (a) understand and use the terms period, frequency, peak value and root-mean-square value as applied to an alternating current or voltage
(b) deduce that the mean power in a resistive load is half the maximum power for a sinusoidal alternating current
(c) represent an alternating current or voltage by an equation of the form x = x₀ sin ωt
(d) distinguish between r.m.s. and peak values and recall and use I<sub>rms</sub> = I₀ / √2 and V<sub>rms</sub> = V₀ / √2 for a sinusoidal alternating current
(e) distinguish graphically between half-wave and full-wave rectification
(f) explain the use of a single diode for the half-wave rectification of an alternating current
(g) explain the use of four diodes (bridge rectifier) for the full-wave rectification of an alternating current
(h) analyse the effect of a single capacitor in smoothing, including the effect of the value of capacitance in relation to the load resistance

3.1 Momentum and Newton’s laws of motion
Candidates should be able to:
1 understand that mass is the property of an object that resists change in motion
2 recall F = ma and solve problems using it, understanding that acceleration and resultant force are
always in the same direction
3 define and use linear momentum as the product of mass and velocity
4 define and use force as rate of change of momentum
5 state and apply each of Newton’s laws of motion

3.3 Linear momentum and its conservation
Candidates should be able to:
1 state the principle of conservation of momentum
2 apply the principle of conservation of momentum to solve simple problems, including elastic and
inelastic interactions between objects in both one and two dimensions (knowledge of the concept of
coefficient of restitution is not required)
3 recall that, for an elastic collision, total kinetic energy is conserved and the relative speed of approach is
equal to the relative speed of separation
4 understand that, while momentum of a system is always conserved in interactions between objects,
some change in kinetic energy may take place

curriculum_points: 1.1 The microscope in cell studies Learning outcomes Candidates should be able to: 1 make temporary preparations of cellular material suitable for viewing with a light microscope 2 draw cells from microscope slides and photomicrographs 3 calculate magnifications of images and actual sizes of specimens from drawings, photomicrographs and electron micrographs (scanning and transmission) 4 use an eyepiece graticule and stage micrometer scale to make measurements and use the appropriate units, millimetre (mm), micrometre (µm) and nanometre (nm) 5 define resolution and magnification and explain the differences between these terms, with reference to light microscopy and electron microscopy

1.2 Cells as the basic units of living organisms Learning outcomes Candidates should be able to: 1 recognise organelles and other cell structures found in eukaryotic cells and outline their structures and functions, limited to: • cell surface membrane • nucleus, nuclear envelope and nucleolus • rough endoplasmic reticulum • smooth endoplasmic reticulum • Golgi body (Golgi apparatus or Golgi complex) • mitochondria (including the presence of small circular DNA) • ribosomes (80S in the cytoplasm and 70S in chloroplasts and mitochondria) • lysosomes • centrioles and microtubules • cilia • microvilli • chloroplasts (including the presence of small circular DNA) • cell wall • plasmodesmata • large permanent vacuole and tonoplast of plant cells

1.2 Cells as the basic units of living organisms (continued) Learning outcomes Candidates should be able to: 2 describe and interpret photomicrographs, electron micrographs and drawings of typical plant and animal cells 3 compare the structure of typical plant and animal cells

cs-cam-igcse-databases-igc.name: 数据库
cs-cam-igcse-databases-igc.subject: cs
cs-cam-igcse-databases-igc.domain: 数据库与逻辑 / Databases & Logic / Databases & Logic
curriculum_points:
Candidates should be able to: Notes and guidance
1 Understand how and why computers use binary to represent all forms of data • Any form of data needs to be converted to binary to be processed by a computer
• Data is processed using logic gates and stored in registers

Candidates should be able to: Notes and guidance
2 (a) Understand the denary, binary and hexadecimal number systems • Denary is a base 10 system
• Binary is a base 2 system
• Hexadecimal is a base 16 system
(b) Convert between • Values used will be integers only
(i) positive denary and positive binary • Conversions in both directions, e.g. denary to binary or binary to denary
(ii) positive denary and positive hexadecimal • Maximum binary number length of 16-bit
(iii) positive hexadecimal and positive binary

Candidates should be able to: Notes and guidance
3 Understand how and why hexadecimal is used as a beneficial method of data representation • Areas within computer science that hexadecimal is used should be identified
• Hexadecimal is easier for humans to understand than binary, as it is a shorter representation of binary

Candidates should be able to: Notes and guidance
4 (a) Add two positive 8-bit binary integers
(b) Understand the concept of overflow and why it occurs in binary addition • An overflow error will occur if the value is greater than 255 in an 8-bit register
• A computer or a device has a predefined limit that it can represent or store, for example 16-bit
• An overflow error occurs when a value outside this limit should be returned

humanities-cam-igcse-climate-gp.name: 气候变化
humanities-cam-igcse-climate-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-climate-gp.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.
Learners are encouraged to work both independently and collaboratively, with respect for and understanding of different cultures and perspectives.

This is a skills-based subject and schools can make their own selection of global topics to study. Learners can draw upon knowledge and understanding gained from studying other subjects. Learners will develop skills that are interdisciplinary and highly transferable to other subjects.

humanities-cam-igcse-poverty-inequality.name: 贫困与不平等
humanities-cam-igcse-poverty-inequality.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-poverty-inequality.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.
Candidates undertake independent research on a topic of their own choice. They devise and develop a research question and then conduct personal research to answer that question.

Candidates should select research methods that are appropriate for their chosen topic. The research can be based on primary and/or secondary evidence, depending on the subject matter and the approach. The types of research methods that might be used are:
• reviewing the secondary literature in the field
• close analysis of texts
• producing evidence through other qualitative research methods (e.g. ethnography, interviews, focus groups)
• analysis and evaluation of statistical or other numerical data
• producing evidence through other quantitative research methods (e.g. surveys, experiments)
• a mixture of the methods above.

The study of the reasons why particular sets of methods are appropriate for specific academic subject areas is known as research methodology. Candidates are expected to engage with the methodology underlying their choice of research methods, and consider the impact of their choice on their report.

humanities-cam-igcse-disease-health.name: 疾病与健康
humanities-cam-igcse-disease-health.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-disease-health.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Analyse the causes, transmission and global impact of communicable and non-communicable diseases, evaluating the effectiveness of different international and national health interventions.
Construct and present a personal and critical perspective on a global health issue, synthesising research from a range of sources to evaluate individual, local and international responses.

humanities-cam-igcse-migration-gp.name: 迁徙
humanities-cam-igcse-migration-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-migration-gp.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: The aims are to enable students to: • acknowledge and understand the diversity of perspectives on global issues • develop the ability to evaluate claims and evidence • make independent judgements supported by reasoning and evidence, and understand the importance of justifying their own opinions • become effective researchers, using appropriate sources to support judgements and understanding of research processes • develop their ability to reflect on their own learning and judgements and on their work with others • develop a range of effective written and spoken communication skills.

Cambridge International AS & A Level Global Perspectives & Research develops learners’ thinking skills of analysis, evaluation and synthesis through considering a range of global topics. Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.

The Critical Path offers a systematic approach to teaching and learning where learners develop the ability to deconstruct and reconstruct arguments about global issues through personal research and interrogation of evidence. By reflecting on the implications of their research and the personal judgements it leads them to make, students learn to communicate their findings and ideas through a range of appropriate formats.

The Critical Path is an approach to teaching and learning that provides a systematic way for learners to develop the skills of research, reasoning and communication. These valuable thinking and reasoning skills are useful for a range of subjects and can also be used in higher education and a wide range of careers.

humanities-cam-igcse-conflict-gp.name: 冲突与和平
humanities-cam-igcse-conflict-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-conflict-gp.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points:
The aims are to enable students to:
• develop the ability to evaluate claims and evidence
• make independent judgements supported by reasoning and evidence, and understand the importance of justifying their own opinions
Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.

AO1 Research, analysis and evaluation
• identify and analyse arguments, evidence and perspectives
• synthesise and evaluate arguments, evidence and perspectives

AO2 Reflection
• acknowledge different perspectives and evaluate their impact on the learner’s own standpoint

humanities-cam-igcse-technology-society.name: 技术与社会
humanities-cam-igcse-technology-society.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-technology-society.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: The aims are to enable students to: • acknowledge and understand the diversity of perspectives on global issues • develop the ability to evaluate claims and evidence • make independent judgements supported by reasoning and evidence, and understand the importance of justifying their own opinions
Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.

Learners will be encouraged to develop research, reasoning and communication skills systematically, using an approach called the Critical Path. This approach helps them to interrogate information, explore different perspectives and communicate personal reflections.

AO1 Research, analysis and evaluation • identify and analyse arguments, evidence and perspectives • synthesise and evaluate arguments, evidence and perspectives • locate through research a range of appropriate sources and perspectives • use research to support judgements about arguments and perspectives

humanities-cam-igcse-research-question-g.name: 研究问题
humanities-cam-igcse-research-question-g.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-research-question-g.domain: 研究技能 / Research Skills / Research Skills
curriculum_points: Candidates research a global issue of their own choice from topics studied during the course, exploring different perspectives.
Candidates devise their own essay title and write an essay in continuous prose of 1750-2000 words.

In their research, candidates should identify and explore the context and basis of arguments that respond to the question from different global perspectives. They should identify globally contrasting perspectives, understand the arguments, reasoning or claims upon which these perspectives are based and offer a critical view of them.

In the essay, candidates should demonstrate AO1, AO2 and AO3 skills. They will be assessed on their ability to: • analyse their chosen question (AO1) • synthesise arguments to build contrasting perspectives (AO1) • select and refer to a range of sources (AO1) • assess and justify the appropriateness of the sources selected (AO1) • compare and evaluate contrasting perspectives (AO1) • reflect on the impact of alternative perspectives on their viewpoint (AO2) • suggest further relevant research (AO2) • write a clear, structured essay using effective referencing (AO3).

humanities-cam-igcse-sources-gp.name: 信息源评估
humanities-cam-igcse-sources-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-sources-gp.domain: 研究技能 / Research Skills / Research Skills
curriculum_points: The aims are to enable students to: • develop the ability to evaluate claims and evidence • make independent judgements supported by reasoning and evidence, and understand the importance of justifying their own opinions
Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.

The Critical Path offers a systematic approach to teaching and learning where learners develop the ability to deconstruct and reconstruct arguments about global issues through personal research and interrogation of evidence.

Deconstruction: ... What are the strengths and weaknesses of arguments, reasoning or claims? Evaluate the strengths and weaknesses in the arguments, reasoning or claims. Assess the use of analogy and identify any errors in the reasoning. How strong is the evidence? Identify whether evidence gives strong or weak support to the conclusion or claim. Consider the reliability of the evidence and suggest other evidence required to substantiate or refute claims or counterclaims.

humanities-cam-igcse-bias-perspective.name: 偏见与视角
humanities-cam-igcse-bias-perspective.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-bias-perspective.domain: 研究技能 / Research Skills / Research Skills
curriculum_points: Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.
Candidates undertake independent research on a topic of their own choice. They devise and develop a research question and then conduct personal research to answer that question.

Candidates should select research methods that are appropriate for their chosen topic. The research can be based on primary and/or secondary evidence, depending on the subject matter and the approach. The types of research methods that might be used are:
• reviewing the secondary literature in the field
• close analysis of texts
• producing evidence through other qualitative research methods (e.g. ethnography, interviews, focus groups)
• analysis and evaluation of statistical or other numerical data
• producing evidence through other quantitative research methods (e.g. surveys, experiments)
• a mixture of the methods above.

humanities-cam-igcse-data-gp.name: 数据分析
humanities-cam-igcse-data-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-data-gp.domain: 研究技能 / Research Skills / Research Skills
curriculum_points:
Component 1 – Written Exam
Written paper, 1 hour 30 minutes, 45 marks
This component is an externally set assessment, marked by Cambridge International.
Component 1 contains three questions based on two source documents provided with the exam paper. The source documents may present different perspectives on an issue of global significance.
Candidates answer all the questions.
Question 1 (5 marks) is a structured question based on two source documents. Candidates are required to read both sources and answer short questions that require candidates to demonstrate AO1 skills.
Question 2 (15 marks) requires a longer response based on one of the source documents. Candidates are required to focus on the evidence in the document and demonstrate AO1 skills.
Question 3 (25 marks) requires a longer, essay response based on the two source documents. Candidates are required to focus on the arguments and perspectives given in the documents and provide a reasoned judgement, demonstrating AO1 and AO3 skills.

In carrying out a critical and comparative analysis of the source material, candidates are assessed on their ability to:
• identify evidence, analyse its strengths and weaknesses and evaluate the evidence (AO1)
• identify and compare key components of arguments (AO1)
• analyse and compare perspectives (AO1)
• evaluate arguments and make judgements about arguments and perspectives (AO1)
• communicate clearly and logically (AO3).

humanities-cam-igcse-action-plan.name: 行动方案
humanities-cam-igcse-action-plan.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-action-plan.domain: 协作与反思 / Collaboration & Reflection / Collaboration & Reflection
curriculum_points: Candidates analyse and evaluate arguments, interrogate evidence and compare perspectives.
The source documents present perspectives on an issue of global significance.

In their research, candidates should identify and explore the context and basis of arguments that respond to the question from different global perspectives. They should identify globally contrasting perspectives, understand the arguments, reasoning or claims upon which these perspectives are based and offer a critical view of them.
A perspective is a coherent world view which is a response to an issue. A perspective is made up of argument, evidence and assumptions and may be influenced by a particular context.

humanities-cam-igcse-essay-gp.name: 个人论文
humanities-cam-igcse-essay-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-essay-gp.domain: 论证与沟通 / Argument & Communication / Argument & Communication
curriculum_points: The aims are to enable students to: • acknowledge and understand the diversity of perspectives on global issues • develop the ability to evaluate claims and evidence • make independent judgements supported by reasoning and evidence, and understand the importance of justifying their own opinions • become effective researchers, using appropriate sources to support judgements and understanding of research processes • develop their ability to reflect on their own learning and judgements and on their work with others • develop a range of effective written and spoken communication skills.

Cambridge International AS & A Level Global Perspectives & Research develops learners’ thinking skills of analysis, evaluation and synthesis through considering a range of global topics. Learners will develop skills of independent enquiry and learn about appropriate research methods and methodology.

Learners will be encouraged to develop research, reasoning and communication skills systematically, using an approach called the Critical Path. This approach helps them to interrogate information, explore different perspectives and communicate personal reflections.

The Critical Path is an approach to teaching and learning that provides a systematic way for learners to develop the skills of research, reasoning and communication. These valuable thinking and reasoning skills are useful for a range of subjects and can also be used in higher education and a wide range of careers.

humanities-cam-igcse-presentation-gp.name: 口头陈述
humanities-cam-igcse-presentation-gp.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-presentation-gp.domain: 论证与沟通 / Argument & Communication / Argument & Communication
curriculum_points:
Candidates research a global issue of their own choice from topics studied during the course, exploring different perspectives.
Candidates devise their own essay title and write an essay in continuous prose of 1750-2000 words.

In their research, candidates should identify and explore the context and basis of arguments that respond to the question from different global perspectives. They should identify globally contrasting perspectives, understand the arguments, reasoning or claims upon which these perspectives are based and offer a critical view of them.

In the essay, candidates should demonstrate AO1, AO2 and AO3 skills. They will be assessed on their ability to:
• analyse their chosen question (AO1)
• synthesise arguments to build contrasting perspectives (AO1)
• select and refer to a range of sources (AO1)
• assess and justify the appropriateness of the sources selected (AO1)
• compare and evaluate contrasting perspectives (AO1)
• reflect on the impact of alternative perspectives on their viewpoint (AO2)
• suggest further relevant research (AO2)
• write a clear, structured essay using effective referencing (AO3).

humanities-cam-igcse-written-report.name: 书面报告
humanities-cam-igcse-written-report.subject: humanities
humanities-cam-igcse-written-report.domain: 论证与沟通 / Argument & Communication / Argument & Communication
curriculum_points: Candidates must work in teams of two to five members to identify a local problem that has global relevance. Each team works together to identify the problem to research. The team must outline their local problem in a single statement that allows the team members to address contrasting perspectives on the problem. The scope of the research should be sufficiently broad to enable teams to explore the problem from local and global perspectives.

Each candidate will write a reflective paper (maximum 800 words) focusing on how effectively the team worked together, what could be improved, and any changes to their personal views shaped by the collaborative experience.
Candidates will be assessed on their: • reflection on the effectiveness of the team collaboration (AO2) • reflection on the learning (AO2).

curriculum_points: Stage 1 Reading: Word structure (phonics): Read aloud accurately words of two or more syllables that use the same GPCs (grapheme–phoneme correspondences) as above.
Stage 1 Reading: Word structure (phonics): Read aloud accurately words that use the common GPCs (grapheme–phoneme correspondences) taught in Stage 1.
Stage 2 Reading: Word structure (phonics): Read aloud accurately many words of two or more syllables that use known GPCs (grapheme–phoneme correspondences).
Stage 2 Reading: Word structure (phonics): Read aloud accurately many words containing the common GPCs (grapheme–phoneme correspondences) for the 40+ phonemes taught in Stages 1 and 2.
Stage 3 Reading: Vocabulary and language: Read and understand a range of words using knowledge of morphology and etymology, e.g. word roots and families, prefixes and suffixes.
Stage 4 Reading: Vocabulary and language: Read and understand a range of words using knowledge of morphology and etymology, e.g. word roots and families, prefixes and suffixes.

curriculum_points: • *6Ri.06 Explore explicit meanings in a range of texts.

• *6Ri.14 Support answers to questions with reference to, or quotations from, one or more points in a text.

• *6Ri.04 Read and explore a range of non-fiction text types.

• *6Wc.06 Develop writing for a purpose using language and features appropriate for a range of text types.

english-cam-primary-letter-writing.name: 书信写作
english-cam-primary-letter-writing.subject: english
curriculum_points: Stage 3 Writing: Creation of texts: Write a range of non-fiction texts for different purposes and audiences, using appropriate form and features, e.g. letters, reports, instructions, advertisements.

Stage 4 Writing: Creation of texts: Write a range of non-fiction texts for different purposes and audiences, using appropriate form and features, e.g. letters, reports, explanations, persuasive texts.

Stage 5 Writing: Creation of texts: Write a range of non-fiction texts for different purposes and audiences, using appropriate form and features, e.g. formal and informal letters, reports, articles, biographies.

Stage 6 Writing: Creation of texts: Write a range of non-fiction texts for different purposes and audiences, using appropriate form and features, e.g. formal and informal letters, reports, articles, biographies, arguments.

english-cam-primary-speaking-basics.name: 口语基础
english-cam-primary-speaking-basics.subject: english
english-cam-primary-speaking-basics.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Speaking & Listening
curriculum_points: • 6SLm.01 Adapt pace and tone of speech appropriately in formal and informal contexts.
• 6SLm.02 Structure information to aid the listener’s understanding of the main and subsidiary points.
• 6SLm.03 Use language to convey ideas and opinions, with increasing clarity and detail.
• *6SLm.04 Adapt non-verbal communication techniques for different purposes and contexts.
• *6SLm.05 Show awareness of different audiences, e.g. by using the appropriate register.

english-cam-primary-group-discussion.name: 小组讨论
english-cam-primary-group-discussion.subject: english
english-cam-primary-group-discussion.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Speaking & Listening
curriculum_points: • *6SLg.01 Take different assigned roles within groups, and begin to assign roles within a group.
• *6SLg.02 Show consideration of another point of view.
• 6SLg.03 Extend a discussion by building on own and other's ideas.
• 6SLg.04 Encourage others to take turns in a discussion.

english-cam-primary-presentation-primary.name: 口头表达
english-cam-primary-presentation-primary.subject: english
english-cam-primary-presentation-primary.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Speaking & Listening
curriculum_points: • *6SLp.03 Plan and deliver independent and group presentations confidently to a range of audiences, adapting presentations appropriately to the audience.
• *6SLp.04 Begin to make choices about the most appropriate media for a particular presentation.

english-cam-primary-parts-of-speech.name: 词性入门
english-cam-primary-parts-of-speech.subject: english
english-cam-primary-parts-of-speech.domain: 语法与词汇 / Grammar & Vocabulary / Grammar & Vocabulary
curriculum_points: • 6Rg.05 Identify different word classes in texts and understand their purposes.
• 6Rg.08 Explore and discuss grammatical features in a range of texts, e.g. verb forms, sentence types, use of different word classes.

english-cam-primary-tenses-primary.name: 时态（一般/进行/完成）
english-cam-primary-tenses-primary.subject: english
english-cam-primary-tenses-primary.domain: 语法与词汇 / Grammar & Vocabulary / Grammar & Vocabulary
curriculum_points: • 6Rg.06 Explore, and understand, the use of active and passive verb forms.
• 6Wg.05 Use active and passive verb forms within sentences.

english-cam-primary-punctuation.name: 标点符号
english-cam-primary-punctuation.subject: english
english-cam-primary-punctuation.domain: 语法与词汇 / Grammar & Vocabulary / Grammar & Vocabulary
curriculum_points: • 6Rg.01 Explore in texts, and understand, the uses of colons, semi-colons, ellipses, parenthetic commas, dashes and brackets.
• 6Wg.01 Use commas, dashes and brackets parenthetically.
• 6Wg.02 Punctuate direct and reported speech accurately.
• 6Wg.03 Use punctuation effectively to clarify meaning in complex sentences.

english-cam-primary-vocabulary-building.name: 词汇拓展
english-cam-primary-vocabulary-building.subject: english
english-cam-primary-vocabulary-building.domain: 语法与词汇 / Grammar & Vocabulary / Grammar & Vocabulary
curriculum_points: • 6Rv.01 Deduce the meanings of unfamiliar phrases from their context, including phrases which are no longer common in modern times.
• 6Rv.02 Explore word origins and derivations, including the use of words from other languages.
• *6Rv.03 Identify and record interesting and significant words, and synonyms, from texts to inform own writing.
• 6Wv.01 Use specialised vocabulary accurately to match a familiar topic.
• 6Wv.02 Explore and use words and phrases to convey shades of meaning appropriate to the context.
• 6Wv.03 Transform meaning with prefixes and suffixes.
• *6Wv.06 Use own lists of interesting and significant words, dictionaries and thesauruses to extend the range of vocabulary used in written work.

english-cam-primary-spelling-rules.name: 拼写规则
english-cam-primary-spelling-rules.subject: english
english-cam-primary-spelling-rules.domain: 语法与词汇 / Grammar & Vocabulary / Grammar & Vocabulary
curriculum_points: • 6Ww.01 Explore and use different ways of representing consonants, e.g. -ck, -k, -ke, -que or -ch for /k/; -ch or -tch for /ʧ/; j-, g- or -dge for /ʤ/.
• 6Ww.02 Explore and spell words with different suffixes but similar pronunciation, e.g. -tion, -cian, -sion, -ssion; -ance, -ence.
• 6Ww.03 Further develop understanding of how to add prefixes and suffixes to root words, and when the root word changes.
• 6Ww.04 Spell familiar homophones and commonly confused words correctly, e.g. aloud, allowed; past, passed; advice, advise; desert, dessert.
• 6Ww.05 Explore a range of spelling rules and exceptions.
• 6Ww.06 Use effective strategies to spell a wide range of words correctly.
• *6Ww.07 Use paper-based and on-screen tools to find the correct spelling of words; keep and use spelling logs of misspelt words, and identify words that need to be learned.

math-cam-primary-counting.name: 计数与数感
math-cam-primary-counting.subject: math
curriculum_points: Learners explore the five principles of counting and develop number fluency, demonstrating flexibility, efficiency and accuracy in the computational strategies that they choose.

math-cam-primary-place-value.name: 位值
math-cam-primary-place-value.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 5Np.01 Understand and explain the value of each digit in decimals (tenths and hundredths).
• 5Np.02 Use knowledge of place value to multiply and divide whole numbers by 10, 100 and 1000.
• 5Np.03 Use knowledge of place value to multiply and divide decimals by 10 and 100.
• 5Np.04 Compose, decompose and regroup numbers, including decimals (tenths and hundredths).

math-cam-primary-add-sub.name: 加减法
math-cam-primary-add-sub.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 5Nf.09 Estimate, add and subtract numbers with the same number of decimal places.
• 6Ni.01 Estimate, add and subtract integers.
• 6Nf.09 Estimate, add and subtract numbers with the same or different number of decimal places.

math-cam-primary-mul-div.name: 乘除法
math-cam-primary-mul-div.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 5Nf.10 Estimate and multiply numbers with one decimal place by 1-digit whole numbers.
• 6Ni.04 Estimate and multiply whole numbers up to 10 000 by 1-digit or 2-digit whole numbers.
• 6Ni.05 Estimate and divide whole numbers up to 1000 by 1-digit or 2-digit whole numbers.
• 6Nf.10 Estimate and multiply numbers with one or two decimal places by 1-digit and 2-digit whole numbers.
• 6Nf.11 Estimate and divide numbers with one or two decimal places by whole numbers.

math-cam-primary-fractions.name: 分数
math-cam-primary-fractions.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 5Nf.01 Understand that a fraction can be represented as a division of the numerator by the denominator (unit fractions, three-quarters, tenths and hundredths).
• 5Nf.02 Understand that proper fractions can act as operators.
• 5Nf.03 Recognise that improper fractions and mixed numbers can have an equivalent value.
• 5Nf.05 Estimate, add and subtract fractions with the same denominator and denominators that are multiples of each other.
• 5Nf.06 Estimate, multiply and divide unit fractions by a whole number.

math-cam-primary-decimals.name: 小数
math-cam-primary-decimals.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 5Np.01 Understand and explain the value of each digit in decimals (tenths and hundredths).
• 5Np.03 Use knowledge of place value to multiply and divide decimals by 10 and 100.
• 5Np.05 Round numbers with one decimal place to the nearest whole number.
• 5Nf.09 Estimate, add and subtract numbers with the same number of decimal places.

math-cam-primary-percentages-primary.name: 百分数
math-cam-primary-percentages-primary.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 5Nf.04 Recognise that proper fractions, decimals (one decimal place) and percentages can have equivalent values.
• 5Nf.07 Recognise percentages of shapes, and write percentages as a fraction with denominator 100.
• 5Nf.08 Understand the relative size of quantities to compare and order numbers with one decimal place, proper fractions with the same denominator and percentages, using the symbols =, > and <.

math-cam-primary-negative-numbers.name: 负数
math-cam-primary-negative-numbers.subject: math
curriculum_points: • 6Nc.01 Count on and count back in steps of constant size, including fractions and decimals, and extend beyond zero to include negative numbers.

math-cam-primary-2d-shapes.name: 二维图形
math-cam-primary-2d-shapes.subject: math
math-cam-primary-2d-shapes.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 5Gg.01 Identify, describe, classify and sketch isosceles, equilateral or scalene triangles, including reference to angles and symmetrical properties.
• 6Gg.01 Identify, describe, classify and sketch quadrilaterals, including reference to angles, symmetrical properties, parallel sides and diagonals.

math-cam-primary-3d-shapes.name: 三维图形
math-cam-primary-3d-shapes.subject: math
math-cam-primary-3d-shapes.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 5Gg.04 Identify, describe and sketch 3D shapes in different orientations.
• 5Gg.05 Identify and sketch different nets for a cube.
• 6Gg.04 Identify, describe and sketch compound 3D shapes.
• 6Gg.06 Identify and sketch different nets for cubes, cuboids, prisms and pyramids.

math-cam-primary-angles-primary.name: 角的认识
math-cam-primary-angles-primary.subject: math
math-cam-primary-angles-primary.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 5Gg.07 Estimate, compare and classify angles, using geometric vocabulary including acute, right, obtuse and reflex.
• 5Gg.08 Know that the sum of the angles on a straight line is 180°, and use this to calculate missing angles on a straight line.
• 6Gg.09 Classify, estimate, measure and draw angles.
• 6Gg.10 Know that the sum of the angles in a triangle is 180°, and use this to calculate missing angles in a triangle.

math-cam-primary-symmetry.name: 对称
math-cam-primary-symmetry.subject: math
math-cam-primary-symmetry.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 5Gg.06 Use knowledge of reflective symmetry to identify and complete symmetrical patterns.
• 6Gg.08 Identify rotational symmetry in familiar shapes, patterns or images with maximum order 4. Describe rotational symmetry as ‘order x’.

math-cam-primary-perimeter-area-primary.name: 周长与面积
math-cam-primary-perimeter-area-primary.subject: math
math-cam-primary-perimeter-area-primary.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 5Gg.02 Estimate and measure perimeter and area of 2D shapes, understanding that shapes with the same perimeter can have different areas and vice versa.
• 5Gg.03 Draw compound shapes that can be divided into rectangles and squares. Estimate, measure and calculate their perimeter and area.
• 6Gg.03 Use knowledge of area of rectangles to estimate and calculate the area of right-angled triangles.

math-cam-primary-measurement.name: 长度/质量/容积
math-cam-primary-measurement.subject: math
math-cam-primary-measurement.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 6Gg.05 Understand the difference between capacity and volume.
• 6Gg.07 Understand the relationship between area of 2D shapes and surface area of 3D shapes.

math-cam-primary-time.name: 时间
math-cam-primary-time.subject: math
math-cam-primary-time.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: • 5Gt.01 Understand time intervals less than one second.
• 5Gt.02 Compare times between time zones in digital notation (12- and 24-hour) and on analogue clocks.
• 5Gt.03 Find time intervals in seconds, minutes and hours that bridge through 60.
• 5Gt.04 Recognise that a time interval can be expressed as a decimal, or in mixed units.
• 6Gt.01 Convert between time intervals expressed as a decimal and in mixed units.

math-cam-primary-money-primary.name: 货币
math-cam-primary-money-primary.subject: math
math-cam-primary-money-primary.domain: 几何与测量 / Geometry & Measure / Geometry & Measure
curriculum_points: Number: 2Nc.03 Use knowledge of number facts and place value to add and subtract money, e.g. 35¢ + 45¢ = 80¢, $1.20 – 40¢ = 80¢
Number: 3Nc.03 Add and subtract amounts of money, including mixed units, e.g. $2.15 + $4.35, $5.75 – $3.50
Number: 4Nc.03 Add and subtract amounts of money, including mixed units, e.g. $2.15 + $4.35, $5.75 – $3.50
Number: 5Nc.03 Solve problems involving money, including profit and loss, discounts and tax
Number: 6Nc.03 Solve problems involving money, including profit and loss, discounts and tax

math-cam-primary-data-handling.name: 数据处理
math-cam-primary-data-handling.subject: math
math-cam-primary-data-handling.domain: 统计 / Statistics & Probability / Statistics & Probability
curriculum_points: • 5Ss.01 Plan and conduct an investigation to answer a set of related statistical questions, considering what data to collect (categorical, discrete and continuous data).
• 5Ss.02 Record, organise and represent categorical, discrete and continuous data. Choose and explain which representation to use in a given situation:
• 6Ss.01 Plan and conduct an investigation and make predictions for a set of related statistical questions, considering what data to collect (categorical, discrete and continuous data).

math-cam-primary-graphs-charts.name: 图表
math-cam-primary-graphs-charts.subject: math
math-cam-primary-graphs-charts.domain: 统计 / Statistics & Probability / Statistics & Probability
curriculum_points: • 5Ss.02 Record, organise and represent categorical, discrete and continuous data. Choose and explain which representation to use in a given situation: Venn and Carroll diagrams, tally charts and frequency tables, bar charts, waffle diagrams, frequency diagrams for continuous data, line graphs, dot plots (one dot per data point).

• 6Ss.02 Record, organise and represent categorical, discrete and continuous data. Choose and explain which representation to use in a given situation: Venn and Carroll diagrams, tally charts and frequency tables, bar charts, waffle diagrams and pie charts, frequency diagrams for continuous data, line graphs, scatter graphs, dot plots.

math-cam-primary-averages-primary.name: 平均数
math-cam-primary-averages-primary.subject: math
math-cam-primary-averages-primary.domain: 统计 / Statistics & Probability / Statistics & Probability
curriculum_points: • 5Ss.03 Understand that the mode and median are ways to describe and summarise data sets. Find and interpret the mode and the median, and consider their appropriateness for the context.

• 6Ss.03 Understand that the mode, median, mean and range are ways to describe and summarise data sets. Find and interpret the mode (including bimodal data), median, mean and range, and consider their appropriateness for the context.

math-cam-primary-probability-intro-p.name: 概率入门
math-cam-primary-probability-intro-p.subject: math
math-cam-primary-probability-intro-p.domain: 统计 / Statistics & Probability / Statistics & Probability
curriculum_points: • 5Sp.01 Use the language associated with likelihood to describe and compare likelihood and risk of familiar events, including those with equally likely outcomes.
• 5Sp.02 Recognise that some outcomes are equally likely to happen and some outcomes are more (or less) likely to happen, when doing practical activities.
• 5Sp.03 Conduct chance experiments or simulations, using small and large numbers of trials, and present and describe the results using the language of probability.

math-cam-primary-patterns.name: 规律与模式
math-cam-primary-patterns.subject: math
math-cam-primary-patterns.domain: 数学思维 / Mathematical Thinking / Mathematical Thinking
curriculum_points: improve numerical fluency and knowledge of key mathematical concepts to make sense of numbers, patterns, shapes, measurements and data

math-cam-primary-problem-solving-p.name: 问题解决
math-cam-primary-problem-solving-p.subject: math
math-cam-primary-problem-solving-p.domain: 数学思维 / Mathematical Thinking / Mathematical Thinking
curriculum_points: solve new and unfamiliar problems using innovative mathematical thinking. They can select their own preferred mathematical strategies and can suggest alternative routes to develop efficient solutions.

math-cam-primary-reasoning-primary.name: 推理
math-cam-primary-reasoning-primary.subject: math
math-cam-primary-reasoning-primary.domain: 数学思维 / Mathematical Thinking / Mathematical Thinking
curriculum_points: They are keen to ask mathematical questions in a structured, systematic, critical and analytical way. They are able to present their findings and defend their strategies and solutions as well as critique and improve solutions of others.

science-cam-primary-plants-p.name: 植物
science-cam-primary-plants-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Bp.01 Know that plants are living things.
Bp.02 Know that plants need water, light and a suitable temperature to grow.
Bp.03 Identify and describe the basic parts of a plant (roots, stem/trunk, leaves, flowers).
Bp.04 Know that plants produce flowers, which develop into fruits containing seeds.
Bp.05 Describe the stages in a plant's life cycle, including seed, germination, growth and reproduction.
Bp.06 Investigate the way in which water is transported within plants.
Bp.07 Explore the role of flowers in the life cycle of flowering plants, including pollination, seed formation and seed dispersal.

science-cam-primary-animals-p.name: 动物
science-cam-primary-animals-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Ba.01 Know that animals are living things.
Ba.02 Identify and name a variety of common animals including fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals.
Ba.03 Identify and name a variety of common animals that are carnivores, herbivores and omnivores.
Ba.04 Describe the basic needs of animals for survival (water, food, air).
Ba.05 Identify how animals are suited to their environment.
Ba.06 Describe the differences in the life cycles of a mammal, an amphibian, an insect and a bird.

science-cam-primary-human-body-p.name: 人体
science-cam-primary-human-body-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Bh.01 Identify, name, draw and label the basic parts of the human body.
Bh.02 Know the importance of exercise, eating the right amounts of different types of food, and hygiene for humans.
Bh.03 Identify that animals, including humans, need the right types and amount of nutrition, and that they cannot make their own food; they get nutrition from what they eat.
Bh.04 Identify that humans and some other animals have skeletons and muscles for support, protection and movement.
Bh.05 Describe the ways in which nutrients and water are transported within animals, including humans.
Bh.06 Describe the changes as humans develop to old age.

science-cam-primary-habitats-p.name: 栖息地
science-cam-primary-habitats-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Bi.01 Explore and compare the differences between things that are living, dead, and things that have never been alive.

Bi.02 Identify that most living things live in habitats to which they are suited and describe how different habitats provide for the basic needs of different kinds of animals and plants, and how they depend on each other.
Bi.03 Identify and name a variety of plants and animals in their habitats, including microhabitats.
Bi.04 Describe how animals obtain their food from plants and other animals, using the idea of a simple food chain, and identify and name different sources of food.
Bi.05 Recognise that environments can change and that this can sometimes pose dangers to living things.

science-cam-primary-food-chains-p.name: 食物链
science-cam-primary-food-chains-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Bi.04 Describe how animals obtain their food from plants and other animals, using the idea of a simple food chain, and identify and name different sources of food.

science-cam-primary-life-cycles-p.name: 生命周期
science-cam-primary-life-cycles-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Bp.05 Describe the stages in a plant's life cycle, including seed, germination, growth and reproduction.
Ba.06 Describe the differences in the life cycles of a mammal, an amphibian, an insect and a bird.
Bh.06 Describe the changes as humans develop to old age.

science-cam-primary-materials-p.name: 材料
science-cam-primary-materials-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Cm.01 Distinguish between an object and the material from which it is made.
Cm.02 Identify and name a variety of everyday materials, including wood, plastic, glass, metal, water, and rock.
Cm.03 Describe the simple physical properties of a variety of everyday materials.
Cm.04 Compare and group together a variety of everyday materials on the basis of their simple physical properties.
Cm.05 Identify and compare the suitability of a variety of everyday materials, including wood, metal, plastic, glass, brick, rock, paper and cardboard for particular uses.

science-cam-primary-states-matter-p.name: 物态变化
science-cam-primary-states-matter-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Cm.06 Compare and group materials together, according to whether they are solids, liquids or gases.
Cm.07 Observe that some materials change state when they are heated or cooled, and measure or research the temperature at which this happens in degrees Celsius (°C).
Cm.08 Identify the part played by evaporation and condensation in the water cycle and associate the rate of evaporation with temperature.

science-cam-primary-mixtures-p.name: 混合物与分离
science-cam-primary-mixtures-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Cm.09 Know that some materials will dissolve in liquid to form a solution, and describe how to recover a substance from a solution.
Cm.10 Use knowledge of solids, liquids and gases to decide how mixtures might be separated, including through filtering, sieving and evaporating.

science-cam-primary-changes-materials.name: 材料变化
science-cam-primary-changes-materials.subject: science
curriculum_points: Cc.01 Understand how some changes result in the formation of new materials, and that this kind of change is not usually reversible, including changes associated with burning and the action of acid on bicarbonate of soda.

science-cam-primary-forces-p.name: 力与运动
science-cam-primary-forces-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Pf.01 Compare how things move on different surfaces.
Pf.02 Notice that some forces need contact between two objects, but magnetic forces can act at a distance.
Pf.03 Observe how magnets attract or repel each other and attract some materials and not others.
Pf.04 Compare and group together a variety of everyday materials on the basis of whether they are attracted to a magnet, and identify some magnetic materials.
Pf.05 Describe magnets as having two poles.
Pf.06 Predict whether two magnets will attract or repel each other, depending on which poles are facing.
Pf.07 Explain that unsupported objects fall towards the Earth because of the force of gravity acting between the Earth and the falling object.
Pf.08 Identify the effects of air resistance, water resistance and friction, that act between moving surfaces.
Pf.09 Recognise that some mechanisms, including levers, pulleys and gears, allow a smaller force to have a greater effect.

science-cam-primary-light-p.name: 光
science-cam-primary-light-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Pl.01 Recognise that they need light in order to see things and that dark is the absence of light.
Pl.02 Notice that light is reflected from surfaces.
Pl.03 Recognise that light from the sun can be dangerous and that there are ways to protect their eyes.
Pl.04 Recognise that shadows are formed when the light from a light source is blocked by a solid object.
Pl.05 Find patterns in the way that the sizes of shadows change.
Pl.06 Recognise that light appears to travel in straight lines.
Pl.07 Use the idea that light travels in straight lines to explain that objects are seen because they give out or reflect light into the eye.
Pl.08 Explain that we see things because light travels from light sources to our eyes or from light sources to objects and then to our eyes.
Pl.09 Use the idea that light travels in straight lines to explain why shadows have the same shape as the objects that cast them.

science-cam-primary-sound-p.name: 声
science-cam-primary-sound-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Ps.01 Identify how sounds are made, associating some of them with something vibrating.
Ps.02 Recognise that vibrations from sounds travel through a medium to the ear.
Ps.03 Find patterns between the pitch of a sound and features of the object that produced it.
Ps.04 Find patterns between the volume of a sound and the strength of the vibrations that produced it.
Ps.05 Recognise that sounds get fainter as the distance from the sound source increases.

science-cam-primary-electricity-p.name: 电路
science-cam-primary-electricity-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Pe.01 Identify common appliances that run on electricity.
Pe.02 Construct a simple series electrical circuit, identifying and naming its basic parts, including cells, wires, bulbs, switches and buzzers.
Pe.03 Identify whether or not a lamp will light in a simple series circuit, based on whether or not the lamp is part of a complete loop with a battery.
Pe.04 Recognise that a switch opens and closes a circuit and associate this with whether or not a lamp lights in a simple series circuit.
Pe.05 Recognise some common conductors and insulators, and associate metals with being good conductors.
Pe.06 Associate the brightness of a lamp or the volume of a buzzer with the number and voltage of cells used in the circuit.
Pe.07 Compare and give reasons for variations in how components function, including the brightness of bulbs, the loudness of buzzers and the on/off position of switches.
Pe.08 Use recognised symbols when representing a simple circuit in a diagram.

science-cam-primary-magnets.name: 磁
science-cam-primary-magnets.subject: science
curriculum_points: Pf.02 Notice that some forces need contact between two objects, but magnetic forces can act at a distance.
Pf.03 Observe how magnets attract or repel each other and attract some materials and not others.
Pf.04 Compare and group together a variety of everyday materials on the basis of whether they are attracted to a magnet, and identify some magnetic materials.
Pf.05 Describe magnets as having two poles.
Pf.06 Predict whether two magnets will attract or repel each other, depending on which poles are facing.

science-cam-primary-energy-p.name: 能量
science-cam-primary-energy-p.subject: science
curriculum_points: Pe.01 Identify common appliances that run on electricity.
Bp.02 Know that plants need water, light and a suitable temperature to grow.
Bh.02 Know the importance of exercise, eating the right amounts of different types of food, and hygiene for humans.
Pf.07 Explain that unsupported objects fall towards the Earth because of the force of gravity acting between the Earth and the falling object.

science-cam-primary-earth-p.name: 地球
science-cam-primary-earth-p.subject: science
science-cam-primary-earth-p.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Es.01 Describe the movement of the Earth, and other planets, relative to the Sun in the solar system.
Es.02 Describe the movement of the Moon relative to the Earth.
Es.03 Describe the Sun, Earth and Moon as approximately spherical bodies.
Es.04 Use the idea of the Earth’s rotation to explain day and night and the apparent movement of the sun across the sky.
Es.05 Understand how the tilt of the Earth and its orbit around the Sun cause seasons.
Ee.01 Recognise that soils are made from rocks and organic matter.
Ee.02 Compare and group together different kinds of rocks on the basis of their appearance and simple physical properties.
Ee.03 Describe in simple terms how fossils are formed when things that have lived are trapped within rock.
Ee.04 Recognise that living things have changed over time and that fossils provide information about living things that inhabited the Earth millions of years ago.
Ee.05 Recognise that the Earth’s crust, including the land surface, is divided into tectonic plates that are constantly moving.

science-cam-primary-solar-system-p.name: 太阳系
science-cam-primary-solar-system-p.subject: science
science-cam-primary-solar-system-p.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Es.01 Describe the movement of the Earth, and other planets, relative to the Sun in the solar system.
Es.02 Describe the movement of the Moon relative to the Earth.
Es.03 Describe the Sun, Earth and Moon as approximately spherical bodies.
Es.04 Use the idea of the Earth’s rotation to explain day and night and the apparent movement of the sun across the sky.
Es.05 Understand how the tilt of the Earth and its orbit around the Sun cause seasons.

science-cam-primary-weather-climate.name: 天气与气候
science-cam-primary-weather-climate.subject: science
science-cam-primary-weather-climate.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Ee.06 Identify and describe the part played by evaporation and condensation in the water cycle and associate the rate of evaporation with temperature.
Ee.07 Understand how human activities can affect the water cycle, e.g. deforestation, urbanisation.

science-cam-primary-observation.name: 观察与记录

science-cam-primary-observation.domain: 科学探究 / Scientific Inquiry / Scientific Inquiry
curriculum_points: TWSc.01 Ask questions about the world around us.
TWSc.02 Make predictions and suggest possible outcomes.
TWSc.03 Observe closely, using simple equipment where appropriate.
TWSp.01 Make and record observations and/or measurements using standard units where appropriate.
TWSp.02 Record findings using simple scientific language, drawings, labelled diagrams, keys, bar charts, and tables.

science-cam-primary-experiment-p.name: 简单实验
science-cam-primary-experiment-p.subject: science
science-cam-primary-experiment-p.domain: 科学探究 / Scientific Inquiry / Scientific Inquiry
curriculum_points: TWSc.04 Set up simple practical enquiries, comparative and fair tests.
TWSc.05 Gather and record data to help in answering questions.
TWSp.03 Use results to draw simple conclusions, make predictions for new values, suggest improvements and raise further questions.
TWSp.04 Report on findings from enquiries, including oral and written explanations, displays or presentations of results and conclusions.
