bio-ib-dp-water.name: A1 水的性质与生命
bio-ib-dp-water.subject: biology
bio-ib-dp-water.domain: 主题 A · 统一性与多样性 Unity and Diversity
curriculum_points:
C1.1.1—Enzymes as catalysts
Students should understand the benefit of increasing rates of reaction in cells.

C1.1.2—Role of enzymes in metabolism
Students should understand that metabolism is the complex network of interdependent and interacting chemical reactions occurring in living organisms. Because of enzyme specificity, many different enzymes are required by living organisms, and control over metabolism can be exerted through these enzymes.

C1.1.4—Enzymes as globular proteins with an active site for catalysis
Include that the active site is composed of a few amino acids only, but interactions between amino acids within the overall three-dimensional structure of the enzyme ensure that the active site has the necessary properties for catalysis.

C1.1.5—Interactions between substrate and active site to allow induced-fit binding
Students should recognize that both substrate and enzymes change shape when binding occurs.

bio-ib-dp-nucleic-acids.name: A1 核酸（DNA/RNA 结构与复制）
bio-ib-dp-nucleic-acids.subject: biology
bio-ib-dp-nucleic-acids.domain: 主题 A · 统一性与多样性 Unity and Diversity
curriculum_points:
A1.1.1—Water as the medium for life
Students should appreciate that the first cells originated in water and that water remains the medium in which most processes of life occur.

A1.1.5—Solvent properties of water linked to its role as a medium for metabolism and for transport in plants and animals
Emphasize that a wide variety of hydrophilic molecules dissolve in water and that most enzymes catalyse reactions in aqueous solution. Students should also understand that the functions of some molecules in cells depend on them being hydrophobic and insoluble.

A1.1.2—Hydrogen bonds as a consequence of the polar covalent bonds within water molecules
Students should understand that polarity of covalent bonding within water molecules is due to unequal sharing of electrons and that hydrogen bonding due to this polarity occurs between water molecules. Students should be able to represent two or more water molecules and hydrogen bonds between them with the notation shown below to indicate polarity.

bio-ib-dp-cell-diversity.name: A2 细胞的多样性（原核/真核）
bio-ib-dp-cell-diversity.name_en: A2 Origins of cells & cell diversity
bio-ib-dp-cell-diversity.subject: biology
bio-ib-dp-cell-diversity.domain: 主题 A · 统一性与多样性 Unity and Diversity
curriculum_points:
A1.2.6—DNA as a double helix made of two antiparallel strands of nucleotides with two strands linked by hydrogen bonding between complementary base pairs
In diagrams of DNA structure, students should draw the two strands antiparallel, but are not required to draw the helical shape. Students should show adenine (A) paired with thymine (T), and guanine (G) paired with cytosine (C). Students are not required to memorize the relative lengths of the purine and pyrimidine bases, or the numbers of hydrogen bonds.

A1.2.8—Role of complementary base pairing in allowing genetic information to be replicated and expressed
Students should understand that complementarity is based on hydrogen bonding.

A1.2.12—Purine-to-pyrimidine bonding as a component of DNA helix stability
Adenine–thymine (A–T) and cytosine–guanine (C–G) pairs have equal length, so the DNA helix has the same three-dimensional structure, regardless of the base sequence.

bio-ib-dp-biodiversity-classification.name: A3 生物多样性与分类（进化树）
bio-ib-dp-biodiversity-classification.subject: biology
bio-ib-dp-biodiversity-classification.domain: 主题 A · 统一性与多样性 Unity and Diversity

C1.1.5—Interactions between substrate and active site to allow induced-fit binding
Students should recognize that both substrate and enzymes change shape when binding occurs.

C1.1.7—Relationships between the structure of the active site, enzyme–substrate specificity and denaturation
Students should be able to explain these relationships.

bio-ib-dp-carbohydrates-lipids.name: B1 糖类与脂质
bio-ib-dp-carbohydrates-lipids.subject: biology
bio-ib-dp-carbohydrates-lipids.domain: 主题 B · 形式与功能 Form and Function
curriculum_points:
B2.1.1—Lipid bilayers as the basis of cell membranes
Phospholipids and other amphipathic lipids naturally form continuous sheet-like bilayers in water.

B2.1.2—Lipid bilayers as barriers
Students should understand that the hydrophobic hydrocarbon chains that form the core of a membrane have low permeability to large molecules and hydrophilic particles, including ions and polar molecules, so membranes function as effective barriers between aqueous solutions.

B2.1.10—Fluid mosaic model of membrane structure
Students should be able to draw a two-dimensional representation of the model and include peripheral and integral proteins, glycoproteins, phospholipids and cholesterol. They should also be able to indicate hydrophobic and hydrophilic regions.

bio-ib-dp-proteins.name: B1 蛋白质（结构与变性）
bio-ib-dp-proteins.subject: biology
bio-ib-dp-proteins.domain: 主题 B · 形式与功能 Form and Function
curriculum_points:
C1.1.2—Role of enzymes in metabolism
Students should understand that metabolism is the complex network of interdependent and interacting chemical reactions occurring in living organisms. Because of enzyme specificity, many different enzymes are required by living organisms, and control over metabolism can be exerted through these enzymes.

C1.1.3—Anabolic and catabolic reactions
Examples of anabolism should include the formation of macromolecules from monomers by condensation reactions including protein synthesis, glycogen formation and photosynthesis. Examples of catabolism should include hydrolysis of macromolecules into monomers in digestion and oxidation of substrates in respiration.

C1.1.13—Cyclical and linear pathways in metabolism
Use glycolysis, the Krebs cycle and the Calvin cycle as examples.

A1.2.1—DNA as the genetic material of all living organisms
Some viruses use RNA as their genetic material but viruses are not considered to be living.

A1.2.2—Components of a nucleotide
In diagrams of nucleotides use circles, pentagons and rectangles to represent relative positions of
phosphates, pentose sugars and bases.

A1.2.3—Sugar–phosphate bonding and the sugar–phosphate “backbone” of DNA and RNA
Sugar–phosphate bonding makes a continuous chain of covalently bonded atoms in each strand of DNA
or RNA nucleotides, which forms a strong “backbone” in the molecule.

A1.2.6—DNA as a double helix made of two antiparallel strands of nucleotides with two strands linked by

bases, or the numbers of hydrogen bonds.

B1.1.8—Hydrophobic properties of lipids
Lipids are substances in living organisms that dissolve in non-polar solvents but are only sparingly soluble in aqueous solvents. Lipids include fats, oils, waxes and steroids.

B1.2.10—Effect of polar and non-polar amino acids on tertiary structure of proteins
In proteins that are soluble in water, hydrophobic amino acids are clustered in the core of globular proteins. Integral proteins have regions with hydrophobic amino acids, helping them to embed in membranes.

B2.1.12—Formation of phospholipid bilayers as a consequence of the hydrophobic and hydrophilic regions
Students should use and understand the term “amphipathic”.

Students will be able to apply creative thinking to design, plan, and implement a CAS project, considering resources, constraints, and ethical implications.

cas-ib-dp-dp-innovation-project.name: 创新项目
cas-ib-dp-dp-innovation-project.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to initiate, plan, and execute a sustained, collaborative project that addresses a community need or personal passion through an innovative approach.
Students will be able to demonstrate how their innovation project integrates at least two of the three CAS strands (Creativity, Activity, Service) and involves a series of sequential stages.

cas-ib-dp-dp-physical-activity.name: 体育锻炼
cas-ib-dp-dp-physical-activity.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to participate in regular, purposeful physical exertion that contributes to a healthy lifestyle and presents personal goals and challenges.
Students will be able to reflect on how engagement in physical activity enhances their understanding of personal well-being, perseverance, and resilience.

cas-ib-dp-dp-sports-competition.name: 运动竞赛
cas-ib-dp-dp-sports-competition.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to engage in organized sports or physical competitions, demonstrating an understanding of teamwork, fair play, and personal goal-setting.
Students will be able to analyze the personal and social dynamics experienced through competition, including handling success and adversity with integrity.

cas-ib-dp-dp-outdoor-adventure.name: 户外探险
cas-ib-dp-dp-outdoor-adventure.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to undertake challenging expeditions or outdoor pursuits that require preparation, perseverance, and the development of new skills in unfamiliar environments.
Students will be able to evaluate how outdoor adventures fostered an appreciation for the natural world, personal limits, and collaborative problem-solving.

cas-ib-dp-dp-community-service.name: 社区服务
cas-ib-dp-dp-community-service.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to engage in sustained, reciprocal collaboration with a community, addressing genuine needs while developing an understanding of social and civic responsibility.
Students will be able to critically examine the ethical dimensions of their service, including power dynamics, sustainability, and the impact on both self and community.

cas-ib-dp-dp-volunteer-work.name: 志愿工作
cas-ib-dp-dp-volunteer-work.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to commit their time and energy to an organization or cause without financial reward, demonstrating reliability, empathy, and a willingness to contribute to the work of others.
Students will be able to articulate the skills and attitudes developed through voluntary service and how these relate to their personal and academic growth.

cas-ib-dp-dp-advocacy.name: 倡议与倡导
cas-ib-dp-dp-advocacy.subject: cas
curriculum_points: Students will be able to research, plan, and execute an initiative to raise awareness or promote action on a local or global issue of significance, demonstrating ethical engagement.
Students will be able to analyze the role of communication, persuasion, and collaboration in advocacy work and reflect on their personal stance and influence.

cas-ib-dp-dp-cas-project.name: CAS Project（1 月）
cas-ib-dp-dp-cas-project.subject: cas
cas-ib-dp-dp-cas-project.domain: CAS 项目与反思 / CAS Project & Reflection / CAS Project & Reflection
curriculum_points: Students will be able to collaboratively design, implement, and evaluate a significant CAS Project of at least one month's duration that integrates one or more CAS strands and addresses a defined purpose.
Students will be able to demonstrate project management skills, including planning, division of roles, problem-solving, and sustained collaboration, throughout the CAS Project lifecycle.

cas-ib-dp-dp-learning-outcomes.name: 7 项学习成果
cas-ib-dp-dp-learning-outcomes.subject: cas
cas-ib-dp-dp-learning-outcomes.domain: CAS 项目与反思 / CAS Project & Reflection / CAS Project & Reflection
curriculum_points: Students will be able to provide evidence in their CAS portfolio of achieving the seven prescribed learning outcomes through a balanced range of CAS experiences.
Students will be able to demonstrate personal development across the seven learning outcomes, showing increased self-awareness, new skills, and engagement with issues of global importance.

cas-ib-dp-dp-reflection-portfolio.name: 反思作品集
cas-ib-dp-dp-reflection-portfolio.subject: cas
cas-ib-dp-dp-reflection-portfolio.domain: CAS 项目与反思 / CAS Project & Reflection / CAS Project & Reflection
curriculum_points: Students will be able to maintain a curated, ongoing CAS portfolio that documents experiences, collects evidence, and provides structured reflections demonstrating growth against the CAS learning outcomes.
Students will be able to use their reflection portfolio to identify patterns in their learning, make connections between different experiences, and evaluate their overall CAS journey.

cas-ib-dp-dp-interview-final.name: 终期面谈
cas-ib-dp-dp-interview-final.subject: cas
cas-ib-dp-dp-interview-final.domain: CAS 项目与反思 / CAS Project & Reflection / CAS Project & Reflection
curriculum_points: Students will be able to participate in a final, structured interview with their CAS coordinator to present and discuss their CAS journey, highlighting personal growth, achievements, and insights gained.
Students will be able to articulate how CAS has influenced their development as an IB learner and their preparedness for future endeavors beyond the Diploma Programme.

chem-ib-dp-introduction-particles.name: 物质的粒子本质与物态
chem-ib-dp-introduction-particles.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-introduction-particles.domain: 结构 1 · 物质的模型 Structure 1 · Models of the Particulate Nature of Matter
curriculum_points:

chem-ib-dp-atomic-structure.name: 原子结构（电子构型/同位素）
chem-ib-dp-atomic-structure.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-atomic-structure.domain: 结构 1 · 物质的模型 Structure 1 · Models of the Particulate Nature of Matter
curriculum_points: Atomic structure

chem-ib-dp-quantum-atom-hl.name: 量子力学的原子模型（HL）
chem-ib-dp-quantum-atom-hl.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-quantum-atom-hl.domain: 结构 1 · 物质的模型 Structure 1 · Models of the Particulate Nature of Matter
curriculum_points: Describe the quantum mechanical model of the atom, including the concepts of atomic orbitals, quantum numbers (n, l, m_l, m_s), and electron spin, and use it to explain atomic emission spectra and ionization energies (AO2, AO3).

Apply the Aufbau principle, Hund's rule, and the Pauli exclusion principle to deduce electron configurations for atoms and ions up to Z = 36, and explain the anomalous configurations of Cr and Cu (AO2).

chem-ib-dp-ionic-bonding.name: 离子键
chem-ib-dp-ionic-bonding.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-ionic-bonding.domain: 结构 2 · 化学键与结构 Structure 2 · The Molecular World
curriculum_points: Explain the formation of ionic bonds between metals and non-metals as a result of electron transfer, leading to the formation of cations and anions held together by electrostatic forces (AO2).

Deduce the formula and name of an ionic compound from the charges on the constituent ions, and describe the typical physical properties (high melting point, brittleness, electrical conductivity) of ionic compounds in relation to their lattice structure (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-covalent-bonding.name: 共价键（VSEPR/极性/杂化）
chem-ib-dp-covalent-bonding.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-covalent-bonding.domain: 结构 2 · 化学键与结构 Structure 2 · The Molecular World
curriculum_points: Describe the formation of a covalent bond by the sharing of a pair of electrons between atoms, and represent it using Lewis (electron dot) structures for molecules and ions containing up to six electron pairs on each atom (AO2).

Explain the shapes of, and bond angles in, simple molecules and ions with up to six electron pairs (including lone pairs) on the central atom using the valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-metallic-bonding.name: 金属键
chem-ib-dp-metallic-bonding.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-metallic-bonding.domain: 结构 2 · 化学键与结构 Structure 2 · The Molecular World
curriculum_points: Describe the metallic bond as the electrostatic attraction between a lattice of positive ions and delocalized electrons, and use this model to explain the typical physical properties of metals, such as malleability, ductility, and electrical and thermal conductivity (AO2).
Explain the trends in melting points of metals across a period and down a group in terms of the strength of the metallic bond, relating it to ionic radius and charge (AO3).

chem-ib-dp-imf-hl.name: 分子间作用力（HL：伦敦/偶极/氢键）
chem-ib-dp-imf-hl.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-imf-hl.domain: 结构 2 · 化学键与结构 Structure 2 · The Molecular World
curriculum_points:
c (cid:31) f(cid:30)
E =hf

Speed of light in vacuum c 3.00 × 108 m s−1
Planck constant h 6.63 × 10−34 J s

chem-ib-dp-periodic-table.name: 周期表与周期性趋势
chem-ib-dp-periodic-table.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-periodic-table.domain: 结构 3 · 周期表与分类 Structure 3 · Classification of Matter
curriculum_points: Periodic table & trends

chem-ib-dp-functional-groups.name: 有机化学官能团与命名
chem-ib-dp-functional-groups.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-functional-groups.domain: 结构 3 · 周期表与分类 Structure 3 · Classification of Matter
curriculum_points: Identify and name organic compounds containing up to six carbon atoms and one or more of the following functional groups: alkene, alkyne, halogenoalkane, alcohol, aldehyde, ketone, carboxylic acid, ester, amine, nitrile, amide, and arene, using IUPAC nomenclature (AO1).
Deduce structural formulas and apply IUPAC rules to name isomers of non-cyclic organic compounds containing up to six carbon atoms and one or more functional groups (AO2).

chem-ib-dp-spectroscopy-hl.name: 光谱分析（HL：IR/NMR/MS）
chem-ib-dp-spectroscopy-hl.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-spectroscopy-hl.domain: 结构 3 · 周期表与分类 Structure 3 · Classification of Matter
curriculum_points: Analyze and interpret data from mass spectrometry (MS) to determine the relative molecular mass of a compound and identify fragments to gain information about structure (AO2, AO3).

Deduce the structure of organic compounds from information provided by a combination of analytical techniques, including high resolution 1H NMR (number of signals, chemical shift, integration, and splitting patterns), infrared (IR) spectroscopy, and mass spectrometry (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-enthalpy.name: 焓变与反应热
chem-ib-dp-enthalpy.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-enthalpy.domain: 反应性 1 · 热力学 Reactivity 1 · What Drives Chemical Reactions
curriculum_points: Define and apply the terms standard enthalpy change of reaction (ΔH°), formation (ΔH_f°), combustion (ΔH_c°), and neutralization (AO1).
Calculate the heat energy change (q) for a reaction using the expression q = mcΔT, and apply calorimetry data to determine the enthalpy change per mole for a reaction (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-hess-bonding-energy.name: Hess 定律与键能
chem-ib-dp-hess-bonding-energy.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-hess-bonding-energy.domain: 反应性 1 · 热力学 Reactivity 1 · What Drives Chemical Reactions
curriculum_points: State Hess's Law and use it to calculate enthalpy changes from provided data, including constructing and using enthalpy cycles (AO2).
Calculate approximate enthalpy changes from average bond enthalpy data, and explain why such values may differ from those determined using Hess's Law (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-entropy-free-energy-hl.name: 熵与自由能（HL）
chem-ib-dp-entropy-free-energy-hl.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-entropy-free-energy-hl.domain: 反应性 1 · 热力学 Reactivity 1 · What Drives Chemical Reactions
curriculum_points: Predict whether a reaction will be spontaneous by calculating the standard entropy change, ΔS°, for the system and the standard Gibbs free energy change, ΔG°, using the equation ΔG° = ΔH° - TΔS° (AO2).
Explain the relationship between the total entropy change (ΔS_total), the feasibility of a reaction, and the temperature at which a reaction becomes spontaneous (AO3).

chem-ib-dp-stoichiometry.name: 化学计量学（mol/方程式/产率）
chem-ib-dp-stoichiometry.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-stoichiometry.domain: 反应性 2 · 化学变化 Reactivity 2 · How Much, How Fast, How Far
curriculum_points: Apply the mole concept to substances, using the relationships n = m/M, n = V (gas)/V_m, and n = cV to determine amounts of reactants and products in chemical reactions (AO2).
Determine the limiting reactant and the theoretical yield of a reaction from given data, and calculate percentage yield and atom economy (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-kinetics.name: 反应动力学（速率/活化能/催化剂）
chem-ib-dp-kinetics.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-kinetics.domain: 反应性 2 · 化学变化 Reactivity 2 · How Much, How Fast, How Far
curriculum_points: Describe the factors that affect the rate of a chemical reaction (concentration, pressure, temperature, surface area, catalyst) in terms of collision theory (AO2).

Analyze concentration-time and rate-concentration graphs to deduce zero-, first-, and second-order reactions, and determine the rate expression and the value of the rate constant, k, from initial rates data (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-equilibrium.name: 化学平衡（Le Chatelier/Kc/Kp）
chem-ib-dp-equilibrium.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-equilibrium.domain: 反应性 2 · 化学变化 Reactivity 2 · How Much, How Fast, How Far
curriculum_points: State the characteristics of a system in dynamic equilibrium, and deduce the equilibrium constant expression, K_c, from the equation for a homogeneous reaction (AO1, AO2).

Apply Le Châtelier's principle to predict the qualitative effects of changes in concentration, pressure, and temperature on the position of equilibrium and on the value of the equilibrium constant, explaining the choice of conditions in industrial processes (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-acids-bases.name: 酸碱理论（pH/Ka/Kb/缓冲液）
chem-ib-dp-acids-bases.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-acids-bases.domain: 反应性 3 · 电子转移 Reactivity 3 · Electron Transfer & Proton Transfer
curriculum_points: Define acids and bases according to the Brønsted–Lowry theory, and identify conjugate acid-base pairs in proton transfer reactions (AO1, AO2).

Distinguish between strong and weak acids and bases in terms of the extent of dissociation, and calculate the pH, pOH, [H+], and [OH-] for strong acids and bases and weak acids given appropriate data (AO2, AO3).

chem-ib-dp-redox-electrochemistry.name: 氧化还原与电化学
chem-ib-dp-redox-electrochemistry.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-redox-electrochemistry.domain: 反应性 3 · 电子转移 Reactivity 3 · Electron Transfer & Proton Transfer
curriculum_points:
c = fλ
E = hf

pH = −log [H O+]
10 3
or
pH = −log [H+]

K = [H+] [OH−]
w

chem-ib-dp-organic-mechanisms-hl.name: 有机反应机理（HL：SN1/SN2/E1/E2）
chem-ib-dp-organic-mechanisms-hl.subject: chemistry
chem-ib-dp-organic-mechanisms-hl.domain: 反应性 3 · 电子转移 Reactivity 3 · Electron Transfer & Proton Transfer
curriculum_points: Describe and explain the mechanisms of nucleophilic substitution reactions (S_N1 and S_N2) and electrophilic addition reactions, using curly arrows to show the movement of electron pairs (AO2, AO3).

Predict the major and minor products of organic reactions, including those involving regioselectivity (Markovnikov's rule) and stereochemistry, and explain the formation of stereoisomers in certain addition and substitution reactions (AO2, AO3).

economics-ib-dp-dp-scarcity-choice.name: 稀缺与选择
economics-ib-dp-dp-scarcity-choice.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the central economic problem of scarcity, choice, and opportunity cost, using production possibility curve (PPC) models to illustrate these concepts.
Students will be able to evaluate the trade-offs and opportunity costs involved in economic decision-making by individuals, firms, and governments.

economics-ib-dp-dp-demand-supply.name: 供求分析
economics-ib-dp-dp-demand-supply.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct and analyse demand and supply diagrams to illustrate market equilibrium, and explain the causes and consequences of shifts in and movements along these curves.
Students will be able to apply the model of demand and supply to real-world markets, explaining how the price mechanism allocates resources and rations goods.

economics-ib-dp-dp-elasticity.name: 弹性
economics-ib-dp-dp-elasticity.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to calculate and interpret price elasticity of demand (PED), price elasticity of supply (PES), income elasticity of demand (YED), and cross-price elasticity of demand (XED).
Students will be able to evaluate the significance of elasticity for stakeholders, including consumers, producers, and governments, in areas such as tax incidence and revenue.

economics-ib-dp-dp-market-failure.name: 市场失灵
economics-ib-dp-dp-market-failure.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain, with examples, the concepts of negative externalities, positive externalities, common pool resources, and public goods as sources of market failure.
Students will be able to illustrate market failure using marginal social benefit (MSB) and marginal social cost (MSC) diagrams, identifying the welfare loss.

economics-ib-dp-dp-govt-intervention.name: 政府干预
economics-ib-dp-dp-govt-intervention.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the use of government intervention methods, including indirect taxes, subsidies, tradable permits, and direct provision, to correct market failures.
Students will be able to analyse the consequences of government intervention, including price ceilings and price floors, on market outcomes, stakeholders, and government budgets.

economics-ib-dp-dp-firm-theory-hl.name: 厂商理论（HL）
economics-ib-dp-dp-firm-theory-hl.subject: economics
curriculum_points: HL students will be able to calculate and interpret revenue, cost, and profit data for firms in different market structures (perfect competition, monopoly, monopolistic competition).
HL students will be able to diagrammatically analyse short-run and long-run equilibrium positions for profit-maximizing firms and evaluate the efficiency outcomes of different market structures.

economics-ib-dp-dp-gdp.name: GDP 与国民收入
economics-ib-dp-dp-gdp.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the circular flow of income model and calculate nominal and real GDP using the expenditure, income, and output methods.
Students will be able to evaluate the uses and limitations of GDP statistics as a measure of economic well-being and standard of living.

economics-ib-dp-dp-ad-as.name: AD-AS 模型
economics-ib-dp-dp-ad-as.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct and interpret the aggregate demand (AD) and aggregate supply (AS) model to illustrate macroeconomic equilibrium, the business cycle, and inflationary/deflationary gaps.
Students will be able to analyse the causes of shifts in the AD and AS curves and evaluate their impacts on the price level, real output, and employment.

economics-ib-dp-dp-unemployment.name: 失业
economics-ib-dp-dp-unemployment.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between the main types of unemployment (frictional, structural, cyclical, seasonal) and explain their causes.
Students will be able to calculate the unemployment rate and evaluate the economic and social consequences of unemployment for different stakeholders.

economics-ib-dp-dp-inflation.name: 通货膨胀
economics-ib-dp-dp-inflation.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between demand-pull and cost-push inflation using AD/AS analysis and calculate inflation rates from a set of price index data.
Students will be able to evaluate the consequences of inflation and deflation for consumers, firms, the government, and the economy as a whole.

economics-ib-dp-dp-fiscal-monetary.name: 财政与货币政策
economics-ib-dp-dp-fiscal-monetary.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain how expansionary and contractionary fiscal policy (changes in government spending and taxation) and monetary policy (changes in interest rates) can be used to influence aggregate demand.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness, strengths, and limitations of demand-side policies in achieving macroeconomic objectives such as low unemployment and price stability.

economics-ib-dp-dp-economic-growth.name: 经济增长
economics-ib-dp-dp-economic-growth.subject: economics
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between economic growth (an increase in real GDP) and economic development (broader improvements in living standards).

Students will be able to analyse the sources of long-term economic growth (increases in quantity/quality of factors of production, technological advances) and evaluate its potential consequences, including environmental impacts.

economics-ib-dp-dp-intl-trade.name: 国际贸易
economics-ib-dp-dp-intl-trade.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-intl-trade.domain: 全球经济 / Global Economy / Global Economy
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the theory of comparative advantage and illustrate the potential gains from trade and specialization using diagrams.
Students will be able to analyse the arguments for and against trade protectionism (tariffs, quotas, subsidies) and evaluate their impacts on stakeholders.

economics-ib-dp-dp-exchange-rates.name: 汇率
economics-ib-dp-dp-exchange-rates.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-exchange-rates.domain: 全球经济 / Global Economy / Global Economy
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between floating, fixed, and managed exchange rate systems and illustrate how they are determined in a foreign exchange market diagram.
Students will be able to analyse the causes and consequences of exchange rate fluctuations for a nation's inflation, unemployment, current account balance, and economic growth.

economics-ib-dp-dp-balance-payments.name: 国际收支
economics-ib-dp-dp-balance-payments.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-balance-payments.domain: 全球经济 / Global Economy / Global Economy
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the structure of the balance of payments accounts, distinguishing between the current account and the capital/financial account.
Students will be able to analyse the causes and consequences of current account deficits and surpluses, and evaluate policy responses to address imbalances.

economics-ib-dp-dp-economic-integration.name: 经济一体化
economics-ib-dp-dp-economic-integration.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-economic-integration.domain: 全球经济 / Global Economy / Global Economy
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the different forms of economic integration, from preferential trade agreements to monetary unions, using real-world examples.
Students will be able to evaluate the potential economic benefits (e.g., trade creation) and costs (e.g., trade diversion, loss of sovereignty) of regional economic integration.

economics-ib-dp-dp-development-econ.name: 发展经济学
economics-ib-dp-dp-development-econ.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-development-econ.domain: 全球经济 / Global Economy / Global Economy
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare and contrast alternative measures of economic development, including the Human Development Index (HDI) and the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI).
Students will be able to evaluate the role of domestic factors (e.g., education, institutional quality) and international factors (e.g., debt, aid, trade) in economic development.

economics-ib-dp-dp-efficiency-equity.name: 效率与公平
economics-ib-dp-dp-efficiency-equity.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-efficiency-equity.domain: 关键概念 / Key Concepts / Key Concepts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between allocative, productive, and dynamic efficiency and explain the trade-off between equity and efficiency in economic policy.
Students will be able to evaluate government policies, such as progressive taxation and transfer payments, aimed at promoting greater equity (fairness) in the distribution of income and wealth.

economics-ib-dp-dp-sustainability-econ.name: 可持续性
economics-ib-dp-dp-sustainability-econ.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-sustainability-econ.domain: 关键概念 / Key Concepts / Key Concepts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the concept of sustainable development and evaluate the role of market-based (e.g., carbon taxes) and government-led strategies in promoting environmental sustainability.
Students will be able to critically assess the potential conflict between the macroeconomic objectives of economic growth and environmental sustainability.

economics-ib-dp-dp-inequality.name: 不平等
economics-ib-dp-dp-inequality.subject: economics
economics-ib-dp-dp-inequality.domain: 关键概念 / Key Concepts / Key Concepts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the causes of income and wealth inequality within and between countries using tools such as the Lorenz curve and Gini coefficient.
Students will be able to evaluate the economic and social impacts of inequality and assess the effectiveness of policies designed to reduce it.

ee-ib-dp-dp-choose-subject.name: 选题与学科
ee-ib-dp-dp-choose-subject.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-choose-subject.domain: 研究设计 / Research Design / Research Design
curriculum_points: Students will be able to select an appropriate Diploma Programme subject for their Extended Essay, ensuring it aligns with their academic interests and the subject's availability within the IB framework.
Students will justify their subject choice by demonstrating a clear understanding of the subject's disciplinary conventions and its suitability for a 4000-word independent investigation.

ee-ib-dp-dp-research-question.name: 研究问题（RQ）
ee-ib-dp-dp-research-question.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-research-question.domain: 研究设计 / Research Design / Research Design
curriculum_points: Students will formulate a clear, focused, and arguable research question that is appropriate for an in-depth, 4000-word academic investigation within their chosen subject.
Students will refine their initial research question through preliminary reading to ensure it is manageable, specific, and conducive to critical analysis and argument.

ee-ib-dp-dp-research-methodology.name: 研究方法
ee-ib-dp-dp-research-methodology.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-research-methodology.domain: 研究设计 / Research Design / Research Design
curriculum_points: Students will design and justify a coherent research methodology appropriate to their subject and research question, demonstrating an understanding of primary and/or secondary research methods.
Students will apply their chosen methodology systematically, explaining its application and acknowledging any limitations in their research process.

ee-ib-dp-dp-literature-review.name: 文献综述
ee-ib-dp-dp-literature-review.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-literature-review.domain: 研究设计 / Research Design / Research Design
curriculum_points: Students will conduct a comprehensive review of relevant scholarly literature, situating their research question within the existing academic discourse of the field.
Students will synthesize and critically evaluate the literature, identifying gaps, debates, or contexts that their own investigation will address.

ee-ib-dp-dp-outline-structure.name: 大纲与结构
ee-ib-dp-dp-outline-structure.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-outline-structure.domain: 写作阶段 / Writing Process / Writing Process
curriculum_points: Students will construct a logical and coherent outline for their essay, ensuring the structure effectively supports the development of their argument and analysis.
Students will organize their essay according to the formal conventions of academic writing in their subject area, including a clear introduction, development, and conclusion.

ee-ib-dp-dp-argument-analysis.name: 论证与分析
ee-ib-dp-dp-argument-analysis.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-argument-analysis.domain: 写作阶段 / Writing Process / Writing Process
curriculum_points: Students will develop a sustained, reasoned argument in response to their research question, supported by critical analysis of evidence.
Students will demonstrate higher-order thinking skills by evaluating different perspectives, interpreting data or sources, and drawing well-substantiated conclusions.

ee-ib-dp-dp-citations.name: 引用规范（MLA/APA）
ee-ib-dp-dp-citations.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-citations.domain: 写作阶段 / Writing Process / Writing Process
curriculum_points: Students will consistently and accurately apply a recognized system of academic referencing (e.g., MLA, APA, Chicago) to acknowledge all sources used in their work.
Students will understand and avoid academic misconduct by distinguishing between their own ideas and those of others through proper paraphrasing, quoting, and citation.

ee-ib-dp-dp-formatting.name: 格式规范
ee-ib-dp-dp-formatting.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-formatting.domain: 写作阶段 / Writing Process / Writing Process
curriculum_points: Students will adhere to the formal presentation requirements for the Extended Essay, including title page, abstract, contents page, page numbering, and overall layout as specified in the EE guide.
Students will ensure their final submission is professionally presented and meets the technical requirements for assessment.

ee-ib-dp-dp-first-reflection.name: 初次反思
ee-ib-dp-dp-first-reflection.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-first-reflection.domain: 反思与面谈 / Reflection & RPPF / Reflection & RPPF
curriculum_points: Students will complete the first reflection session on the Reflections on planning and progress form (RPPF), reflecting on their initial ideas, the formulation of their research question, and their planning process.

ee-ib-dp-dp-interim-reflection.name: 中期反思
ee-ib-dp-dp-interim-reflection.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-interim-reflection.domain: 反思与面谈 / Reflection & RPPF / Reflection & RPPF
curriculum_points: Students will complete the interim reflection on the RPPF, evaluating the progress of their research and writing, discussing challenges encountered, and outlining their plan for completion.

ee-ib-dp-dp-viva-voce.name: 终审面谈
ee-ib-dp-dp-viva-voce.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-viva-voce.domain: 反思与面谈 / Reflection & RPPF / Reflection & RPPF
curriculum_points: Following the completion of the Extended Essay, students will participate in a concluding interview (viva voce) with their supervisor to reflect on their learning journey, the research process, and the outcomes of their work.

ee-ib-dp-dp-rppf.name: RPPF 反思表
ee-ib-dp-dp-rppf.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-rppf.domain: 反思与面谈 / Reflection & RPPF / Reflection & RPPF
curriculum_points: Students will complete all three mandatory reflections on the official RPPF, using the form to document their engagement with the research process and their intellectual and personal development.

ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-sciences.name: 理科 EE 要点
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-sciences.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-sciences.domain: 学科差异 / Subject-Specific Tips / Subject-Specific
curriculum_points: Students undertaking a Science EE will design and conduct a safe and ethically sound investigation, collecting and processing primary or secondary data to explore a specific scientific question.
Students will analyze their results using appropriate scientific methods and techniques, discussing their significance in relation to established scientific theory and the research question.

ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-humanities.name: 文科 EE 要点
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-humanities.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-humanities.domain: 学科差异 / Subject-Specific Tips / Subject-Specific
curriculum_points: Students undertaking a Humanities EE (e.g., History, Economics, Psychology) will construct a critical argument based on the systematic analysis and evaluation of primary and secondary sources.
Students will demonstrate an understanding of the theoretical frameworks and historiographical or disciplinary debates relevant to their chosen topic.

ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-math.name: 数学 EE 要点
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-math.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-math.domain: 学科差异 / Subject-Specific Tips / Subject-Specific
curriculum_points: Students undertaking a Mathematics EE will engage in the exploration of a specific mathematical topic, demonstrating rigorous and precise mathematical reasoning and communication.
The essay may involve applying mathematics to solve a problem, proving theorems, or analyzing mathematical models, with a clear emphasis on the beauty and utility of mathematics itself.

ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-world-studies.name: 世界研究 EE
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-world-studies.subject: ee
ee-ib-dp-dp-ee-world-studies.domain: 学科差异 / Subject-Specific Tips / Subject-Specific
curriculum_points: Students undertaking a World Studies EE will formulate a research question of contemporary global significance that examines an issue through the lens of at least two IB disciplines.
Students will integrate concepts, theories, and methods from their chosen disciplines to construct a coherent interdisciplinary analysis of the complex global issue.

english-ib-dp-dp-close-reading.name: 文本细读
english-ib-dp-dp-close-reading.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-close-reading.domain: 读者-作者-文本 / Readers, Writers, Texts / Readers, Writers, Texts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to perform a detailed, line-by-line analysis of a literary or non-literary text, identifying and interpreting the effects of specific word choices, imagery, and syntax.
Students will be able to construct a coherent interpretation of a text's local meaning and overall significance based on sustained attention to textual detail.

english-ib-dp-dp-literary-analysis.name: 文学分析
english-ib-dp-dp-literary-analysis.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-literary-analysis.domain: 读者-作者-文本 / Readers, Writers, Texts / Readers, Writers, Texts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to formulate a clear, arguable thesis about a literary work's meaning, supported by well-chosen textual evidence and logical reasoning.
Students will be able to evaluate how various literary elements (e.g., character, setting, theme, structure) interact to shape a work's overall impact and meaning.

english-ib-dp-dp-narrative-techniques.name: 叙事技巧
english-ib-dp-dp-narrative-techniques.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-narrative-techniques.domain: 读者-作者-文本 / Readers, Writers, Texts / Readers, Writers, Texts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse and evaluate the function of narrative point of view, voice, focalization, and temporal structure in shaping meaning and reader response in prose fiction.
Students will be able to explain how authors use techniques such as characterization, dialogue, and plot development to construct narrative and convey thematic concerns.

english-ib-dp-dp-poetry-analysis.name: 诗歌分析
english-ib-dp-dp-poetry-analysis.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-poetry-analysis.domain: 读者-作者-文本 / Readers, Writers, Texts / Readers, Writers, Texts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the relationship between form (e.g., sonnet, free verse, stanzaic structure) and content in a poem, explaining how poetic techniques (meter, rhyme, enjambment) contribute to its meaning and effect.
Students will be able to interpret complex imagery, metaphor, and symbolism in poetry, situating their analysis within the poem's broader thematic and emotional landscape.

english-ib-dp-dp-non-literary.name: 非文学文本
english-ib-dp-dp-non-literary.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-non-literary.domain: 读者-作者-文本 / Readers, Writers, Texts / Readers, Writers, Texts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to apply principles of textual analysis to a range of non-literary text types (e.g., advertisements, opinion columns, speeches, infographics), identifying their purpose, audience, and contextual constraints.
Students will be able to deconstruct the use of visual and linguistic design elements in non-literary texts to understand how they construct meaning and position the audience.

english-ib-dp-dp-context-production.name: 创作语境
english-ib-dp-dp-context-production.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-context-production.domain: 时间与空间 / Time and Space / Time & Space
curriculum_points: Students will be able to research and explain how the circumstances of a text's creation (author's background, historical events, publishing milieu, technological means) influence its form, content, and potential meanings.

english-ib-dp-dp-context-reception.name: 接受语境
english-ib-dp-dp-context-reception.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-context-reception.domain: 时间与空间 / Time and Space / Time & Space
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate how different audiences, across time and culture, might receive and interpret a text differently, considering factors like ideology, social norms, and medium of consumption.

english-ib-dp-dp-cultural-context.name: 文化语境
english-ib-dp-dp-cultural-context.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-cultural-context.domain: 时间与空间 / Time and Space / Time & Space
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how texts reflect, represent, or challenge the values, beliefs, and social practices of specific cultures or subcultures.
Students will be able to explore the role of texts in shaping cultural identity and negotiating cultural power dynamics.

english-ib-dp-dp-historical-period.name: 历史背景
english-ib-dp-dp-historical-period.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-historical-period.domain: 时间与空间 / Time and Space / Time & Space
curriculum_points: Students will be able to situate a text within its specific historical period, drawing connections between its themes, style, or concerns and broader historical developments or intellectual movements of the era.

english-ib-dp-dp-genre-conventions.name: 文体传统
english-ib-dp-dp-genre-conventions.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-genre-conventions.domain: 互文与跨文本 / Intertextuality / Intertextuality
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify the defining formal and thematic conventions of major literary genres (e.g., tragedy, satire, bildungsroman) and analyse how individual texts adhere to or subvert these conventions.
Students will be able to classify non-literary texts by genre (e.g., editorial, manifesto, travel writing) and evaluate the strategic use of generic conventions to achieve a communicative purpose.

english-ib-dp-dp-comparative-study.name: 比较研究
english-ib-dp-dp-comparative-study.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-comparative-study.domain: 互文与跨文本 / Intertextuality / Intertextuality
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct a focused, insightful comparison of two or more texts, moving beyond superficial similarities/differences to explore deeper connections in theme, technique, or context.
Students will be able to use comparison as a critical tool to illuminate the distinctive features and broader significance of each text in the pair or group.

english-ib-dp-dp-literary-movements.name: 文学流派
english-ib-dp-dp-literary-movements.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-literary-movements.domain: 互文与跨文本 / Intertextuality / Intertextuality
curriculum_points: Students will be able to define key characteristics of major literary movements (e.g., Romanticism, Modernism, Postcolonialism) and analyse how specific texts exemplify the aesthetic and philosophical principles of a movement.

english-ib-dp-dp-ia-oral.name: IA 个人口头
english-ib-dp-dp-ia-oral.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-ia-oral.domain: 评估任务 / Assessment Components / Assessment
curriculum_points: Students will be able to deliver a structured, 10-minute oral presentation that analyses the relationship between a global issue and the content and form of one literary and one non-literary text.
Students will be able to engage in a thoughtful, spontaneous discussion with the teacher, demonstrating the ability to expand upon and defend their analysis in response to probing questions.

english-ib-dp-dp-paper1.name: Paper 1 · 引导分析
english-ib-dp-dp-paper1.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-paper1.domain: 评估任务 / Assessment Components / Assessment
curriculum_points: Students will be able to write a coherent, analytical essay in response to an unseen literary or non-literary text, guided by a provided prompt, within a timed examination setting.
Students will be able to select and analyse salient features of the text (both linguistic and stylistic), building a sustained argument that addresses the guiding question.

english-ib-dp-dp-paper2.name: Paper 2 · 比较小论文
english-ib-dp-dp-paper2.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-paper2.domain: 评估任务 / Assessment Components / Assessment
curriculum_points: Students will be able to write a comparative essay, under timed conditions, in response to one of four general questions, drawing on their knowledge of at least two literary works studied in the course.
Students will be able to structure an essay that integrates comparison throughout, using relevant evidence from both works to develop a persuasive response to the chosen question.

english-ib-dp-dp-hl-essay.name: HL 论文（4000词）
english-ib-dp-dp-hl-essay.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-hl-essay.domain: 评估任务 / Assessment Components / Assessment
curriculum_points: Students will be able to produce a formal, 1200-1500 word academic essay that develops a clear line of inquiry into a literary or non-literary text(s) studied in the course, demonstrating independent research and critical thinking.

Students will be able to construct a sophisticated, sustained argument, supported by precise textual evidence and engagement with relevant scholarly sources, and presented with appropriate academic conventions.

english-ib-dp-dp-rhetoric-devices.name: 修辞手法
english-ib-dp-dp-rhetoric-devices.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-rhetoric-devices.domain: 语言与表达 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and analyse the strategic use of rhetorical devices (e.g., anaphora, antithesis, rhetorical questions) in both literary and non-literary texts, explaining their effect on the audience and contribution to the author's purpose.

english-ib-dp-dp-register-tone.name: 语域与语气
english-ib-dp-dp-register-tone.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-register-tone.domain: 语言与表达 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how variations in register (levels of formality, technicality) and tone (e.g., ironic, solemn, polemical) are constructed through diction, syntax, and modality to position the reader and shape meaning.

english-ib-dp-dp-persuasion.name: 说服与修辞
english-ib-dp-dp-persuasion.subject: english
english-ib-dp-dp-persuasion.domain: 语言与表达 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: Students will be able to deconstruct the persuasive strategies employed in argumentative texts, evaluating the use of logical appeals (logos), emotional appeals (pathos), and ethical appeals (ethos).
Students will be able to analyse how texts seek to influence audience beliefs, attitudes, or behaviours, considering the interplay of argument, style, and context.

history-ib-dp-dp-wwi.name: 第一次世界大战
history-ib-dp-dp-wwi.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-wwi.domain: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the long-term and short-term causes of the First World War, evaluating the relative significance of factors such as militarism, alliances, imperialism, and nationalism (HL/SL).
Students will be able to assess the social, economic, and political consequences of WWI, including the Treaty of Versailles and its impact on the post-war order.

history-ib-dp-dp-russian-rev.name: 俄国革命
history-ib-dp-dp-russian-rev.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-russian-rev.domain: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the causes of the 1917 February and October Revolutions, evaluating the roles of the Tsarist regime, WWI, and revolutionary leadership (HL/SL).
Students will be able to compare and contrast the nature of the Provisional Government and the Bolshevik state under Lenin.

history-ib-dp-dp-interwar.name: 战间期
history-ib-dp-dp-interwar.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-interwar.domain: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the challenges to democracy and the rise of authoritarian states in the interwar period, using specific case studies (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the causes and consequences of the Great Depression on international relations and domestic politics.

history-ib-dp-dp-wwii.name: 第二次世界大战
history-ib-dp-dp-wwii.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-wwii.domain: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History
curriculum_points: Students will be able to assess the causes of WWII, analysing the role of appeasement, the failure of collective security, and the aggressive foreign policies of Germany, Italy, and Japan (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the social, economic, and technological impact of total war on civilian populations.

history-ib-dp-dp-cold-war.name: 冷战
history-ib-dp-dp-cold-war.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-cold-war.domain: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the origins of the Cold War, analysing ideological, political, and economic differences between the superpowers (HL/SL).
Students will be able to compare and contrast the nature and impact of Cold War crises (e.g., Berlin, Cuba) and conflicts in different regions.

history-ib-dp-dp-decolonization.name: 非殖民化
history-ib-dp-dp-decolonization.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-decolonization.domain: 20世纪世界史 / 20th Century World History / 20th Century World History
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the causes and processes of decolonization in two regions, evaluating the role of nationalist movements, colonial policies, and the Cold War (HL/SL).
Students will be able to assess the challenges faced by newly independent states in the post-colonial period.

history-ib-dp-dp-hitler.name: 希特勒统治下的德国
history-ib-dp-dp-hitler.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-hitler.domain: 权威与威权 / Authoritarian States / Authoritarian States
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the methods used by the Nazi regime to establish and maintain total control, including the use of terror, propaganda, and economic policies (HL/SL).
Students will be able to analyse the ideological roots and implementation of Nazi racial and social policies, including the persecution of minorities and the Holocaust.

history-ib-dp-dp-stalin.name: 斯大林的苏联
history-ib-dp-dp-stalin.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-stalin.domain: 权威与威权 / Authoritarian States / Authoritarian States
curriculum_points: Students will be able to assess the methods used by Stalin to consolidate power, including political purges, show trials, and the cult of personality (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the economic and social impact of Stalin's policies, including collectivization, the Five-Year Plans, and the Great Terror.

history-ib-dp-dp-mao.name: 毛泽东时代的中国
history-ib-dp-dp-mao.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-mao.domain: 权威与威权 / Authoritarian States / Authoritarian States
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the methods used by Mao Zedong to consolidate power in China, including ideological campaigns and mass mobilization (HL/SL).
Students will be able to evaluate the social and economic impact of Mao's domestic policies, such as the Great Leap Forward and the Cultural Revolution.

history-ib-dp-dp-castro.name: 卡斯特罗的古巴
history-ib-dp-dp-castro.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-castro.domain: 权威与威权 / Authoritarian States / Authoritarian States
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the causes of the Cuban Revolution and evaluate the methods used by Fidel Castro to establish and maintain power (HL/SL).
Students will be able to assess the impact of Castro's domestic policies and Cuba's relationship with the superpowers during the Cold War.

history-ib-dp-dp-primary-sources.name: 一手史料
history-ib-dp-dp-primary-sources.subject: history
curriculum_points: Students will be able to extract and record relevant information from a variety of primary sources, demonstrating comprehension of their explicit meaning (HL/SL).
Students will be able to identify the origin, purpose, and content of primary sources as part of source analysis.

history-ib-dp-dp-source-evaluation.name: 史料评估（OPVL）
history-ib-dp-dp-source-evaluation.subject: history
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the value and limitations of historical sources, with reference to their origin, purpose, and content (HL/SL, Paper 1 skill).
Students will be able to analyse the tone, perspective, and argument presented in a source, placing it in its historical context.

history-ib-dp-dp-historians-views.name: 史学观点比较
history-ib-dp-dp-historians-views.subject: history
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare and contrast the arguments, perspectives, and methodologies of different historians on a specific historical issue (HL/SL).
Students will be able to explain how and why historical interpretations differ over time.

history-ib-dp-dp-historical-debate.name: 历史争鸣
history-ib-dp-dp-historical-debate.subject: history
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct a sustained argument that engages with historical debate, using evidence to support their analysis (HL/SL, Paper 2/3 skill).
Students will be able to evaluate the relative merits of different historical interpretations in forming their own reasoned conclusion.

history-ib-dp-dp-ia-investigation.name: IA 历史探究
history-ib-dp-dp-ia-investigation.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-ia-investigation.domain: IA 与 Paper / IA & Papers / IA & Papers
curriculum_points: Students will be able to formulate a clear, focused research question for an historical investigation on a topic of their choice (HL/SL Internal Assessment).
Students will be able to critically analyse a range of sources and historical perspectives in their investigation, leading to a substantiated conclusion.

history-ib-dp-dp-paper1-source.name: Paper 1 · 史料题
history-ib-dp-dp-paper1-source.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-paper1-source.domain: IA 与 Paper / IA & Papers / IA & Papers
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse and evaluate primary sources in response to structured questions, demonstrating understanding of origin, purpose, value, and limitation (HL/SL Exam).
Students will be able to synthesize information from multiple sources to construct a coherent short-term response.

history-ib-dp-dp-paper2-essay.name: Paper 2 · 专题论文
history-ib-dp-dp-paper2-essay.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-paper2-essay.domain: IA 与 Paper / IA & Papers / IA & Papers
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct two well-structured, analytical essays in response to prompts from two different world history topics (HL/SL Exam).
Students will be able to integrate specific, relevant historical evidence and explicit historiography to support a balanced argument.

history-ib-dp-dp-paper3-hl.name: Paper 3 · 区域专题（HL）
history-ib-dp-dp-paper3-hl.subject: history
history-ib-dp-dp-paper3-hl.domain: IA 与 Paper / IA & Papers / IA & Papers
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct three in-depth, analytical essays on aspects of the history of a specific region, demonstrating detailed knowledge and conceptual understanding (HL Exam).
Students will be able to evaluate different historical perspectives and interpretations in their essays, showing awareness of historiographical debate.

math-ib-dp-sequences-series.name: 数列与级数（等差/等比/Σ 求和）

math-ib-dp-exp-log.name: 指数与对数（换底/方程）
math-ib-dp-exp-log.subject: math
math-ib-dp-exp-log.domain: 主题 1 · 数与代数 Number and Algebra
curriculum_points:
SL 2.1
Content
Different forms of the equation of a straight line.
Gradient; intercepts.
Lines with gradients m1 and m2
Parallel lines m1=m2.
Perpendicular lines m1×m2= −1.

SL 2.2
Content
Concept of a function, domain, range and graph.
Function notation, for example f(x), v(t), C(n).
The concept of a function as a mathematical model.
Informal concept that an inverse function reverses or undoes the effect of a function.
Inverse function as a reflection in the line y=x, and the notation f−1(x).

SL 2.3
Content
The graph of a function; its equation y= f(x).
Creating a sketch from information given or a context, including transferring a graph from screen to paper.
Using technology to graph functions including their sums and differences.

SL 2.4
Content
Determine key features of graphs.
Maximum and minimum values; intercepts; symmetry; vertex; zeros of functions or roots of equations; vertical and horizontal asymptotes using graphing technology.
Finding the point of intersection of two curves or lines using technology.

math-ib-dp-binomial.name: 二项式定理
math-ib-dp-binomial.subject: math
math-ib-dp-binomial.domain: 主题 1 · 数与代数 Number and Algebra
curriculum_points:

Lines with gradients m1 and m2
Parallel lines m1=m2.
Perpendicular lines m1×m2= −1.

Function notation, for example f(x), v(t), C(n).
The concept of a function as a mathematical model.
Informal concept that an inverse function reverses or undoes the effect of a function.

Creating a sketch from information given or a context, including transferring a graph from screen to paper.

Determine key features of graphs.
Maximum and minimum values; intercepts; symmetry; vertex; zeros of functions or roots of equations; vertical and horizontal asymptotes using graphing technology.
Finding the point of intersection of two curves or lines using technology.

math-ib-dp-proof.name: 数学归纳法与证明（HL）
math-ib-dp-proof.subject: math
math-ib-dp-proof.domain: 主题 1 · 数与代数 Number and Algebra
curriculum_points:
SL 1.5

math-ib-dp-linear-quadratic.name: 一次函数与二次函数
math-ib-dp-linear-quadratic.subject: math
curriculum_points: The aim of the SL content of the number and algebra topic is to introduce students to numerical concepts and techniques which, combined with an introduction to arithmetic and geometric sequences and series, can be used for financial and other applications. Students will also be introduced to the formal concept of proof.

SL 1.2
Content
Arithmetic sequences and series.
Use of the formulae for the nth term and the sum of the first n terms of the sequence.
Use of sigma notation for sums of arithmetic sequences.
Applications.

SL 1.3
Content
Geometric sequences and series.
Use of the formulae for the n th term and the sum of the first n terms of the sequence.
Use of sigma notation for the sums of geometric sequences.
Applications.

SL 1.4
Content
Financial applications of geometric sequences and series:
• compound interest
• annual depreciation.

math-ib-dp-rational-functions.name: 有理函数与渐近线
math-ib-dp-rational-functions.subject: math
curriculum_points:
SL 1.7
Content Guidance, clarification and syllabus links
Laws of exponents with rational exponents. \(\frac{1}{a^{m}} = \sqrt[m]{a}\), if \(m\) is even this refers to the positive root.
For example: \(16^{\frac{3}{4}} = 8\).
Laws of logarithms. \(y = a^{x} \Leftrightarrow x = \log_{a} y\); \(\log_{a} a = 1\), \(\log_{a} 1 = 0\),
\(\log_{a} xy = \log_{a} x + \log_{a} y\) \(a, y \in \mathbb{N}, x \in \mathbb{Z}\)
\(\log_{a} \frac{x}{y} = \log_{a} x - \log_{a} y\)
\(\log_{a} x^{m} = m \log_{a} x\)
Examples: \(\log_{2} 4^{3} = \log_{2} 64 = 6\), \(\log_{2} 8 + \log_{2} 3 = \log_{2} 24\)
\(\log_{3} \frac{10}{4} = \log_{3} 10 - \log_{3} 4\)
\(\log_{4} 3^{5} = 5 \log_{4} 3\)
Link to: logarithmic and exponential graphs (SL2.9)
Change of base of a logarithm. \(\log_{4} 7 = \frac{\ln 7}{\ln 4}\)
\(\log_{a} x = \frac{\log_{b} x}{\log_{b} a}\), for \(a, b, x > 0\) Examples: \(\log_{25} 125 = \frac{\log_{5} 125}{\log_{5} 25} (= \frac{3}{2})\)
Solving exponential equations, including using \(x\)
logarithms. Examples: \((\frac{1}{3})^{x} = 9^{x+1}\), \(2^{x-1} = 10\).
Link to: using logarithmic and exponential graphs
(SL2.9).

SL 1.5
Content Guidance, clarification and syllabus links
Laws of exponents with integer exponents. Examples:
\(5^{3} \times 5^{-6} = 5^{-3}\), \(6^{4} \div 6^{3} = 6\), \((2^{3})^{-4} = 2^{-12}\),
\((2x)^{4} = 16x^{4}\), \(2x^{-3} = \frac{2}{x^{3}}\).
Introduction to logarithms with base 10 and e. Awareness that \(a^{x} = b\) is equivalent to \(\log_{a} b = x\),
Numerical evaluation of logarithms using that \(b > 0\), and \(\log_{e} x = \ln x\).
technology.

math-ib-dp-vectors-hl.name: 向量、直线与平面（HL）
math-ib-dp-vectors-hl.subject: math
math-ib-dp-vectors-hl.domain: 主题 3 · 几何与三角 Geometry and Trigonometry
curriculum_points:
SL 2.1
Content
Different forms of the equation of a straight line.
Gradient; intercepts.
Lines with gradients m1 and m2
Parallel lines m1=m2.
Perpendicular lines m1×m2=−1.

SL 2.2
Content
Concept of a function, domain, range and graph.
Function notation, for example f(x), v(t), C(n).
The concept of a function as a mathematical model.
Informal concept that an inverse function reverses or undoes the effect of a function.
Inverse function as a reflection in the line y=x, and the notation f−1(x).

SL 2.3
Content
The graph of a function; its equation y= f(x).
Creating a sketch from information given or a context, including transferring a graph from screen to paper.
Using technology to graph functions including their sums and differences.

SL 2.4
Content
Determine key features of graphs.
Maximum and minimum values; intercepts; symmetry; vertex; zeros of functions or roots of equations; vertical and horizontal asymptotes using graphing technology.
Finding the point of intersection of two curves or lines using technology.

math-ib-dp-integration-apps.name: 积分应用（面积/体积/动力学）
math-ib-dp-integration-apps.subject: math
curriculum_points:
SL 1.6
Content Guidance, clarification and syllabus links
Simple deductive proof, numerical and algebraic; how to lay out a left-hand side to right-hand side (LHS to RHS) proof.
The symbols and notation for equality and identity.
LHS to RHS proofs require students to begin with the left-hand side expression and transform this using known algebraic steps into the expression on the right-hand side (or vice versa).
Example: Show that (x−3)2+5≡x2−6x+14.
Students will be expected to show how they can check a result including a check of their own results.

AHL 1.15
Content Guidance, clarification and syllabus links
Proof by mathematical induction.
Proof should be incorporated throughout the course where appropriate.
Mathematical induction links specifically to a wide variety of topics, for example complex numbers, differentiation, sums of sequences and divisibility.
Proof by contradiction.
Examples: Irrationality of 3; irrationality of the cube root of 5; Euclid’s proof of an infinite number of prime numbers; if a is a rational number and b is an irrational number, then a+b is an irrational number.
Use of a counterexample to show that a statement is not always true.
Example: Consider the set P of numbers of the form n2+41n+41, n∈ℕ, show that not all elements of P are prime.
Example: Show that the following statement is not always true: there are no positive integer solutions to the equation x2+y2=10.
It is not sufficient to state the counterexample alone. Students must explain why their example is a counterexample.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-number.name: 指数/科学计数法/近似
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-number.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-number.domain: 数与代数 / Number & Algebra / Number & Algebra
curriculum_points: Use and interpret scientific notation, decimal approximations, and significant figures to represent and communicate quantitative information accurately (AO2).
Apply rounding conventions and percentage error calculations to assess the reasonableness of solutions in applied contexts.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-sequences.name: 等差与等比数列
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-sequences.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-sequences.domain: 数与代数 / Number & Algebra / Number & Algebra
curriculum_points: Model real-world situations using arithmetic and geometric sequences and series, including applications in finance and growth (AO3).
Solve problems involving the nth term and the sum of n terms for arithmetic and geometric sequences.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-finance.name: 金融数学（复利/年金）
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-finance.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-finance.domain: 数与代数 / Number & Algebra / Number & Algebra
curriculum_points: Solve problems in financial mathematics involving compound interest, annuities, amortization, and depreciation using appropriate technology (AO3).
Interpret and calculate the real purchasing power of money, considering inflation, in financial decision-making.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-exp-log.name: 指数与对数
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-exp-log.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-exp-log.domain: 数与代数 / Number & Algebra / Number & Algebra
curriculum_points: Manipulate and solve exponential and logarithmic equations to model growth and decay phenomena, such as population growth and radioactive decay (AO2).
Sketch and interpret graphs of exponential and logarithmic functions, identifying key features such as asymptotes and intercepts.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-matrices-hl.name: 矩阵（HL）
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-matrices-hl.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-matrices-hl.domain: 数与代数 / Number & Algebra / Number & Algebra
curriculum_points: Perform operations with matrices, including addition, scalar multiplication, and matrix multiplication, and calculate determinants and inverses of 2x2 and 3x3 matrices (AO1).
Use matrices to represent and solve systems of linear equations, and apply them to transformations in two and three dimensions.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-linear-quad.name: 一次/二次函数
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-linear-quad.subject: math
curriculum_points: Solve systems of linear equations and quadratic equations, both analytically and graphically, and interpret the solutions in context (AO2).
Analyze and model relationships between variables using linear and quadratic functions, determining key features such as vertex and axis of symmetry.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-models.name: 指数/三角模型
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-models.subject: math
curriculum_points: Construct and interpret exponential and trigonometric models (sine and cosine) to represent periodic or growth/decay behavior in real-world data (AO3).
Use trigonometric models to solve problems involving periodic phenomena such as tides, seasonal temperatures, or harmonic motion.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-regression.name: 回归与拟合
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-regression.subject: math
curriculum_points: Use technology to perform linear, quadratic, exponential, and sinusoidal regression on bivariate data and interpret the parameters in context (AO3).
Assess the validity of a model by analyzing residuals and the coefficient of determination (R²).

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-logistic-hl.name: Logistic 模型（HL）
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-logistic-hl.subject: math
curriculum_points: Understand the characteristics of the logistic function and its use in modeling growth with a limiting factor or carrying capacity (AO3).
Fit a logistic model to data using technology and interpret the parameters, including the carrying capacity and point of inflection.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-3d-geom.name: 三维几何
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-3d-geom.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-3d-geom.domain: 几何与三角 / Geometry & Trig / Geometry & Trig
curriculum_points: Solve problems involving points, lines, and planes in three dimensions using vector and Cartesian forms (AO2).
Calculate angles between lines and planes, and distances from a point to a line or plane in three-dimensional space.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-voronoi.name: Voronoi 图
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-voronoi.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-voronoi.domain: 几何与三角 / Geometry & Trig / Geometry & Trig
curriculum_points: Construct and interpret Voronoi diagrams from a given set of sites, and determine equations of edges and vertices (AO2).
Apply Voronoi diagrams to solve optimization problems, such as finding the nearest neighbor or locating a new facility.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-trig-app.name: 三角应用
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-trig-app.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-trig-app.domain: 几何与三角 / Geometry & Trig / Geometry & Trig
curriculum_points: Apply trigonometric ratios and the sine and cosine rules to solve practical problems involving non-right-angled triangles, including navigation and surveying (AO3).
Use trigonometric functions to model and solve problems involving areas of triangles and lengths of arcs and sectors.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-graph-theory-hl.name: 图论（HL）
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-graph-theory-hl.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-graph-theory-hl.domain: 几何与三角 / Geometry & Trig / Geometry & Trig
curriculum_points: Understand and use terminology of graph theory, including vertices, edges, adjacency, and weight, and represent networks using adjacency matrices (AO1).
Apply algorithms, such as Kruskal's and Dijkstra's, to solve problems related to minimum spanning trees and shortest paths in weighted graphs.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-descriptive.name: 描述统计
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-descriptive.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-descriptive.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Prob
curriculum_points: Calculate, interpret, and compare descriptive statistics for univariate data, including measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and dispersion (range, interquartile range, variance, standard deviation) (AO2).
Construct and analyze box-and-whisker plots and cumulative frequency graphs to summarize and compare data sets.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-correlation.name: 相关与回归
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-correlation.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-correlation.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Prob
curriculum_points: Calculate and interpret the Pearson’s product-moment correlation coefficient (r) and the equation of the regression line y on x (AO2).
Make predictions using the regression line and understand the dangers of extrapolation and the distinction between correlation and causation.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-chi-squared.name: 卡方检验
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-chi-squared.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-chi-squared.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Prob
curriculum_points: Set up and perform a chi-squared test for independence, including calculating expected frequencies, degrees of freedom, and the p-value (AO3).
Interpret the result of a chi-squared test in context, understanding the meaning of statistical significance at a given level.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-normal-dist.name: 正态分布
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-normal-dist.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-normal-dist.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Prob
curriculum_points: Use the normal distribution to model continuous data, calculate probabilities using technology, and work with standardized values (z-scores) (AO2).
Apply the normal distribution to solve problems in context, including inverse normal calculations to find unknown mean or standard deviation.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-markov-hl.name: 马尔可夫链（HL）
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-markov-hl.subject: math
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-markov-hl.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Prob
curriculum_points: Represent transition states using matrices and use Markov chains to model probabilistic systems that change over time (AO3).
Find steady-state vectors and interpret their meaning in the context of long-term behavior of the system.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-rate-change.name: 变化率
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-rate-change.subject: math
curriculum_points: Interpret the derivative as an instantaneous rate of change in applied contexts, such as velocity, acceleration, and marginal cost (AO2).
Estimate rates of change from tables and graphs, and use difference quotients to approximate derivatives.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-deriv-basic.name: 基本导数
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-deriv-basic.subject: math
curriculum_points: Differentiate functions of the form f(x) = ax^n, sin x, cos x, e^x, and ln x, and apply rules for sums and constant multiples (AO1).
Use differentiation to find equations of tangents and normals, and to identify intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-integration.name: 积分入门
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-integration.subject: math
curriculum_points: Integrate functions of the form f(x) = ax^n, e^x, sin x, and cos x, and apply the fundamental theorem of calculus (AO1).
Use definite integration to calculate areas under curves and between curves, interpreting the result in an applied context.

math-ib-dp-dp-ai-diff-eq-hl.name: 微分方程（HL）
math-ib-dp-dp-ai-diff-eq-hl.subject: math
curriculum_points: Formulate and solve first order differential equations using separation of variables and the integrating factor method (AO3).
Model real-world phenomena, such as Newton's Law of Cooling or simple population models, using first order differential equations and interpret the solutions.

phy-ib-dp-kinematics.name: 运动学（位移/速度/加速度/SUVAT）
phy-ib-dp-kinematics.subject: physics
phy-ib-dp-kinematics.domain: 主题 A · 空间、时间与运动 Space, Time and Motion
curriculum_points:
• that the motion of bodies through space and time can be described and analysed in terms of position, velocity, and acceleration
• velocity is the rate of change of position, and acceleration is the rate of change of velocity
• the change in position is the displacement
• the difference between distance and displacement
• the difference between instantaneous and average values of velocity, speed and acceleration, and how to determine them
• the equations of motion for solving problems with uniformly accelerated motion as given by
\(s=\frac{u+v}{2}t\)
\(v=u+at\)
\(s=ut+\frac{1}{2}at^2\)
\(v^2=u^2+2as\)
• motion with uniform and non-uniform acceleration

• the behaviour of projectiles in the absence of fluid resistance, and the application of the equations of motion resolved into vertical and horizontal components
• the qualitative effect of fluid resistance on projectiles, including time of flight, trajectory, velocity, acceleration, range and terminal speed.

phy-ib-dp-forces-momentum.name: 力与动量（牛顿定律/冲量/守恒）
phy-ib-dp-forces-momentum.subject: physics
phy-ib-dp-forces-momentum.domain: 主题 A · 空间、时间与运动 Space, Time and Motion
curriculum_points:
Students should understand:
• that the motion of bodies through space and time can be described and analysed in terms of position, velocity, and acceleration
• velocity is the rate of change of position, and acceleration is the rate of change of velocity
• the change in position is the displacement
• the difference between distance and displacement
• the difference between instantaneous and average values of velocity, speed and acceleration, and how to determine them
• the equations of motion for solving problems with uniformly accelerated motion as given by
  u+v
  s= t
  2
  v=u+at
  1
  s=ut+ at2

A quantitative approach to projectile motion will be limited to situations where fluid resistance is absent or can be neglected.
The trajectory of projectile motion is parabolic in the absence of fluid resistance, but the equation of the trajectory is not required.
Familiarity with projectiles launched horizontally, at angles above, and at angles below the horizontal is required.
Projectile motion will only involve problems using a constant value of g close to the surface of the Earth.
Fluid resistance refers to the effects of gases and liquids on the motion of a body.

A.2 Forces and momentum
Guidance
Sketches and interpretations of free-body diagrams and a determination of the resultant force are for one- and two-dimensional situations only.
Forces should be labelled using commonly accepted names or symbols.
Newton’s first law will be applied to problems involving translational equilibrium.
Examples of Newton’s third law will include the identification of force pairs in various situations.
