Skill Description
Applying general mathematics • Use basic arithmetic and algebraic calculations to solve problems.
• Calculate areas and volumes for simple shapes.
• Carry out calculations involving decimals, fractions, percentages, ratios, reciprocals, exponents and trigonometric ratios.
• Carry out calculations involving logarithmic and exponential functions.
• Determine rates of change.
• Calculate mean and range.
• Use and interpret scientific notation (for example, 3.5 × 106).
• Select and manipulate equations.
• Derive relationships algebraically.
• Use approximation and estimation.
• Appreciate when some effects can be neglected and why this is useful.
• Compare and quote ratios, values and approximations to the nearest order of magnitude.
• Distinguish between continuous and discrete variables.
• Understand direct and inverse proportionality, as well as positive and negative relationships or correlations between variables.
• Determine the effect of changes to variables on other variables in a relationship.
• Calculate and interpret percentage change and percentage difference.
• Calculate and interpret percentage error and percentage uncertainty.
• Construct and use scale diagrams.
• Identify a quantity as a scalar or vector.

Skill Description
Graphing • Sketch graphs, with labelled but unscaled axes, to qualitatively describe trends.
• Construct and interpret tables, charts and graphs for raw and processed data including bar charts, pie charts, histograms, scatter graphs and line and curve graphs.
• Construct and interpret graphs using logarithmic scales.
• Plot linear and non-linear graphs showing the relationship between two variables with appropriate scales and axes.
• Draw lines or curves of best fit.
• Draw and interpret uncertainty bars.
• Extrapolate and interpolate graphs.
• Linearize graphs (only where appropriate).
• On a best-fit linear graph, construct lines of maximum and minimum gradients with relative accuracy (by eye) considering all uncertainty bars.
• Determining the uncertainty in gradients and intercepts.
• Interpret features of graphs including gradient, changes in gradient, intercepts, maxima and minima, and areas under the graph.

• Newton’s three laws of motion
• forces as interactions between bodies
• that forces acting on a body can be represented in a free-body diagram
• that free-body diagrams can be analysed to find the resultant force on a system
• the nature and use of the following contact forces
◦ normal force F is the component of the contact force acting perpendicular to the surface that counteracts the body
◦ surface frictional force F acting in a direction parallel to the plane of contact between a body and a surface, on a stationary body as given by F ≤μF or a body in motion as given by F =μF where μ and μ are the coefficients of static and dynamic friction respectively
◦ tension
◦ elastic restoring force F following Hooke’s law as given by F =–kx where k is the spring constant
◦ viscous drag force F acting on a small sphere opposing its motion through a fluid as given by F =6πηrv where η is the fluid viscosity, r is the radius of the sphere and v is the velocity of the sphere through the fluid
◦ buoyancy F acting on a body due to the displacement of the fluid as given by F = ρVg where V is the volume of fluid displaced
• the nature and use of the following field forces
◦ gravitational force F is the weight of the body and calculated is given by F =mg
◦ electric force F
◦ magnetic force F
• that linear momentum as given by p=mv remains constant unless the system is acted upon by a resultant external force
• that a resultant external force applied to a system constitutes an impulse J as given by J =FΔt where F is the average resultant force and Δt is the time of contact
• that the applied external impulse equals the change in momentum of the system
• Δp
that Newton’s second law in the form F=ma assumes mass is constant whereas F= allows for situations where mass is changing
• the elastic and inelastic collisions of two bodies
• explosions
• energy considerations in elastic collisions, inelastic collisions, and explosions
• that bodies moving along a circular trajectory at a constant speed experience an acceleration that is directed radially towards the centre of the circle—known as a centripetal acceleration as given by v2 4π2r
a= =ω2r=
r T2
• that circular motion is caused by a centripetal force acting perpendicular to the velocity
• that a centripetal force causes the body to change direction even if its magnitude of velocity may remain constant
• that the motion along a circular trajectory can be described in terms of the angular velocity ω which is related to the linear speed v by the equation as given by v= =ωr.

A.3 Work, energy and power
Guiding questions
How are concepts of work, energy and power used to predict changes within a system?
How can a consideration of energetics be used as a method to solve problems in kinematics?
How can transfer of energy be used to do work?
Understandings
Standard level and higher level: 8 hours
Students should understand:
• the principle of the conservation of energy
• that work done by a force is equivalent to a transfer of energy
• that energy transfers can be represented on a Sankey diagram
• that work W done on a body by a constant force depends on the component of the force along the line of displacement as given by W =Fs cos θ
• that work done by the resultant force on a system is equal to the change in the energy of the system
• that mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic energy, gravitational potential energy and elastic potential energy
• that in the absence of frictional, resistive forces, the total mechanical energy of a system is conserved
• that if mechanical energy is conserved, work is the amount of energy transformed between different forms of mechanical energy in a system, such as:
◦ 1 p2
the kinetic energy of translational motion as given by E = mv2=
k 2 2m
◦ the gravitational potential energy, when close to the surface of the Earth as given by ΔE =mgΔh
p
◦ the elastic potential energy as given by E = 1 k(Δx)2
H 2
• that power developed P is the rate of work done, or the rate of energy transfer, as given by ΔW
P= =Fv
Δt
• useful work out useful power out
efficiency η in terms of energy transfer or power as given by η= =
total work in total power in
• energy density of the fuel sources.

phy-ib-dp-atomic-structure.name: 原子结构与发射光谱
phy-ib-dp-atomic-structure.subject: physics

• the motion of bodies through space and time can be described and analysed in terms of position, velocity, and acceleration
• velocity is the rate of change of position, and acceleration is the rate of change of velocity
• the change in position is the displacement
• the difference between distance and displacement
• the difference between instantaneous and average values of velocity, speed and acceleration, and how to determine them
• the equations of motion for solving problems with uniformly accelerated motion as given by

tok-ib-dp-dp-knower-perspective.name: 知者视角
tok-ib-dp-dp-knower-perspective.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-knower-perspective.domain: 核心主题 / Core Theme / Core Theme
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the role of personal and shared knowledge, and evaluate how factors such as emotion, reason, and culture shape the knower's perspective.
Students will be able to reflect on their own perspectives as knowers and identify how their position in the world influences their understanding and claims.

tok-ib-dp-dp-knowledge-claims.name: 知识声明
tok-ib-dp-dp-knowledge-claims.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-knowledge-claims.domain: 核心主题 / Core Theme / Core Theme
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between shared and personal knowledge claims, and construct meaningful knowledge questions that arise from them.
Students will be able to evaluate the implications of asserting 'I know' versus 'I believe' in different contexts.

tok-ib-dp-dp-truth-belief.name: 真理与信念
tok-ib-dp-dp-truth-belief.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-truth-belief.domain: 核心主题 / Core Theme / Core Theme
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare and contrast theories of truth (e.g., correspondence, coherence, pragmatic) and analyse their applicability in different Areas of Knowledge.
Students will be able to critically examine the relationship between justification, evidence, belief, and truth.

tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-natural-sci.name: 自然科学
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-natural-sci.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-natural-sci.domain: 知识领域 / Areas of Knowledge / Areas of Knowledge
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate the roles of observation, experimentation, and falsification in the construction of scientific knowledge, considering the provisional nature of scientific claims.
Students will be able to analyse how paradigms, models, and technological advances shape the methods and scope of the natural sciences.

tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-human-sci.name: 人文科学
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-human-sci.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-human-sci.domain: 知识领域 / Areas of Knowledge / Areas of Knowledge
curriculum_points: Students will be able to assess the methodological challenges in the human sciences, such as the role of interpretation, the observer effect, and the difficulty of establishing causal laws.
Students will be able to compare the nature of evidence and explanation in the human sciences with that in the natural sciences.

tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-history.name: 历史
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-history.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-history.domain: 知识领域 / Areas of Knowledge / Areas of Knowledge
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how historians construct knowledge through the selection, interpretation, and synthesis of evidence from the past.
Students will be able to evaluate the challenges of historical objectivity, including the role of perspective, bias, and the influence of present values on historical narratives.

tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-math.name: 数学
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-math.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-math.domain: 知识领域 / Areas of Knowledge / Areas of Knowledge
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the roles of axiomatic systems, deductive reasoning, and proof in establishing mathematical certainty and truth.
Students will be able to discuss the relationship between abstract mathematical concepts and their application in the real world, considering whether mathematics is discovered or invented.

tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-arts.name: 艺术
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-arts.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-aok-arts.domain: 知识领域 / Areas of Knowledge / Areas of Knowledge
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the role of imagination, emotion, and perception in the creation and interpretation of artistic knowledge.
Students will be able to evaluate different criteria used to judge artistic value and knowledge, considering the tension between subjective interpretation and objective standards.

tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-technology.name: 技术与知识
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-technology.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-technology.domain: 可选主题 / Optional Themes / Optional Themes
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the impact of technological tools and media on the production, acquisition, and dissemination of knowledge across various AOKs.
Students will be able to evaluate ethical considerations and the potential for bias inherent in the algorithms and design of knowledge technologies.

tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-language.name: 语言与知识
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-language.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-language.domain: 可选主题 / Optional Themes / Optional Themes
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how language shapes thought, classification, and the communication of knowledge within and between communities.
Students will be able to evaluate the challenges of translation, ambiguity, and the use of metaphor in the pursuit of precise knowledge.

tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-politics.name: 政治与知识
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-politics.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-politics.domain: 可选主题 / Optional Themes / Optional Themes
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how power structures and ideologies influence the production, validation, and control of knowledge within societies.
Students will be able to evaluate the concept of 'official knowledge' and the role of education, media, and institutions in shaping public understanding.

tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-religion.name: 宗教与知识
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-religion.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-religion.domain: 可选主题 / Optional Themes / Optional Themes
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare the nature of faith-based knowledge claims with those based on evidence and reason, analysing their different foundations and methods of justification.
Students will be able to discuss the role of religious knowledge systems in providing frameworks for understanding the world and human experience.

tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-indigenous.name: 本土社会
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-indigenous.subject: tok
tok-ib-dp-dp-opt-indigenous.domain: 可选主题 / Optional Themes / Optional Themes
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse distinctive features of indigenous knowledge systems, such as holistic perspectives, oral tradition, and connections to land and community.
Students will be able to reflect on the implications of different knowledge systems for understanding issues of sustainability, identity, and cultural preservation.

tok-ib-dp-dp-tok-exhibition.name: TOK 展览（IA）
tok-ib-dp-dp-tok-exhibition.subject: tok
curriculum_points: Students will be able to select a prescribed title and curate an exhibition of three objects that effectively explore knowledge questions arising from it in a concrete, real-world context.
Students will be able to produce a written commentary that clearly justifies the selection of each object and explains its specific connection to the chosen title and relevant TOK concepts.

tok-ib-dp-dp-tok-essay.name: TOK Essay（1600 词）
tok-ib-dp-dp-tok-essay.subject: tok
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct a focused, coherent, and critical argument in response to a prescribed essay title, integrating analysis of specific Areas of Knowledge and relevant TOK concepts.
Students will be able to demonstrate independent thinking, precise use of TOK terminology, and effective evaluation of competing perspectives and implications in their essay.

art-ib-myp-myp-drawing-skills.name: 素描与观察
art-ib-myp-myp-drawing-skills.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-drawing-skills.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 1-3 will be able to employ a range of drawing techniques (e.g., contour, gesture, tonal rendering) to accurately represent form, space, and proportion.
Students in MYP years 4-5 will be able to select and refine drawing skills purposefully to develop a personal visual language and support artistic intention in their body of work.

art-ib-myp-myp-painting-media.name: 绘画媒材
art-ib-myp-myp-painting-media.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-painting-media.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to demonstrate technical competence in applying various painting media (e.g., watercolor, acrylic, gouache) to explore color theory, texture, and compositional effects.
Students will be able to make informed choices about painting materials and techniques to convey specific moods, concepts, or cultural contexts in their artwork.

art-ib-myp-myp-sculpture-3d.name: 雕塑与立体
art-ib-myp-myp-sculpture-3d.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-sculpture-3d.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 2-4 will be able to manipulate a variety of materials (e.g., clay, wire, found objects) using additive, subtractive, and construction techniques to create three-dimensional forms.
Students will be able to apply an understanding of spatial relationships, scale, and structural integrity to design and realize sculptural artworks.

art-ib-myp-myp-printmaking.name: 版画
art-ib-myp-myp-printmaking.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-printmaking.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to execute basic printmaking processes (e.g., relief, monotype) to produce multiple images, demonstrating an understanding of registration, inking, and editioning.
Students will be able to utilize the unique qualities of printmaking (e.g., repetition, variation) to explore pattern, sequence, or social commentary in their visual communication.

art-ib-myp-myp-digital-media.name: 数字媒体
art-ib-myp-myp-digital-media.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-digital-media.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 3-5 will be able to use digital tools and software for creating, manipulating, and presenting still and moving images, demonstrating digital literacy.
Students will be able to critically evaluate the role of digital media in contemporary art practice and society, applying this understanding to their own creative projects.

art-ib-myp-myp-art-history.name: 艺术史
art-ib-myp-myp-art-history.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-art-history.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and analyze key artworks, movements, and cultural contexts from different historical periods and global traditions.
Students will be able to investigate the connections between art historical knowledge and their own artistic development, using research to inform and contextualize their work.

art-ib-myp-myp-music-reading.name: 识谱
art-ib-myp-myp-music-reading.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 1-3 will be able to interpret and apply standard musical notation (pitch, rhythm, dynamics, articulation) in performance and composition tasks.
Students in MYP years 4-5 will be able to fluently read and notate musical ideas using appropriate conventions, facilitating collaboration and analysis.

art-ib-myp-myp-instrument.name: 乐器演奏
art-ib-myp-myp-instrument.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students will be able to develop technical proficiency on a chosen instrument or voice, demonstrating control over tone, intonation, and expressive techniques.
Students will be able to perform repertoire from diverse genres and cultures with stylistic awareness, both as a soloist and as part of an ensemble.

art-ib-myp-myp-composition.name: 创作与编曲
art-ib-myp-myp-composition.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students will be able to create original musical pieces by structuring and developing musical ideas (melody, rhythm, harmony, texture) using appropriate tools and technology.
Students will be able to apply knowledge of musical elements and structures to compose for specific purposes, audiences, or contexts, and reflect on their creative process.

art-ib-myp-myp-music-genres.name: 音乐体裁
art-ib-myp-myp-music-genres.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and describe the distinguishing characteristics, cultural origins, and historical development of a range of musical genres.
Students will be able to compare and contrast musical genres, evaluating how social, historical, and technological factors influence musical style and practice.

art-ib-myp-myp-acting-basics.name: 表演基础
art-ib-myp-myp-acting-basics.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 1-2 will be able to apply fundamental acting techniques (e.g., vocal projection, physical characterization, focus) in prepared and improvised scenarios.
Students will be able to use voice, movement, and gesture intentionally to create believable characters and communicate clear intentions on stage.

art-ib-myp-myp-scene-work.name: 剧目排练
art-ib-myp-myp-scene-work.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students in MYP years 3-5 will be able to analyze a scripted scene to make directorial and performance choices regarding character motivation, relationship, and staging.
Students will be able to collaborate effectively within an ensemble to rehearse, refine, and perform scenes, demonstrating responsiveness and stagecraft.

art-ib-myp-myp-theatre-history.name: 戏剧史
art-ib-myp-myp-theatre-history.subject: art
curriculum_points: Students will be able to trace the development of theatrical forms, conventions, and practitioners from classical traditions to contemporary practice.
Students will be able to investigate how theatre reflects and influences the society and culture in which it is created, and apply this understanding to their own dramatic work.

art-ib-myp-myp-artist-study.name: 艺术家研究
art-ib-myp-myp-artist-study.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-artist-study.domain: 回应与反思 / Responding & Reflection / Responding
curriculum_points: Students will be able to conduct in-depth research into the life, influences, techniques, and body of work of a selected artist, musician, or theatre practitioner.
Students will be able to critically analyze the work of studied practitioners to evaluate their impact and relevance, and draw connections to their own artistic practice.

art-ib-myp-myp-critique.name: 艺术评论
art-ib-myp-myp-critique.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-critique.domain: 回应与反思 / Responding & Reflection / Responding
curriculum_points: Students will be able to use appropriate arts terminology to describe, analyze, interpret, and evaluate artworks and performances (their own and others').
Students will be able to engage constructively in critique sessions, providing and applying feedback to refine artistic work in relation to stated intentions and criteria.

art-ib-myp-myp-process-journal.name: 过程日志
art-ib-myp-myp-process-journal.subject: art
art-ib-myp-myp-process-journal.domain: 回应与反思 / Responding & Reflection / Responding
curriculum_points: Students will be able to document their artistic process, research, experimentation, and reflections consistently in a journal, demonstrating the development of ideas over time.
Students will use the process journal to plan, record, and evaluate their work against the MYP Arts criteria, showing evidence of critical thinking and artistic growth.

design-ib-myp-myp-inquire-analyse.name: 探究与分析
design-ib-myp-myp-inquire-analyse.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-inquire-analyse.domain: 设计循环 / Design Cycle / Design Cycle
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to formulate a detailed design brief by identifying and analysing a need for a specific client or target audience.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to conduct and synthesize research from a range of primary and secondary sources to inform the development of a design specification.

design-ib-myp-myp-develop-ideas.name: 构思与方案
design-ib-myp-myp-develop-ideas.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-develop-ideas.domain: 设计循环 / Design Cycle / Design Cycle
curriculum_points: Students will be able to generate a range of feasible design ideas, using techniques such as annotation and sketching, that address the specifications outlined in the design brief.
Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to develop detailed planning drawings/diagrams and justify the selection of one final design against the specification.

design-ib-myp-myp-create-solution.name: 制作解决方案
design-ib-myp-myp-create-solution.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-create-solution.domain: 设计循环 / Design Cycle / Design Cycle
curriculum_points: Students will be able to competently use a range of techniques and equipment to construct a logical plan and create a high-quality prototype or product.
Students in MYP 2-5 will demonstrate technical skill and safety in the use of tools and materials, documenting the creation process with photographic evidence.

design-ib-myp-myp-evaluate.name: 评估与迭代
design-ib-myp-myp-evaluate.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-evaluate.domain: 设计循环 / Design Cycle / Design Cycle
curriculum_points: Students will be able to test their final product/solution against the original design specification and explain the success and limitations of the outcome.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to critically evaluate the impact of their solution on the client/target audience and propose detailed, realistic modifications for improvement.

design-ib-myp-myp-web-basics.name: 网页基础（HTML/CSS）
design-ib-myp-myp-web-basics.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-web-basics.domain: 数字设计 / Digital Design / Digital Design
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to explain the core components of web architecture (e.g., HTML, CSS, servers) and create a simple, functional multi-page website using basic tags and styling.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to design and develop a website that follows principles of user-centered design and digital accessibility.

design-ib-myp-myp-programming-intro.name: 编程入门
design-ib-myp-myp-programming-intro.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-programming-intro.domain: 数字设计 / Digital Design / Digital Design
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to use visual or text-based programming environments to create simple algorithms that solve defined problems, using constructs such as sequences, loops, and conditionals.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to design, write, debug, and document a functional program to address a specific need within a design project.

design-ib-myp-myp-ui-design.name: 用户界面设计
design-ib-myp-myp-ui-design.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-ui-design.domain: 数字设计 / Digital Design / Digital Design
curriculum_points: Students will be able to apply fundamental principles of user interface design (e.g., consistency, hierarchy, feedback) to develop low-fidelity wireframes for a digital product.
Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to justify their UI design choices in relation to the needs and characteristics of a specific user group.

design-ib-myp-myp-user-testing.name: 用户测试
design-ib-myp-myp-user-testing.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-user-testing.domain: 数字设计 / Digital Design / Digital Design
curriculum_points: Students will be able to plan and conduct simple user tests to gather qualitative and quantitative feedback on a prototype.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to analyse user testing data, identify patterns, and translate findings into specific design modifications.

design-ib-myp-myp-sketching.name: 设计草图
design-ib-myp-myp-sketching.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-sketching.domain: 产品设计 / Product Design / Product Design
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to use freehand sketching techniques, including annotation, to communicate design ideas and explore form.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to produce annotated, rendered sketches that effectively communicate aesthetic and functional details of a proposed solution.

design-ib-myp-myp-prototyping.name: 原型制作
design-ib-myp-myp-prototyping.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-prototyping.domain: 产品设计 / Product Design / Product Design
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct a physical or digital prototype that models key features of their final design idea for the purpose of testing and evaluation.
Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to select and justify appropriate prototyping methods (e.g., paper, 3D print, digital mock-up) based on the design context and testing goals.

design-ib-myp-myp-materials-tools.name: 材料与工具
design-ib-myp-myp-materials-tools.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-materials-tools.domain: 产品设计 / Product Design / Product Design
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to identify and describe the working properties of common materials (e.g., wood, polymers, textiles) and select appropriate tools for basic processing.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to justify their selection of specific materials, components, and tools based on functional requirements, availability, cost, and environmental impact.

design-ib-myp-myp-cad-basics.name: CAD 入门
design-ib-myp-myp-cad-basics.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-cad-basics.domain: 产品设计 / Product Design / Product Design
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 2-4 will be able to use basic CAD software tools to create and modify simple 2D drawings and 3D digital models.
Students in MYP 5 will be able to produce a set of annotated CAD drawings suitable for guiding the manufacture of a prototype, applying scale and dimensioning conventions.

design-ib-myp-myp-user-research.name: 用户研究
design-ib-myp-myp-user-research.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-user-research.domain: 研究与评估 / Research & Evaluation / Research & Evaluation
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to use simple methods (e.g., surveys, interviews, observation) to gather information about user needs and preferences.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to synthesize findings from user research to create detailed user personas and scenarios that drive the design specification.

design-ib-myp-myp-design-specs.name: 设计规范书
design-ib-myp-myp-design-specs.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-design-specs.domain: 研究与评估 / Research & Evaluation / Research & Evaluation
curriculum_points: Students will be able to develop a design specification with a list of clear, measurable criteria derived from the analysis of their design brief and research.
Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to prioritize specification criteria and justify how they address the needs of the client and the requirements of the problem.

design-ib-myp-myp-ethics-sustainability.name: 伦理与可持续
design-ib-myp-myp-ethics-sustainability.subject: design
design-ib-myp-myp-ethics-sustainability.domain: 研究与评估 / Research & Evaluation / Research & Evaluation
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to identify ethical and environmental considerations (e.g., sourcing, waste, inclusivity) relevant to a design context.
Students in MYP 4-5 will be able to critically evaluate a design solution's social and environmental impact and propose modifications that align with principles of sustainable design.

humanities-ib-myp-myp-ancient-civs.name: 古代文明
humanities-ib-myp-myp-ancient-civs.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Analyze the causes and consequences of the rise and fall of ancient civilizations, evaluating their enduring legacies on contemporary societies (AO2, AO3).
Investigate the social, political, and economic structures of selected ancient civilizations using primary and secondary sources to develop historical arguments (AO1, AO2).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-medieval-world.name: 中世纪世界
humanities-ib-myp-myp-medieval-world.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Evaluate the significance of social, religious, and political systems (e.g., feudalism, manorialism) in shaping medieval societies across different regions (AO3).
Assess the causes and effects of cross-cultural interactions and conflicts during the medieval period, such as the Crusades or the Silk Road exchanges (AO2).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-industrial-revolut.name: 工业革命
humanities-ib-myp-myp-industrial-revolut.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Analyze the complex causes and global consequences of the Industrial Revolution, evaluating its social, economic, and environmental impacts (AO2, AO3).
Investigate primary sources to construct arguments about the experiences of different social groups during the period of industrialization (AO1, AO2).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-world-wars.name: 两次世界大战
humanities-ib-myp-myp-world-wars.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Evaluate the interplay of long-term and short-term causes of the World Wars, and assess their geopolitical, social, and technological consequences (AO3).
Use a variety of historical sources to analyze different perspectives on the conduct and experience of total war, developing critical interpretations (AO1, AO2).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-cold-war.name: 冷战
humanities-ib-myp-myp-cold-war.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Analyze the ideological, political, and economic origins of the Cold War, and evaluate its manifestation as a global conflict through proxy wars and spheres of influence (AO2, AO3).
Investigate the social and cultural impacts of the Cold War on different nations and groups, using source analysis to support conclusions (AO1, AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-historical-sources.name: 史料分析
humanities-ib-myp-myp-historical-sources.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Demonstrate the ability to critically evaluate the origin, purpose, value, and limitation (OPVL) of different types of historical sources (AO1).
Synthesize information from multiple, often conflicting, sources to construct well-supported historical narratives and arguments (AO2).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-physical-geo.name: 自然地理
humanities-ib-myp-myp-physical-geo.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Explain how natural processes shape the physical characteristics of landscapes and environments, using geographic terminology and concepts (e.g., plate tectonics, geomorphological cycles) (AO1).
Analyze the complex interactions between physical geography and human activity, evaluating opportunities and constraints (AO2, AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-human-geo.name: 人文地理
humanities-ib-myp-myp-human-geo.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Investigate patterns and trends in human geography (e.g., cultural landscapes, economic activity, political organization) and explain the factors that produce them (AO1, AO2).
Evaluate the consequences of human geographic processes on places and populations at various scales (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-urbanization.name: 城市化
humanities-ib-myp-myp-urbanization.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Analyze the causes, processes, and spatial patterns of urbanization, including the growth of megacities and world cities (AO2).
Evaluate the environmental, social, and economic opportunities and challenges arising from urban growth, proposing sustainable strategies (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-climate-geo.name: 气候与环境
humanities-ib-myp-myp-climate-geo.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Explain the natural and enhanced greenhouse effect, analyzing the interrelationships between climate systems, biomes, and human societies (AO1, AO2).
Evaluate different responses (mitigation and adaptation) to climate change and environmental degradation, considering their feasibility and ethical dimensions (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-population-migrati.name: 人口与迁徙
humanities-ib-myp-myp-population-migrati.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Analyze global and regional demographic patterns using population pyramids, dependency ratios, and the Demographic Transition Model (AO2).
Evaluate the complex push-pull factors influencing migration flows and assess their consequences for source and destination regions (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-map-skills.name: 地图技能
humanities-ib-myp-myp-map-skills.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Demonstrate proficiency in using and creating maps, including the interpretation of topographic maps, thematic maps, and GIS data (AO1).
Apply geographic skills such as calculating scale, gradient, and area, and interpreting spatial patterns from maps to solve geographic problems (AO2).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-basic-economics.name: 经济学入门
humanities-ib-myp-myp-basic-economics.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Explain the fundamental economic problem of scarcity and the need for choice, illustrating concepts of opportunity cost, factors of production, and economic sectors (AO1).
Compare different economic systems (traditional, command, market, mixed) and evaluate their approaches to answering the basic economic questions (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-supply-demand.name: 供给与需求
humanities-ib-myp-myp-supply-demand.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Analyze how the interaction of market forces (supply and demand) determines equilibrium price and quantity in a competitive market (AO2).
Evaluate the causes and consequences of government interventions in markets, such as price controls, taxes, and subsidies (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-market-types.name: 市场类型
humanities-ib-myp-myp-market-types.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Compare and contrast the characteristics of different market structures (perfect competition, monopoly, oligopoly, monopolistic competition) (AO2).
Evaluate the impact of market structure on efficiency, choice, innovation, and the allocation of resources (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-global-trade.name: 全球贸易
humanities-ib-myp-myp-global-trade.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: Explain the theories of absolute and comparative advantage, analyzing their role in promoting patterns of international trade and specialization (AO1, AO2).
Evaluate the arguments for and against free trade and protectionism, considering their impacts on different stakeholders and sustainable development (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-human-rights.name: 人权
humanities-ib-myp-myp-human-rights.subject: humanities
humanities-ib-myp-myp-human-rights.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Investigate the origins, evolution, and codification of human rights, including key documents like the UDHR (AO1).
Analyze case studies of human rights challenges and evaluate the effectiveness of responses by state and non-state actors in upholding these rights (AO2, AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-globalization.name: 全球化
humanities-ib-myp-myp-globalization.subject: humanities
humanities-ib-myp-myp-globalization.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Analyze the economic, political, cultural, and technological dimensions of globalization and their interconnections (AO2).
Evaluate the impacts of globalization on individuals, communities, and nations, considering issues of equity, sustainability, and identity (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-sustainable-dev.name: 可持续发展
humanities-ib-myp-myp-sustainable-dev.subject: humanities
humanities-ib-myp-myp-sustainable-dev.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Explain the concept of sustainable development and analyze the tensions between its environmental, social, and economic pillars using models like the triple bottom line (AO1, AO2).
Evaluate strategies for sustainable development at local, national, and global scales, assessing their potential to meet the needs of present and future generations (AO3).

humanities-ib-myp-myp-conflict-peace.name: 冲突与和平
humanities-ib-myp-myp-conflict-peace.subject: humanities
humanities-ib-myp-myp-conflict-peace.domain: 全球议题 / Global Issues / Global Issues
curriculum_points: Analyze the causes of conflict (e.g., ideological, economic, ethnic, religious) at various scales, from interpersonal to international (AO2).
Evaluate the roles of different actors and strategies (e.g., diplomacy, international law, peacekeeping, reconciliation) in conflict resolution and peacebuilding (AO3).

english-ib-myp-myp-listen-phase1.name: Phase 1 · 基本听辨
english-ib-myp-myp-listen-phase1.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 1 (Listening): Listen and respond to simple songs, rhymes and stories. Listen and respond to simple instructions and routines. Listen and respond to simple questions.

english-ib-myp-myp-listen-phase3.name: Phase 3 · 主题听力
english-ib-myp-myp-listen-phase3.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 3 (Listening): Listen and respond to a range of spoken texts for different purposes. Listen and respond to a range of spoken texts for enjoyment. Listen and respond to a range of spoken texts for information.

english-ib-myp-myp-listen-phase5.name: Phase 5 · 复杂对话
english-ib-myp-myp-listen-phase5.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 5 (Listening): Listen and respond to a range of spoken texts for different purposes. Listen and respond to a range of spoken texts for enjoyment. Listen and respond to a range of spoken texts for information.

english-ib-myp-myp-listen-media.name: 媒体听力（广播/视频）
english-ib-myp-myp-listen-media.subject: english
curriculum_points: Visual language—viewing and presenting: View and critically analyse a range of visual texts, communicating understanding through oral, written and visual media.

english-ib-myp-myp-read-phase1.name: Phase 1 · 基础阅读
english-ib-myp-myp-read-phase1.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 1 (Reading): Read and understand a range of visual and written texts. Read and understand a range of visual and written texts for different purposes. Read and understand a range of visual and written texts for enjoyment.

english-ib-myp-myp-read-phase3.name: Phase 3 · 短篇阅读
english-ib-myp-myp-read-phase3.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 3 (Reading): Read and understand a range of visual and written texts. Read and understand a range of visual and written texts for different purposes. Read and understand a range of visual and written texts for enjoyment.

english-ib-myp-myp-read-phase5.name: Phase 5 · 文学与议论文
english-ib-myp-myp-read-phase5.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 5 (Reading): Read and understand a range of visual and written texts. Read and understand a range of visual and written texts for different purposes. Read and understand a range of visual and written texts for enjoyment.

english-ib-myp-myp-read-skimming.name: 略读与寻读
english-ib-myp-myp-read-skimming.subject: english
curriculum_points: Reading: Use a range of strategies to construct meaning, including using prior knowledge and experiences, making connections, predicting, questioning, skimming, scanning, and synthesizing.

english-ib-myp-myp-speak-phase1.name: Phase 1 · 自我介绍
english-ib-myp-myp-speak-phase1.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 1 (Speaking): Use language to communicate ideas, information and experiences. Use language to interact with others. Use language to express feelings and opinions.

english-ib-myp-myp-speak-phase3.name: Phase 3 · 日常交流
english-ib-myp-myp-speak-phase3.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 3 (Speaking): Use language to communicate ideas, information and experiences. Use language to interact with others. Use language to express feelings and opinions.

english-ib-myp-myp-speak-phase5.name: Phase 5 · 观点陈述
english-ib-myp-myp-speak-phase5.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 5 (Speaking): Use language to communicate ideas, information and experiences. Use language to interact with others. Use language to express feelings and opinions.

english-ib-myp-myp-pronunciation.name: 发音与语调
english-ib-myp-myp-pronunciation.subject: english
curriculum_points: Oral language—listening and speaking: Use appropriate pronunciation, intonation and volume when speaking.

english-ib-myp-myp-write-phase1.name: Phase 1 · 简单句型
english-ib-myp-myp-write-phase1.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 1 (Writing): Write to communicate ideas, information and experiences. Write to express feelings and opinions. Write to interact with others.

english-ib-myp-myp-write-phase3.name: Phase 3 · 段落写作
english-ib-myp-myp-write-phase3.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 3 (Writing): Write to communicate ideas, information and experiences. Write to express feelings and opinions. Write to interact with others.

english-ib-myp-myp-write-phase5.name: Phase 5 · 议论与分析
english-ib-myp-myp-write-phase5.subject: english
curriculum_points: Phase 5 (Writing): Write to communicate ideas, information and experiences. Write to express feelings and opinions. Write to interact with others.

english-ib-myp-myp-grammar-l2.name: 语法（二外）
english-ib-myp-myp-grammar-l2.subject: english
curriculum_points: Learning about language—as learners grow in their understanding of how language works.

english-ib-myp-myp-vocabulary-l2.name: 词汇积累
english-ib-myp-myp-vocabulary-l2.subject: english
curriculum_points: Learning about language—as learners grow in their understanding of how language works.

english-ib-myp-myp-target-culture.name: 目标语文化
english-ib-myp-myp-target-culture.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-target-culture.domain: 跨文化意识 / Intercultural Awareness / Intercultural Awareness
curriculum_points: Open-minded: They understand and appreciate their own cultures and personal histories, and are open to the perspectives, values and traditions of other individuals and communities.

english-ib-myp-myp-compare-culture.name: 文化比较
english-ib-myp-myp-compare-culture.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-compare-culture.domain: 跨文化意识 / Intercultural Awareness / Intercultural Awareness
curriculum_points: Open-minded: They understand and appreciate their own cultures and personal histories, and are open to the perspectives, values and traditions of other individuals and communities.

english-ib-myp-myp-intl-mindedness.name: 国际情怀
english-ib-myp-myp-intl-mindedness.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-intl-mindedness.domain: 跨文化意识 / Intercultural Awareness / Intercultural Awareness
curriculum_points: Acquisition of more than one language enriches personal development and helps facilitate international-mindedness.

english-ib-myp-myp-literal-comprehension.name: 字面理解
english-ib-myp-myp-literal-comprehension.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-literal-comprehension.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: "Learners listen, talk, read and write their way to negotiating new meanings and understanding new concepts."

english-ib-myp-myp-inference.name: 推断与隐含义
english-ib-myp-myp-inference.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-inference.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • make inferences and draw conclusions from information, ideas and values in texts • interpret and analyse information, ideas and/or opinions in texts • identify and explain the author’s purpose and point of view • identify and explain the use of language to create tone or atmosphere • identify and explain the use of language to influence the reader

english-ib-myp-myp-literary-devices.name: 文学手法（比喻/象征）
english-ib-myp-myp-literary-devices.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-literary-devices.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • identify and explain the use of literary devices, for example, imagery, figurative language, symbolism • identify and explain the use of language to create tone or atmosphere • identify and explain the use of language to influence the reader

english-ib-myp-myp-theme-analysis.name: 主题分析
english-ib-myp-myp-theme-analysis.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-theme-analysis.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • identify and explain the main ideas and supporting details in texts • identify and explain the author’s purpose and point of view • identify and explain the use of language to create tone or atmosphere • identify and explain the use of language to influence the reader

english-ib-myp-myp-genre-study.name: 文体辨析
english-ib-myp-myp-genre-study.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-genre-study.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: "For example, this may include: a series of books read as an author study; regional fairy tales as part of a unit of inquiry with a particular social studies emphasis; discussing a scientist’s biography or a newspaper article to front-load a science investigation; early years counting stories as reinforcement for mathematics development; and the comparison and practice of illustration techniques to encourage the development of art skills."

english-ib-myp-myp-context-culture.name: 语境与文化
english-ib-myp-myp-context-culture.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-context-culture.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: "Language is socially constructed and dependent on the number and nature of our social interactions and relationships."
"Acquisition of more than one language enriches personal development and helps facilitate international-mindedness."
"Exposure to and experience with languages, with all their richness and diversity, creates an inquisitiveness about life and learning, and a confidence about creating new social interactions."

english-ib-myp-myp-critical-reading.name: 批判性阅读
english-ib-myp-myp-critical-reading.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-critical-reading.domain: 阅读与文本分析 / Reading & Textual Analysis / Reading
curriculum_points: "It empowers the learner and provides an intellectual framework to support conceptual development and critical thinking."
"They exercise initiative in applying thinking skills critically and creatively to recognize and approach complex problems, and make reasoned, ethical decisions."

english-ib-myp-myp-narrative.name: 记叙文
english-ib-myp-myp-narrative.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-narrative.domain: 写作与创作 / Writing & Composition / Writing
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • write narratives that develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences • use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters • use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another • use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events

english-ib-myp-myp-descriptive.name: 描写文
english-ib-myp-myp-descriptive.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-descriptive.domain: 写作与创作 / Writing & Composition / Writing
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events • use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters • use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another

english-ib-myp-myp-persuasive.name: 议论与劝说文
english-ib-myp-myp-persuasive.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-persuasive.domain: 写作与创作 / Writing & Composition / Writing
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence • introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically • support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text • use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence • establish and maintain a formal style • provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented

english-ib-myp-myp-analytical-essay.name: 分析性散文
english-ib-myp-myp-analytical-essay.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-analytical-essay.domain: 写作与创作 / Writing & Composition / Writing
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content • introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension • develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples • use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts • use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic • establish and maintain a formal style • provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented

english-ib-myp-myp-research-writing.name: 研究型写作
english-ib-myp-myp-research-writing.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-research-writing.domain: 写作与创作 / Writing & Composition / Writing
curriculum_points: "They acquire the skills necessary to conduct inquiry and research and show independence in learning."

english-ib-myp-myp-creative-writing.name: 创意写作
english-ib-myp-myp-creative-writing.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-creative-writing.domain: 写作与创作 / Writing & Composition / Writing
curriculum_points: "They understand and express ideas and information confidently and creatively in more than one language and in a variety of modes of communication."

english-ib-myp-myp-oral-presentation.name: 口头陈述
english-ib-myp-myp-oral-presentation.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-oral-presentation.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Oral Communication
curriculum_points: "Oral language—listening and speaking"
"Learners listen, talk, read and write their way to negotiating new meanings and understanding new concepts."

english-ib-myp-myp-debate.name: 辩论
english-ib-myp-myp-debate.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-debate.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Oral Communication
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade-level topics, texts, and issues, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly • come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion • follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed • pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion • review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing

english-ib-myp-myp-active-listening.name: 主动聆听
english-ib-myp-myp-active-listening.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-active-listening.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Oral Communication
curriculum_points: "Oral language—listening and speaking"
"learning through language—as learners use language as a tool to listen, think, discuss and reflect on information, ideas and issues"

english-ib-myp-myp-discussion.name: 讨论与对话
english-ib-myp-myp-discussion.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-discussion.domain: 口语与聆听 / Speaking & Listening / Oral Communication
curriculum_points: "learning through language—as learners use language as a tool to listen, think, discuss and reflect on information, ideas and issues"

english-ib-myp-myp-grammar-syntax.name: 语法与句法
english-ib-myp-myp-grammar-syntax.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-grammar-syntax.domain: 语言运用 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: "learning about language—as learners grow in their understanding of how language works"

english-ib-myp-myp-vocabulary.name: 词汇与语域
english-ib-myp-myp-vocabulary.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-vocabulary.domain: 语言运用 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies • use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word’s position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase • use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede) • consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech • verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary)

english-ib-myp-myp-punctuation-mechanics.name: 标点与规范
english-ib-myp-myp-punctuation-mechanics.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-punctuation-mechanics.domain: 语言运用 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking • explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences • choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas • place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers • demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing • use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break • use an ellipsis to indicate an omission • spell correctly

english-ib-myp-myp-rhetoric.name: 修辞策略
english-ib-myp-myp-rhetoric.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-rhetoric.domain: 语言运用 / Language in Use / Language in Use
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • identify and explain the use of language to create tone or atmosphere • identify and explain the use of language to influence the reader • use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence • establish and maintain a formal style

english-ib-myp-myp-media-texts.name: 媒介文本分析
english-ib-myp-myp-media-texts.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-media-texts.domain: 媒介与跨文本 / Media & Intertextuality / Media Literacy
curriculum_points: "discussing a scientist’s biography or a newspaper article"

english-ib-myp-myp-visual-literacy.name: 视觉素养
english-ib-myp-myp-visual-literacy.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-visual-literacy.domain: 媒介与跨文本 / Media & Intertextuality / Media Literacy
curriculum_points: "Visual language—viewing and presenting"

"Literacy, including oral and visual literacy as well as the ability to read and write, becomes increasingly important as greater demands are placed on learners as participants in the learning process."

english-ib-myp-myp-digital-publishing.name: 数字出版
english-ib-myp-myp-digital-publishing.subject: english
english-ib-myp-myp-digital-publishing.domain: 媒介与跨文本 / Media & Intertextuality / Media Literacy
curriculum_points: Students will be able to: • use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently • interact and collaborate with others (e.g., articulate ideas, clarify thinking, and justify opinions) in a range of contexts and for a variety of purposes through speaking, writing, and presenting • use a range of technologies to create, edit and publish texts individually and collaboratively • apply a range of literary, visual and multimedia techniques to create particular effects • use appropriate language and style for particular purposes and audiences

curriculum_points: The aim of the SL content of the number and algebra topic is to introduce students to numerical concepts and techniques which, combined with an introduction to arithmetic and geometric sequences and series, can be used for financial and other applications. Students will also be introduced to the formal concept of proof.

Arithmetic sequences and series.
Use of the formulae for the nth term and the sum of the first n terms of the sequence.
Use of sigma notation for sums of arithmetic sequences.
Applications.

Geometric sequences and series.
Use of the formulae for the n th term and the sum of the first n terms of the sequence.
Use of sigma notation for the sums of geometric sequences.
Applications.

Financial applications of geometric sequences and series:
• compound interest
• annual depreciation.

The concept of a function as a mathematical model.
Informal concept that an inverse function reverses or undoes the effect of a function.
Inverse function as a reflection in the line y=x, and the notation f−1(x).

SL 2.3
Content
The graph of a function; its equation y= f(x).
Creating a sketch from information given or a context, including transferring a graph from screen to paper.
Using technology to graph functions including their sums and differences.

SL 2.4
Content
Determine key features of graphs.
Maximum and minimum values; intercepts; symmetry; vertex; zeros of functions or roots of equations; vertical and horizontal asymptotes using graphing technology.
Finding the point of intersection of two curves or lines using technology.

pe-ib-myp-myp-locomotor.name: 基本位移
pe-ib-myp-myp-locomotor.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-locomotor.domain: 运动技能 / Movement Skills / Movement Skills
curriculum_points: Analyze and apply fundamental locomotor skills (running, jumping, hopping, skipping) to demonstrate efficient and controlled movement in a variety of physical activities, linking to Criterion B: Movement Composition.

Evaluate the effectiveness of locomotor skill application in individual and group contexts, demonstrating an understanding of biomechanical principles (e.g., force, balance) to improve performance, as per Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

pe-ib-myp-myp-ball-skills.name: 球类技能
pe-ib-myp-myp-ball-skills.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-ball-skills.domain: 运动技能 / Movement Skills / Movement Skills
curriculum_points: Demonstrate proficiency in a range of manipulative skills (catching, throwing, striking, dribbling) with control and accuracy in isolated and applied settings, addressing Criterion D: Applying and Performing.

Adapt and transfer ball skills across different invasion, net/wall, and striking/fielding games, analyzing the similarities and differences in technique and application, which relates to Criterion C: Applying and Performing.

pe-ib-myp-myp-team-sports.name: 团队运动
pe-ib-myp-myp-team-sports.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-team-sports.domain: 运动技能 / Movement Skills / Movement Skills
curriculum_points: Collaborate effectively within a team to apply strategies and tactics in an invasion game (e.g., basketball, soccer), demonstrating communication, role-specific responsibilities, and adherence to rules, as assessed in Criterion D: Applying and Performing.

Analyze the social and personal benefits of participating in team sports, evaluating how cooperation, leadership, and conflict resolution contribute to a positive group dynamic and personal development, linking to Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

pe-ib-myp-myp-individual-sports.name: 个人项目（田径/游泳）
pe-ib-myp-myp-individual-sports.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-individual-sports.domain: 运动技能 / Movement Skills / Movement Skills
curriculum_points: Develop and execute a performance plan for an individual sport (e.g., athletics, swimming, tennis), focusing on refining technique, setting personal goals, and applying self-assessment strategies, in line with Criterion B: Planning for Performance.

Compare and contrast the physiological and psychological demands of different individual sports, explaining how training principles and mental preparation are adapted for specific activities, which connects to Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

pe-ib-myp-myp-gymnastics.name: 体操
pe-ib-myp-myp-gymnastics.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-gymnastics.domain: 运动技能 / Movement Skills / Movement Skills
curriculum_points: Compose and perform a sequence on floor or apparatus that demonstrates creativity, fluency, and control, incorporating a variety of skills (travel, rotation, balance, flight) and linking movements, as per Criterion B: Movement Composition.

Apply knowledge of safety principles, including spotting techniques and apparatus setup, while performing and analyzing gymnastic movements, demonstrating responsibility for self and others, assessed in Criterion D: Applying and Performing.

pe-ib-myp-myp-fitness-training.name: 体能训练
pe-ib-myp-myp-fitness-training.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-fitness-training.domain: 运动技能 / Movement Skills / Movement Skills
curriculum_points: Design, implement, and monitor a personal fitness training program based on the FITT principles (Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type) to achieve a specific health- or skill-related goal, demonstrating understanding of Criterion B: Planning for Performance.

Analyze the components of health-related fitness (cardiovascular endurance, muscular strength, muscular endurance, flexibility, body composition) and explain their interrelationship and importance for overall wellbeing, linking to Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

pe-ib-myp-myp-offense-defense.name: 攻守策略
pe-ib-myp-myp-offense-defense.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-offense-defense.domain: 策略与战术 / Tactics & Strategy / Tactics
curriculum_points: Apply offensive (e.g., creating space, penetration) and defensive (e.g., marking, zoning) principles in modified and full-sided games, adapting decisions in response to opponents' actions, as assessed in Criterion C: Applying and Performing.

Compare and contrast offensive and defensive strategies across different game categories (invasion, net/wall), explaining the tactical rationale behind specific formations and movements, which relates to Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

pe-ib-myp-myp-tactical-awareness.name: 战术意识
pe-ib-myp-myp-tactical-awareness.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-tactical-awareness.domain: 策略与战术 / Tactics & Strategy / Tactics
curriculum_points: Demonstrate tactical awareness by selecting and applying appropriate strategies (e.g., width and depth in attack, delay in defense) to solve problems posed during game play, addressing Criterion C: Applying and Performing.

Evaluate the effectiveness of tactical decisions in a game situation, using terminology such as 'advantage' and 'transition' to analyze performance and propose improvements, linking to Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

pe-ib-myp-myp-game-analysis.name: 比赛分析
pe-ib-myp-myp-game-analysis.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-game-analysis.domain: 策略与战术 / Tactics & Strategy / Tactics
curriculum_points: Use systematic observation and notation to analyze the key components (skills, strategies, rules) of a game performance, identifying strengths and areas for improvement for an individual or team, as per Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

Synthesize data from game analysis to formulate a detailed action plan for improving performance in a specific role or position, demonstrating the ability to apply findings, which connects to Criterion B: Planning for Performance.

pe-ib-myp-myp-nutrition.name: 营养与饮食
pe-ib-myp-myp-nutrition.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-nutrition.domain: 健康素养 / Health Literacy / Health
curriculum_points: Explain the functions and sources of major nutrients (carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, water) and analyze their role in supporting physical activity, health, and recovery, addressing Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

Critically evaluate personal dietary habits and create a nutrition plan that supports participation in a specific physical activity, considering energy requirements, hydration, and timing of intake, in line with Criterion B: Planning for Performance.

pe-ib-myp-myp-cardio-health.name: 心肺健康
pe-ib-myp-myp-cardio-health.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-cardio-health.domain: 健康素养 / Health Literacy / Health
curriculum_points: Investigate the structure and function of the cardiovascular system and explain the acute and long-term effects of exercise on heart rate, stroke volume, cardiac output, and blood pressure, linking to Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

Design and participate in a training program aimed at improving cardiovascular endurance, using methods such as continuous training, interval training, and circuit training, and monitor progress using relevant data (e.g., resting heart rate, recovery rate), as assessed in Criterion B: Planning for Performance.

pe-ib-myp-myp-mental-wellbeing.name: 心理健康
pe-ib-myp-myp-mental-wellbeing.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-mental-wellbeing.domain: 健康素养 / Health Literacy / Health
curriculum_points: They understand the importance of intellectual, physical and emotional balance to achieve personal well-being for themselves and others.

pe-ib-myp-myp-substance-awareness.name: 物质滥用防范
pe-ib-myp-myp-substance-awareness.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-substance-awareness.domain: 健康素养 / Health Literacy / Health
curriculum_points: Analyze the short- and long-term physical, social, and emotional effects of substance use (including alcohol, tobacco, and performance-enhancing drugs) on health, wellbeing, and athletic performance, addressing Criterion A: Knowing and Understanding.

Evaluate strategies for making informed, healthy choices regarding substance use and promote positive alternatives within a peer group or team setting, demonstrating an understanding of personal and social responsibility, which relates to Criterion D: Applying and Performing.

pe-ib-myp-myp-goal-setting.name: 目标设定
pe-ib-myp-myp-goal-setting.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-goal-setting.domain: 反思与责任 / Reflection & Responsibility / Reflection
curriculum_points: They are able to assess and understand their strengths and limitations in order to support their learning and personal development.

pe-ib-myp-myp-fair-play.name: 体育精神
pe-ib-myp-myp-fair-play.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-fair-play.domain: 反思与责任 / Reflection & Responsibility / Reflection
curriculum_points: They act with integrity and honesty, with a strong sense of fairness, justice and respect for the dignity of the individual, groups and communities.

pe-ib-myp-myp-self-assessment.name: 自我评估
pe-ib-myp-myp-self-assessment.subject: pe
pe-ib-myp-myp-self-assessment.domain: 反思与责任 / Reflection & Responsibility / Reflection
curriculum_points: They give thoughtful consideration to their own learning and experience. They are able to assess and understand their strengths and limitations in order to support their learning and personal development.

science-ib-myp-myp-cells.name: 细胞结构与功能
science-ib-myp-myp-cells.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to use a microscope to identify and describe the basic structures and functions of plant and animal cells, including the nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane, and chloroplasts.
Students will be able to explain the role of cells as the basic structural and functional units of living organisms, and describe the process of cellular division (mitosis) for growth and repair.

science-ib-myp-myp-human-body.name: 人体系统
science-ib-myp-myp-human-body.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 2-4 will be able to describe the structure and function of at least two major human body systems (e.g., digestive, respiratory, circulatory) and explain how they interact to maintain health.
Students will be able to investigate a lifestyle factor (e.g., diet, exercise) and analyze its impact on the efficient functioning of a chosen body system.

science-ib-myp-myp-genetics-intro.name: 遗传入门
science-ib-myp-myp-genetics-intro.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-4 will be able to define and use basic genetic terminology, including gene, allele, dominant, recessive, genotype, and phenotype.
Students will be able to construct and interpret simple Punnett squares to predict the probability of inherited traits in offspring.

science-ib-myp-myp-evolution-intro.name: 进化入门
science-ib-myp-myp-evolution-intro.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to explain the concept of natural selection as a mechanism for evolution, using specific examples of variation, adaptation, and environmental pressure.
Students will be able to evaluate different types of evidence (e.g., fossil records, anatomical similarities) that support the theory of evolution.

science-ib-myp-myp-ecology.name: 生态学
science-ib-myp-myp-ecology.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-4 will be able to construct and analyze food chains and webs to illustrate the flow of energy and interdependence of organisms within an ecosystem.
Students will be able to investigate an environmental factor (e.g., pH, temperature) and assess its impact on a specific population within a local ecosystem.

science-ib-myp-myp-matter-states.name: 物质与状态
science-ib-myp-myp-matter-states.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-3 will be able to describe the arrangement, movement, and energy of particles in the three common states of matter (solid, liquid, gas).
Students will be able to use the particle model to explain physical changes of state (e.g., melting, evaporation) in terms of energy transfer.

science-ib-myp-myp-atoms-molecules.name: 原子与分子
science-ib-myp-myp-atoms-molecules.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 2-4 will be able to describe the basic structure of an atom (protons, neutrons, electrons) and use the periodic table to identify elements by their atomic number and mass.
Students will be able to differentiate between atoms, elements, molecules, and compounds, and represent simple molecules using chemical formulas (e.g., H2O, CO2).

science-ib-myp-myp-chemical-reactions.name: 化学反应
science-ib-myp-myp-chemical-reactions.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to identify evidence of a chemical reaction (e.g., color change, gas production, temperature change) and distinguish it from a physical change.
Students will be able to write simple word equations and balanced chemical equations for common reactions, such as combustion or acid-metal reactions.

science-ib-myp-myp-acids-bases.name: 酸碱
science-ib-myp-myp-acids-bases.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-4 will be able to use the pH scale to classify solutions as acidic, basic, or neutral, and safely use indicators to test common household substances.
Students will be able to describe the properties of acids and bases, and explain the process of neutralization with examples from everyday life (e.g., indigestion remedies).

science-ib-myp-myp-forces-motion.name: 力与运动
science-ib-myp-myp-forces-motion.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 2-4 will be able to calculate average speed using the formula speed = distance/time and represent motion graphically using distance-time graphs.
Students will be able to identify and describe different types of forces (e.g., friction, gravity, magnetic) and explain their effects on the motion of objects using Newton's First Law.

science-ib-myp-myp-energy.name: 能量
science-ib-myp-myp-energy.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-4 will be able to identify different forms of energy (e.g., kinetic, potential, thermal, chemical) and describe energy transfers and transformations in simple systems.
Students will be able to apply the principle of conservation of energy to explain that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only changed from one form to another.

science-ib-myp-myp-waves.name: 波动
science-ib-myp-myp-waves.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to describe wave properties (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differentiate between transverse and longitudinal waves using examples like light and sound.
Students will be able to explain how the properties of waves (e.g., reflection, refraction) are applied in technologies such as mirrors, lenses, or ultrasound imaging.

science-ib-myp-myp-electricity.name: 电路与电磁
science-ib-myp-myp-electricity.subject: science
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to construct simple series and parallel circuits using schematic diagrams and explain the effect of adding components on current and brightness.
Students will be able to describe the relationship between magnetism and electricity, and explain the principle of electromagnetic induction in generators.

science-ib-myp-myp-hypothesis.name: 假设与变量
science-ib-myp-myp-hypothesis.subject: science
science-ib-myp-myp-hypothesis.domain: 科学探究 / Scientific Inquiry / Scientific Inquiry
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-5 will be able to formulate a clear, testable hypothesis that predicts a relationship between an independent and a dependent variable.
Students will be able to justify their hypothesis by referencing prior scientific knowledge or preliminary observations.

science-ib-myp-myp-experiment-design.name: 实验设计
science-ib-myp-myp-experiment-design.subject: science
science-ib-myp-myp-experiment-design.domain: 科学探究 / Scientific Inquiry / Scientific Inquiry
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 2-5 will be able to design a controlled experiment, identifying and justifying the control of variables (independent, dependent, controlled) to ensure a fair test.
Students will be able to select appropriate equipment and methods, and describe a logical, safe procedure to collect sufficient and relevant data.

science-ib-myp-myp-data-analysis.name: 数据分析
science-ib-myp-myp-data-analysis.subject: science
science-ib-myp-myp-data-analysis.domain: 科学探究 / Scientific Inquiry / Scientific Inquiry
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-5 will be able to process qualitative and/or quantitative data by constructing appropriate graphs (e.g., bar, line) and calculating means.
Students will be able to identify trends and patterns in data, draw conclusions supported by evidence, and evaluate the reliability of their method.

science-ib-myp-myp-scientific-writing.name: 科学写作
science-ib-myp-myp-scientific-writing.subject: science
science-ib-myp-myp-scientific-writing.domain: 科学探究 / Scientific Inquiry / Scientific Inquiry
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 1-5 will be able to structure a laboratory report with clear sections (aim, hypothesis, method, results, conclusion, evaluation).
Students will be able to communicate findings using scientific language and conventions, including proper units, terminology, and citations where appropriate.

science-ib-myp-myp-ethics-science.name: 科学伦理
science-ib-myp-myp-ethics-science.subject: science
science-ib-myp-myp-ethics-science.domain: 科技与社会 / Science & Society / Science & Society
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 3-5 will be able to discuss the ethical implications of a scientific development (e.g., genetic engineering, animal testing) by considering different stakeholder perspectives.
Students will be able to explain the importance of honesty, integrity, and peer review in the scientific process, and identify examples of ethical and unethical scientific conduct.

science-ib-myp-myp-environmental-sci.name: 环境科学
science-ib-myp-myp-environmental-sci.subject: science
science-ib-myp-myp-environmental-sci.domain: 科技与社会 / Science & Society / Science & Society
curriculum_points: Students in MYP 2-5 will be able to analyze human impacts (e.g., pollution, deforestation, climate change) on a specific ecosystem and propose scientifically sound mitigation strategies.
Students will be able to evaluate the sustainability of a resource management practice by considering its environmental, economic, and social impacts.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-oral-language.name: 口头表达
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-oral-language.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-oral-language.domain: 语言表达 / Language / Language Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to use spoken language to share ideas, ask questions, and describe experiences clearly and confidently in small group settings (K-2).
Students will be able to adapt their oral language for different purposes and audiences, such as giving an informative presentation or participating in a discussion (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-storytelling.name: 故事讲述
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-storytelling.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-storytelling.domain: 语言表达 / Language / Language Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and sequence the key elements (beginning, middle, end) of a story and retell it using appropriate expression (K-2).
Students will be able to create and present original narratives, using voice, gesture, and structure to engage an audience and convey meaning (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-poetry.name: 诗歌朗读与创作
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-poetry.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-poetry.domain: 语言表达 / Language / Language Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explore and identify simple patterns of sound (e.g., rhyme, rhythm, repetition) in age-appropriate poems (K-2).
Students will be able to compose original poetic pieces using figurative language and deliberate structural choices to express personal ideas and emotions (3-5).
