inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-drama-role.name: 戏剧与角色扮演
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-drama-role.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-drama-role.domain: 语言表达 / Language / Language Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to use role-play and simple dramatic conventions (e.g., freeze frames, hot-seating) to explore characters, feelings, and different perspectives (K-2).
Students will be able to collaborate to plan, rehearse, and present short devised scenes that communicate a clear message or story (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-creative-writing.name: 创意写作
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-creative-writing.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-creative-writing.domain: 语言表达 / Language / Language Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to generate, draft, and illustrate simple written texts for enjoyment, using familiar structures and imaginative ideas (K-2).
Students will be able to apply the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing) to produce structured creative texts in various genres (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-drawing.name: 绘画入门
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-drawing.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-drawing.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to experiment with a variety of tools and techniques (e.g., line, shape, texture) to represent observations and ideas visually (K-2).
Students will be able to use drawing as a tool for inquiry, planning, and detailed visual communication, explaining their artistic choices (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-color-theory.name: 色彩理论
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-color-theory.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-color-theory.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify primary and secondary colors and explore mixing them to create new hues (K-2).
Students will be able to investigate and apply color relationships (e.g., warm/cool, complementary) to evoke specific moods or effects in their artwork (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-painting.name: 绘画与材料
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-painting.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-painting.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explore the properties of different paints and application tools to create expressive marks and fill defined areas (K-2).
Students will be able to intentionally select and manipulate painting media and techniques to create artwork that communicates a personal response to a stimulus (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-sculpture.name: 雕塑与立体造型
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-sculpture.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-sculpture.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to manipulate and combine a range of materials (e.g., clay, cardboard, found objects) to create simple three-dimensional forms (K-2).
Students will be able to design and construct a sculpture, considering elements such as balance, form, and space, to represent an idea or concept (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-digital-art.name: 数字艺术
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-digital-art.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-digital-art.domain: 视觉艺术 / Visual Arts / Visual Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to use basic digital tools (e.g., drawing apps, cameras) to create, capture, and modify simple images (K-2).
Students will be able to create a digital art piece or composition, demonstrating an understanding of layering, editing, and digital effects to enhance expression (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rhythm.name: 节奏与律动
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rhythm.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rhythm.domain: 表演艺术 / Performing Arts / Performing Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and replicate simple rhythmic patterns using body percussion, voice, and simple instruments (K-2).
Students will be able to create and notate (using symbols or standard notation) rhythmic sequences to accompany music or movement pieces (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-music-basics.name: 音乐入门
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-music-basics.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-music-basics.domain: 表演艺术 / Performing Arts / Performing Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explore the elements of music (pitch, tempo, dynamics) through listening, singing, and playing tuned/untuned percussion (K-2).
Students will be able to compose and perform short melodic phrases, demonstrating an understanding of basic musical structure and notation (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-dance.name: 舞蹈表达
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-dance.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-dance.domain: 表演艺术 / Performing Arts / Performing Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explore and combine basic locomotor and non-locomotor movements in response to different stimuli (e.g., music, stories) (K-2).
Students will be able to choreograph and perform a short dance sequence that uses space, dynamics, and relationship to express an idea or emotion (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-theatre.name: 戏剧表演
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-theatre.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-theatre.domain: 表演艺术 / Performing Arts / Performing Arts
curriculum_points: Students will be able to participate in guided dramatic play and simple performances, understanding the role of an audience (K-2).
Students will be able to contribute to a collaborative theatre performance, taking on specific responsibilities (e.g., actor, designer, stagehand) and reflecting on the process (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-festivals.name: 节日与庆典
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-festivals.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-festivals.domain: 文化与信仰 / Culture & Beliefs / Culture & Beliefs
curriculum_points: We invite students, their families and friends to lead these events, whether it is reading stories to students in English or in another language, sharing food or celebrating festivals.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-symbols-meaning.name: 符号与意义
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-symbols-meaning.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-symbols-meaning.domain: 文化与信仰 / Culture & Beliefs / Culture & Beliefs
curriculum_points: Students will be able to recognize that symbols, signs, and images communicate meaning and identify examples from their own environment (K-2).
Students will be able to analyze how symbols are used in different cultural contexts and art forms to convey messages, beliefs, and values (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-myths-legends.name: 神话与传说
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-myths-legends.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-myths-legends.domain: 文化与信仰 / Culture & Beliefs / Culture & Beliefs
curriculum_points: Students will be able to listen to and retell myths or legends, identifying central characters and events (K-2).
Students will be able to compare myths and legends from different cultures, exploring how they explain natural phenomena, teach lessons, and express cultural identity (3-5).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-family-roles.name: 家庭角色
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-family-roles.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-family-roles.domain: 社会组织 / Social Organization / Social Organization
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-school-community.name: 学校社区
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-school-community.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-school-community.domain: 社会组织 / Social Organization / Social Organization
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-government.name: 政府与治理
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-government.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-government.domain: 社会组织 / Social Organization / Social Organization
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify the basic functions and structures of government (e.g., local, national) and explain how they serve the needs of a community. (Age 9-11)
Students will be able to investigate and compare different forms of governance within their own and other societies, using key concepts such as function and responsibility.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rules-laws.name: 规则与法律
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rules-laws.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rules-laws.domain: 社会组织 / Social Organization / Social Organization
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyze the purpose of rules and laws in different contexts (school, community) and explain the consequences of following or not following them. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to demonstrate an understanding of how rules and laws are created and changed to promote fairness, safety, and order in a society.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-needs-wants.name: 需求与欲望
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-needs-wants.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-needs-wants.domain: 经济活动 / Economic Systems / Economic Systems
curriculum_points: Students will be able to categorize items as needs or wants and explain how these choices impact personal and community resource allocation. (Age 5-7)
Students will be able to critically examine the influence of culture, advertising, and personal values on perceptions of needs and wants. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-goods-services.name: 商品与服务
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-goods-services.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-goods-services.domain: 经济活动 / Economic Systems / Economic Systems
curriculum_points: Students will be able to distinguish between goods (products) and services (actions) and identify examples of each within their local community. (Age 5-7)
Students will be able to map the journey of a good or service from production to consumer, identifying the roles of different community members. (Age 7-9)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-money-trade.name: 货币与贸易
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-money-trade.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-money-trade.domain: 经济活动 / Economic Systems / Economic Systems
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain the role of money as a medium of exchange and investigate simple forms of trade and barter. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to compare different currencies and explore basic concepts of international trade and its benefits. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-supply-demand.name: 供给与需求
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-supply-demand.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-supply-demand.domain: 经济活动 / Economic Systems / Economic Systems
curriculum_points: Students will be able to describe, using simple examples, how the price or availability of a good can be influenced by supply (how much there is) and demand (how many people want it). (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to conduct a basic investigation into a local market to observe the principles of supply and demand in action. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-work-jobs.name: 工作与职业
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-work-jobs.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-work-jobs.domain: 经济活动 / Economic Systems / Economic Systems
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explore a variety of occupations and describe how different jobs contribute to the functioning of a community. (Age 5-7)
Students will be able to analyze the interdependence of jobs within an economic system and the skills and education required for different roles. (Age 7-9)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-teams.name: 团队合作
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-teams.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-teams.domain: 组织与结构 / Organizations & Systems / Organizations
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify the characteristics of effective teamwork (e.g., cooperation, shared responsibility) and apply them in collaborative group tasks. (Age 5-7)
Students will be able to reflect on their own role within a team, evaluating how different roles and perspectives contribute to achieving a shared goal. (Age 7-9)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-leadership.name: 领导力
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-leadership.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-leadership.domain: 组织与结构 / Organizations & Systems / Organizations
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify different leadership styles and qualities, and recognize examples of positive leadership in school and community contexts. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to demonstrate leadership skills by planning and taking action on a shared issue, considering the perspectives of others. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-decision-making.name: 决策过程
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-decision-making.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-decision-making.domain: 组织与结构 / Organizations & Systems / Organizations
curriculum_points: Students will be able to use a simple decision-making model (e.g., identify options, consider consequences) to make informed, ethical choices in familiar situations. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to analyze how groups and organizations make decisions, exploring concepts of consensus, voting, and representation. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cities.name: 城市系统
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cities.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cities.domain: 组织与结构 / Organizations & Systems / Organizations
curriculum_points: Students will be able to investigate the key systems of an urban area (e.g., transportation, housing, waste management) and explain how they are interconnected. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to design a model or plan for a sustainable city, considering the balance between human needs and environmental impact. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-transport-modes.name: 交通方式
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-transport-modes.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-transport-modes.domain: 交通与通信 / Transport & Communication / Transport & Communication
curriculum_points: Students will be able to classify different modes of transport (land, water, air) and analyze their advantages and disadvantages for moving people and goods. (Age 5-7)
Students will be able to map and evaluate transport networks, considering factors like efficiency, cost, and environmental sustainability. (Age 7-9)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-communication.name: 沟通方式
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-communication.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-communication.domain: 交通与通信 / Transport & Communication / Transport & Communication
curriculum_points: Students will be able to compare past and present methods of communication and evaluate their impact on how people connect and share information. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to create and deliver a message using an appropriate medium for a specific audience and purpose, reflecting on its effectiveness. (Age 7-9)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-internet-basics.name: 互联网初识
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-internet-basics.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-internet-basics.domain: 交通与通信 / Transport & Communication / Transport & Communication
curriculum_points: Students will be able to explain, in simple terms, how the internet functions as a network for finding and sharing information. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to demonstrate digital citizenship by applying safe, ethical, and responsible practices when using online resources and communication tools. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-global-networks.name: 全球网络
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-global-networks.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-global-networks.domain: 交通与通信 / Transport & Communication / Transport & Communication
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify examples of global networks (e.g., trade, communication, transportation) and describe how they connect people and countries. (Age 7-9)
Students will be able to investigate a global issue (e.g., supply chains, news dissemination) to understand the complexity and interdependence of worldwide systems. (Age 9-11)

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-materials.name: 材料与性质
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-materials.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-materials.domain: 物理世界 / Physical World / Physical World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-2 will be able to investigate and describe the observable properties of common materials (e.g., texture, flexibility, absorbency) and classify them based on their characteristics.
Students in PYP 3-4 will be able to plan and conduct fair tests to determine how the properties of a material (e.g., strength, transparency, conductivity) make it suitable for a specific purpose.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-forces.name: 力与运动
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-forces.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-forces.domain: 物理世界 / Physical World / Physical World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-3 will be able to explore and demonstrate how pushes and pulls (forces) can change the speed, direction, or shape of an object.
Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to investigate and explain the relationship between force, mass, and movement, and identify examples of friction and gravity in everyday situations.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-light-sound.name: 光与声
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-light-sound.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-light-sound.domain: 物理世界 / Physical World / Physical World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 2-3 will be able to investigate how light and sound are produced, travel, and can be sensed, and explore how they can be reflected or blocked.
Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to construct models to demonstrate how light allows us to see objects and how sound is caused by vibrations, relating findings to real-world technologies.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-electricity.name: 电与磁
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-electricity.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-electricity.domain: 物理世界 / Physical World / Physical World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 3-4 will be able to build simple circuits using batteries, wires, and bulbs, and identify materials that are conductors or insulators of electricity.
Students in PYP 5 will be able to investigate how electricity is generated and transformed in their local environment, and propose ways to use it safely and responsibly.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-energy-forms.name: 能量形式
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-energy-forms.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-energy-forms.domain: 物理世界 / Physical World / Physical World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 3-5 will be able to identify different forms of energy (e.g., heat, light, kinetic, chemical) and provide examples of energy transfer and transformation in simple systems.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-living-things.name: 生物与非生物
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-living-things.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-living-things.domain: 生命世界 / Living World / Living World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-2 will be able to sort and classify objects as living or non-living based on the characteristics of living things (e.g., growth, reproduction, need for resources).

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-plants.name: 植物生长
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-plants.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-plants.domain: 生命世界 / Living World / Living World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-3 will be able to identify the basic structures of plants and investigate the conditions necessary for seed germination and plant growth.
Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to explain the process of photosynthesis in simple terms and analyze the interdependence between plants and other living things in an ecosystem.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-animals.name: 动物特征
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-animals.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-animals.domain: 生命世界 / Living World / Living World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-3 will be able to observe, compare, and contrast the external features and behaviors of animals, and group them based on observable characteristics.
Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to research and explain how specific physical and behavioral adaptations help animals survive in their habitats.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-human-body.name: 人体系统
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-human-body.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-human-body.domain: 生命世界 / Living World / Living World
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-life-cycles.name: 生命周期
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-life-cycles.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-life-cycles.domain: 生命世界 / Living World / Living World
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 2-4 will be able to observe, record, and represent the stages of life cycles for different living things (e.g., plants, insects, amphibians), identifying patterns of growth, change, and reproduction.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-weather.name: 天气现象
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-weather.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-weather.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-3 will be able to observe, measure (using simple tools), and record daily weather conditions, identifying patterns over time.
Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to analyze how the interaction of air, water, and solar energy causes different weather phenomena and extreme weather events.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-water-cycle.name: 水循环
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-water-cycle.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-water-cycle.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 3-4 will be able to describe and model the major processes of the water cycle (evaporation, condensation, precipitation) and explain its importance to living systems.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-earth-sun-moon.name: 日地月系统
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-earth-sun-moon.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-earth-sun-moon.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 3-5 will be able to use models to explain the cyclical patterns of day and night, and the apparent movement of the sun and moon across the sky, caused by the Earth's rotation.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-solar-system.name: 太阳系
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-solar-system.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-solar-system.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to describe the components of our solar system (sun, planets, moons) and compare celestial bodies based on their relative size, position, and composition.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rocks-soil.name: 岩石与土壤
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rocks-soil.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-rocks-soil.domain: 地球与宇宙 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 2-4 will be able to investigate and compare different types of rocks and soils, and explain how natural processes like weathering and erosion contribute to their formation and change.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-simple-machines.name: 简单机械
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-simple-machines.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-simple-machines.domain: 科学与技术 / Science & Technology / Science & Technology
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 3-5 will be able to identify and explore the six types of simple machines (lever, pulley, etc.), demonstrating how they make work easier by changing the force or direction of movement.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-technology-everyday.name: 生活中的技术
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-technology-everyday.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-technology-everyday.domain: 科学与技术 / Science & Technology / Science & Technology
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 1-3 will be able to identify technology in their daily lives and describe its purpose in solving problems or meeting human needs.
Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to analyze how a specific piece of technology works and evaluate its impact on individuals and communities.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-invention-impact.name: 发明与社会影响
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-invention-impact.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-invention-impact.domain: 科学与技术 / Science & Technology / Science & Technology
curriculum_points: Students in PYP 4-5 will be able to research a significant invention or innovation, explaining the scientific principles behind it and evaluating its positive and negative consequences on society and the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-habitats.name: 栖息地
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-habitats.subject: inquiry
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world (physical and biological) and human societies.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-food-chain.name: 食物链
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-food-chain.subject: inquiry
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world (physical and biological) and human societies.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-biodiversity.name: 生物多样性
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-biodiversity.subject: inquiry
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world (physical and biological) and human societies.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-ecosystem-balance.name: 生态平衡
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-ecosystem-balance.subject: inquiry
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world (physical and biological) and human societies.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-endangered.name: 濒危物种
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-endangered.subject: inquiry
curriculum_points: An inquiry into rights and responsibilities in the struggle to share finite resources with other people and with other living things; communities and the relationships within and between them.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-natural-resources.name: 自然资源
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-natural-resources.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-natural-resources.domain: 资源与可持续 / Resources & Sustainability / Resources
curriculum_points: An inquiry into rights and responsibilities in the struggle to share finite resources with other people and with other living things.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-renewable-energy.name: 可再生能源
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-renewable-energy.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-renewable-energy.domain: 资源与可持续 / Resources & Sustainability / Resources
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; how humans use their understanding of scientific principles; the impact of scientific and technological advances on society and on the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-recycling.name: 回收与循环
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-recycling.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-recycling.domain: 资源与可持续 / Resources & Sustainability / Resources
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the interconnectedness of human-made systems and communities; economic activities and their impact on humankind and the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-sustainability.name: 可持续发展
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-sustainability.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-sustainability.domain: 资源与可持续 / Resources & Sustainability / Resources
curriculum_points: An inquiry into rights and responsibilities in the struggle to share finite resources with other people and with other living things; economic activities and their impact on humankind and the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-water-resources.name: 水资源
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-water-resources.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-water-resources.domain: 资源与可持续 / Resources & Sustainability / Resources
curriculum_points: An inquiry into rights and responsibilities in the struggle to share finite resources with other people and with other living things.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-pollution.name: 污染与危害
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-pollution.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-pollution.domain: 环境问题 / Environmental Issues / Environmental Issues
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world and human societies; the impact of scientific and technological advances on society and on the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-climate-change.name: 气候变化
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-climate-change.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-climate-change.domain: 环境问题 / Environmental Issues / Environmental Issues
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world and human societies; how humans use their understanding of scientific principles.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-deforestation.name: 森林砍伐
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-deforestation.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-deforestation.domain: 环境问题 / Environmental Issues / Environmental Issues
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the natural world and its laws; the interaction between the natural world and human societies; the impact of human activities on the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-plastic-waste.name: 塑料污染
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-plastic-waste.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-plastic-waste.domain: 环境问题 / Environmental Issues / Environmental Issues
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the interconnectedness of human-made systems and communities; economic activities and their impact on humankind and the environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-fair-trade.name: 公平贸易
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-fair-trade.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-fair-trade.domain: 公平与和平 / Equity & Peace / Equity & Peace
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the interconnectedness of human-made systems and communities; societal decision-making; economic activities and their impact on humankind.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-global-citizenship.name: 全球公民
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-global-citizenship.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-global-citizenship.domain: 公平与和平 / Equity & Peace / Equity & Peace
curriculum_points: The IB aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect.
Students become active, compassionate and lifelong learners who understand that other people, with their differences, can also be right.

An inquiry into rights and responsibilities in the struggle to share finite resources; communities and the relationships within and between them; access to equal opportunities; peace and conflict resolution.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-peace-education.name: 和平教育
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-peace-education.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-peace-education.domain: 公平与和平 / Equity & Peace / Equity & Peace
curriculum_points: The IB aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world.

An inquiry into rights and responsibilities in the struggle to share finite resources; communities and the relationships within and between them; access to equal opportunities; peace and conflict resolution.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-location.name: 位置与方向
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-location.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-location.domain: 地理与空间 / Place & Geography / Place & Geography
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-maps.name: 地图基础
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-maps.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-maps.domain: 地理与空间 / Place & Geography / Place & Geography
curriculum_points: Students in Grade 3-4 will be able to create and interpret maps using conventional symbols, keys, and simple grid references to describe the location of places.
Students will demonstrate an understanding of scale and perspective by comparing different types of maps (e.g., political, physical, thematic) of the same region.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-landforms.name: 地形地貌
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-landforms.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-landforms.domain: 地理与空间 / Place & Geography / Place & Geography
curriculum_points: Students will investigate how natural processes (erosion, deposition, tectonic activity) shape Earth's physical features, and represent their findings through models or diagrams.
By the end of Grade 5, students will be able to analyze the relationship between specific landforms and human activity, explaining how people adapt to and modify their physical environment.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-climate-zones.name: 气候带
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-climate-zones.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-climate-zones.domain: 地理与空间 / Place & Geography / Place & Geography
curriculum_points: Students will classify global climate zones (e.g., tropical, temperate, polar) and identify their defining characteristics, such as temperature and precipitation patterns.
Learners will explore the connection between climate zones and biomes, and evaluate how climate influences the distribution of plants, animals, and human settlements.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-human-geography.name: 人文地理
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-human-geography.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-human-geography.domain: 地理与空间 / Place & Geography / Place & Geography
curriculum_points: Students will examine patterns of population distribution and density, formulating questions about the factors (e.g., resources, economic opportunity) that influence where people live.
Learners will compare different types of human settlements (rural, urban, suburban) and communicate how these communities are organized to meet human needs.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-time-concepts.name: 时间概念
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-time-concepts.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-time-concepts.domain: 时间与历史 / Time & History / Time & History
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-history.name: 个人历史
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-history.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-history.domain: 时间与历史 / Time & History / Time & History
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-timelines.name: 时间线与年表
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-timelines.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-timelines.domain: 时间与历史 / Time & History / Time & History
curriculum_points: Students will construct personal and historical timelines, sequencing events chronologically and using BCE/CE dating conventions appropriately for their age level.
Learners will use timelines as a tool to identify duration, concurrence, and gaps in historical records, developing a sense of chronology and periodization.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-ancient-civs.name: 古代文明
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-ancient-civs.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-ancient-civs.domain: 时间与历史 / Time & History / Time & History
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-historical-change.name: 历史变迁
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-historical-change.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-historical-change.domain: 时间与历史 / Time & History / Time & History
curriculum_points: Students will analyze cause and effect by investigating the reasons for, and consequences of, a significant historical event or technological discovery.
Learners will compare past and present situations, identifying continuity and change over time in aspects such as social organization, technology, and beliefs.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-home-journey.name: 家与旅程
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-home-journey.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-home-journey.domain: 探索与迁徙 / Exploration & Migration / Exploration & Migration
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-great-explorers.name: 伟大的探险家
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-great-explorers.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-great-explorers.domain: 探索与迁徙 / Exploration & Migration / Exploration & Migration
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-migration-causes.name: 迁徙原因
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-migration-causes.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-migration-causes.domain: 探索与迁徙 / Exploration & Migration / Exploration & Migration
curriculum_points: An inquiry into orientation in place and time; personal histories; homes and journeys; the discoveries, explorations and migrations of humankind; the relationships between, and the interconnectedness of, individuals and civilizations from local and global perspectives.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-diaspora.name: 移民与散居
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-diaspora.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-diaspora.domain: 探索与迁徙 / Exploration & Migration / Exploration & Migration
curriculum_points: Students will explore the concepts of migration and diaspora, investigating the reasons why groups of people move and the effects on both the communities they leave and those they join.
Learners will demonstrate an understanding of how diaspora communities maintain and adapt cultural traditions, contributing to cultural diversity in their new locations.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-trade-routes.name: 贸易路线
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-trade-routes.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-trade-routes.domain: 文明互鉴 / Civilizational Interaction / Civilizations
curriculum_points: Students will trace historical and contemporary trade routes (e.g., Silk Road, modern shipping lanes) and identify the goods, ideas, and technologies exchanged along them.
Learners will assess the social, economic, and environmental impacts of trade, considering concepts of interdependence and sustainability.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cultural-exchange.name: 文化交流
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cultural-exchange.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cultural-exchange.domain: 文明互鉴 / Civilizational Interaction / Civilizations
curriculum_points: Students will investigate how interaction between societies through trade, migration, or conquest leads to cultural diffusion and the exchange of ideas, arts, and innovations.
Learners will evaluate examples of cultural synthesis, demonstrating how elements from different cultures can combine to create new practices or forms of expression.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-world-heritage.name: 世界遗产
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-world-heritage.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-world-heritage.domain: 文明互鉴 / Civilizational Interaction / Civilizations
curriculum_points: Students will research sites designated as UNESCO World Heritage, explaining their cultural or natural significance and the reasons for their preservation.
Learners will develop and present a case for a local site to be considered for heritage protection, identifying its value to community identity and global heritage.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-self-awareness.name: 自我意识
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-self-awareness.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-self-awareness.domain: 身份认同 / Identity / Identity & Self
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-identity.name: 个人身份
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-identity.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-identity.domain: 身份认同 / Identity / Identity & Self
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cultural-identity.name: 文化身份
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cultural-identity.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-cultural-identity.domain: 身份认同 / Identity / Identity & Self
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-values-beliefs.name: 价值观与信念
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-values-beliefs.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-values-beliefs.domain: 身份认同 / Identity / Identity & Self
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-self-expression.name: 自我表达
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-self-expression.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-self-expression.domain: 身份认同 / Identity / Identity & Self
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the ways in which we discover and express ideas, feelings, nature, culture, beliefs and values; the ways in which we reflect on, extend and enjoy our creativity; our appreciation of the aesthetic.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-physical-health.name: 身体健康
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-physical-health.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-physical-health.domain: 健康与福祉 / Health & Wellbeing / Health & Wellbeing
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-mental-wellbeing.name: 心理福祉
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-mental-wellbeing.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-mental-wellbeing.domain: 健康与福祉 / Health & Wellbeing / Health & Wellbeing
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-nutrition.name: 营养与饮食
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-nutrition.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-nutrition.domain: 健康与福祉 / Health & Wellbeing / Health & Wellbeing
curriculum_points: Students in Early Years will be able to identify foods that contribute to a healthy body and explain their importance for growth and energy.
Students in Upper Primary will be able to analyze personal and cultural dietary choices, making connections to physical health and well-being as part of a balanced lifestyle.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-emotional-regulation.name: 情绪调节
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-emotional-regulation.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-emotional-regulation.domain: 健康与福祉 / Health & Wellbeing / Health & Wellbeing
curriculum_points: Students in Lower Primary will be able to identify and name a range of feelings in themselves and others, and demonstrate simple strategies (e.g., breathing, quiet time) to manage them.
Students will be able to reflect on the causes and effects of their emotions and develop a personal toolkit of positive coping strategies to promote their mental well-being.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-safety.name: 个人安全
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-safety.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-personal-safety.domain: 健康与福祉 / Health & Wellbeing / Health & Wellbeing
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify trusted adults in their community and articulate clear safety rules for different contexts, including digital environments.
Students in Middle/Upper Primary will be able to assess potential risks, assert their right to safety, and demonstrate responsible decision-making to protect themselves and others.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-family.name: 家庭关系
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-family.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-family.domain: 关系与社群 / Relationships & Community / Relationships
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-friendship.name: 友谊
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-friendship.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-friendship.domain: 关系与社群 / Relationships & Community / Relationships
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-community.name: 社区归属
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-community.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-community.domain: 关系与社群 / Relationships & Community / Relationships
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-empathy.name: 同理心
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-empathy.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-empathy.domain: 关系与社群 / Relationships & Community / Relationships
curriculum_points: Students in Early Years/Lower Primary will be able to recognize and describe the feelings and perspectives of others through stories, role-play, and shared experiences.
Students will be able to demonstrate empathy and compassion by taking thoughtful action to support the well-being of individuals and groups within their community.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-conflict-resolution.name: 冲突解决
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-conflict-resolution.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-conflict-resolution.domain: 关系与社群 / Relationships & Community / Relationships
curriculum_points: Students in Lower Primary will be able to use 'I' statements to express their feelings during a disagreement and listen actively to the perspective of others.
Students in Upper Primary will be able to apply a range of conflict resolution strategies, such as negotiation and mediation, to resolve interpersonal problems peacefully and fairly.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-children-rights.name: 儿童权利
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-children-rights.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-children-rights.domain: 权利与责任 / Rights & Responsibilities / Rights & Responsibilities
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-responsibilities.name: 个人责任
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-responsibilities.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-responsibilities.domain: 权利与责任 / Rights & Responsibilities / Rights & Responsibilities
curriculum_points: An inquiry into the nature of the self; beliefs and values; personal, physical, mental, social and spiritual health; human relationships including families, friends, communities and cultures; rights and responsibilities; what it means to be human.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-fairness.name: 公平与正义
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-fairness.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-fairness.domain: 权利与责任 / Rights & Responsibilities / Rights & Responsibilities
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify examples of fairness and unfairness in familiar situations and explain their reasoning using the language of rights and responsibilities.
Students will be able to analyze real-world scenarios to understand issues of justice, and propose actions that promote equity and inclusive practices within their sphere of influence.

inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-diversity.name: 多样性与包容
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-diversity.subject: inquiry
inquiry-ib-pyp-pyp-diversity.domain: 权利与责任 / Rights & Responsibilities / Rights & Responsibilities
curriculum_points: Students will be able to describe ways in which people are similar and different, appreciating the unique identities, abilities, and cultural backgrounds within their classroom.

Students will be able to investigate and respect diverse perspectives, beliefs, and family structures, and advocate for an inclusive environment where everyone feels valued and has a sense of belonging.

ext-3966191.name: 城市排水系统基础
ext-3966191.subject: other
curriculum_points: 用真实暴雨情境带学生理解城市排水系统的设计原理：雨水如何从屋顶到河道、为什么会内涝、如何用滑块和计算判断管径是否够用。

ext-b849401.name: 3D 打印工作流程与 G-code 基础
ext-b849401.subject: other
curriculum_points: 面向初中学生的项目式 STEM 课件，围绕 3D 打印从建模到 G-code 再到打印监控的完整流程，帮助学生把代码指令翻译为机器动作。

courses.name: 3D 打印工作流程与 G-code 基础

other-math-m-linear-function-inquiry-pho.name: 一次函数探究课：手机套餐怎么选最划算？
other-math-m-linear-function-inquiry-pho.subject: other
curriculum_points: 探究课试制：从学生自己的套餐选择问题出发，用一次函数表达式和图像比较手机套餐费用。

sci-e-3d-printing-blender.name: 3D建模入门：用Blender做3D打印模型
sci-e-3d-printing-blender.subject: other
curriculum_points: 面向小学生的3D建模基础课件，以制作3D打印模型为最终目标，通过动手操作Blender学习3D建模的核心概念和技能。

math-us-hs-expressions-hs.name: 代数表达式
math-us-hs-expressions-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-expressions-hs.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSA-SSE.A.1 Interpret expressions that represent a quantity in terms of its context. a. Interpret parts of an expression, such as terms, factors, and coefficients. b. Interpret complicated expressions by viewing one or more of their parts as a single entity. For example, interpret P(1+r)^n as the product of P and a factor not depending on P.

HSA-SSE.A.2 Use the structure of an expression to identify ways to rewrite it. For example, see x^4 – y^4 as (x^2)^2 – (y^2)^2, thus recognizing it as a difference of squares that can be factored as (x^2 – y^2)(x^2 + y^2).

math-us-hs-linear-eq-hs.name: 一次方程与不等式
math-us-hs-linear-eq-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-linear-eq-hs.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSA-REI.B.3 Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable, including equations with coefficients represented by letters.

HSA-CED.A.1 Create equations and inequalities in one variable and use them to solve problems. Include equations arising from linear and quadratic functions, and simple rational and exponential functions.
HSA-CED.A.4 Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest, using the same reasoning as in solving equations. For example, rearrange Ohm’s law V = IR to highlight resistance R.

math-us-hs-systems-linear.name: 线性方程组
math-us-hs-systems-linear.subject: math
math-us-hs-systems-linear.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSA-REI.C.5 Prove that, given a system of two equations in two variables, replacing one equation by the sum of that equation and a multiple of the other produces a system with the same solutions.
HSA-REI.C.6 Solve systems of linear equations exactly and approximately (e.g., with graphs), focusing on pairs of linear equations in two variables.

HSA-REI.C.7 Solve a simple system consisting of a linear equation and a quadratic equation in two variables algebraically and graphically. For example, find the points of intersection between the line y = –3x and the circle x^2 + y^2 = 3.

HSA-REI.D.11 Explain why the x-coordinates of the points where the graphs of the equations y = f(x) and y = g(x) intersect are the solutions of the equation f(x) = g(x); find the solutions approximately, e.g., using technology to graph the functions, make tables of values, or find successive approximations. Include cases where f(x) and/or g(x) are linear, polynomial, rational, absolute value, exponential, and logarithmic functions.

math-us-hs-exp-rules.name: 指数运算
math-us-hs-exp-rules.subject: math
math-us-hs-exp-rules.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSN-RN.A.1 Explain how the definition of the meaning of rational exponents follows from extending the properties of integer exponents to those values, allowing for a notation for radicals in terms of rational exponents. For example, we define 5^(1/3) to be the cube root of 5 because we want (5^(1/3))^3 = 5^(1/3·3) to hold, so (5^(1/3))^3 must equal 5.
HSN-RN.A.2 Rewrite expressions involving radicals and rational exponents using the properties of exponents.

math-us-hs-polynomials-hs.name: 多项式
math-us-hs-polynomials-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-polynomials-hs.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSA-APR.A.1 Understand that polynomials form a system analogous to the integers, namely, they are closed under the operations of addition, subtraction, and multiplication; add, subtract, and multiply polynomials.
HSA-APR.B.3 Identify zeros of polynomials when suitable factorizations are available, and use the zeros to construct a rough graph of the function defined by the polynomial.

HSA-APR.C.4 Prove polynomial identities and use them to describe numerical relationships. For example, the polynomial identity (x^2 + y^2)^2 = (x^2 – y^2)^2 + (2xy)^2 can be used to generate Pythagorean triples.

math-us-hs-factoring-hs.name: 因式分解
math-us-hs-factoring-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-factoring-hs.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSA-SSE.A.2 Use the structure of an expression to identify ways to rewrite it. For example, see x^4 – y^4 as (x^2)^2 – (y^2)^2, thus recognizing it as a difference of squares that can be factored as (x^2 – y^2)(x^2 + y^2).

HSA-SSE.B.3 Choose and produce an equivalent form of an expression to reveal and explain properties of the quantity represented by the expression. a. Factor a quadratic expression to reveal the zeros of the function it defines. b. Complete the square in a quadratic expression to reveal the maximum or minimum value of the function it defines. c. Use the properties of exponents to transform expressions for exponential functions. For example the expression 1.15^t can be rewritten as (1.15^(1/12))^(12t) ≈ 1.012^(12t) to reveal the approximate equivalent monthly interest rate if the annual rate is 15%.

math-us-hs-quadratic-hs.name: 一元二次方程
math-us-hs-quadratic-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-quadratic-hs.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSA-REI.B.4 Solve quadratic equations in one variable. a. Use the method of completing the square to transform any quadratic equation in x into an equation of the form (x – p)^2 = q that has the same solutions. Derive the quadratic formula from this form. b. Solve quadratic equations by inspection (e.g., for x^2 = 49), taking square roots, completing the square, the quadratic formula and factoring, as appropriate to the initial form of the equation. Recognize when the quadratic formula gives complex solutions and write them as a ± bi for real numbers a and b.

HSA-CED.A.1 Create equations and inequalities in one variable and use them to solve problems. Include equations arising from linear and quadratic functions, and simple rational and exponential functions.

math-us-hs-functions-alg.name: 函数基础
math-us-hs-functions-alg.subject: math
math-us-hs-functions-alg.domain: 代数 I / Algebra I / Algebra I
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.A.1 Understand that a function from one set (called the domain) to another set (called the range) assigns to each element of the domain exactly one element of the range. If f is a function and x is an element of its domain, then f(x) denotes the output of f corresponding to the input x. The graph of f is the graph of the equation y = f(x).
HSF-IF.A.2 Use function notation, evaluate functions for inputs in their domains, and interpret statements that use function notation in terms of a context.

HSF-IF.A.3 Recognize that sequences are functions, sometimes defined recursively, whose domain is a subset of the integers. For example, the Fibonacci sequence is defined recursively by f(0) = f(1) = 1, f(n+1) = f(n) + f(n-1) for n ≥ 1.

math-us-hs-complex-numbers-hs.name: 复数
math-us-hs-complex-numbers-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-complex-numbers-hs.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSN-CN.A.1 Know there is a complex number i such that i^2 = –1, and every complex number has the form a + bi with a and b real.
HSN-CN.A.2 Use the relation i^2 = –1 and the commutative, associative, and distributive properties to add, subtract, and multiply complex numbers.
HSN-CN.C.7 Solve quadratic equations with real coefficients that have complex solutions.
HSN-CN.C.8 (+) Extend polynomial identities to the complex numbers. For example, rewrite x^2 + 4 as (x + 2i)(x – 2i).

math-us-hs-polynomial-fn.name: 多项式函数
math-us-hs-polynomial-fn.subject: math
math-us-hs-polynomial-fn.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7 Graph functions expressed symbolically and show key features of the graph, by hand in simple cases and using technology for more complicated cases. c. Graph polynomial functions, identifying zeros when suitable factorizations are available, and showing end behavior.

HSF-BF.B.3 Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, k f(x), f(kx), and f(x + k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them.

math-us-hs-rational-fn-hs.name: 有理函数
math-us-hs-rational-fn-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-rational-fn-hs.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7 Graph functions expressed symbolically and show key features of the graph, by hand in simple cases and using technology for more complicated cases. d. (+) Graph rational functions, identifying zeros and asymptotes when suitable factorizations are available, and showing end behavior.

HSF-BF.B.3 Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, k f(x), f(kx), and f(x + k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them.

math-us-hs-exp-log-hs.name: 指数与对数
math-us-hs-exp-log-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-exp-log-hs.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7 Graph functions expressed symbolically and show key features of the graph, by hand in simple cases and using technology for more complicated cases. e. Graph exponential and logarithmic functions, showing intercepts and end behavior, and trigonometric functions, showing period, midline, and amplitude.

HSF-IF.C.8 Write a function defined by an expression in different but equivalent forms to reveal and explain different properties of the function. b. Use the properties of exponents to interpret expressions for exponential functions. For example, identify percent rate of change in functions such as y = (1.02)^t, y = (0.97)^t, y = (1.01)^(12t), y = (1.2)^(t/10), and classify them as representing exponential growth or decay.
HSF-BF.B.5 (+) Understand the inverse relationship between exponents and logarithms and use this relationship to solve problems involving logarithms and exponents.

math-us-hs-sequences-series-hs.name: 数列与级数
math-us-hs-sequences-series-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-sequences-series-hs.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSF-BF.A.1 Write a function that describes a relationship between two quantities. a. Determine an explicit expression, a recursive process, or steps for calculation from a context. b. Combine standard function types using arithmetic operations. For example, build a function that models the temperature of a cooling body by adding a constant function to a decaying exponential, and relate these functions to the model.
HSF-BF.A.2 Write arithmetic and geometric sequences both recursively and with an explicit formula, use them to model situations, and translate between the two forms.
HSA-SSE.B.4 Derive the formula for the sum of a finite geometric series (when the common ratio is not 1), and use the formula to solve problems. For example, calculate mortgage payments.

math-us-hs-conic-sections.name: 圆锥曲线
math-us-hs-conic-sections.subject: math
math-us-hs-conic-sections.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSG-GPE.A.1 Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.
HSG-GPE.A.2 Derive the equation of a parabola given a focus and directrix.
HSG-GPE.A.3 (+) Derive the equations of ellipses and hyperbolas given the foci, using the fact that the sum or difference of distances from the foci is constant.

math-us-hs-matrices-hs.name: 矩阵
math-us-hs-matrices-hs.subject: math
math-us-hs-matrices-hs.domain: 代数 II / Algebra II / Algebra II
curriculum_points: HSN-VM.C.6 Use matrices to represent and manipulate data, e.g., to represent payoffs or incidence relationships in a network.
HSN-VM.C.7 Multiply matrices by scalars to produce new matrices, e.g., as when all of the payoffs in a game are doubled.
HSN-VM.C.8 Add, subtract, and multiply matrices of appropriate dimensions.

HSN-VM.C.9 Understand that, unlike multiplication of numbers, matrix multiplication for square matrices is not a commutative operation, but still satisfies the associative and distributive properties.

HSN-VM.C.10 Understand that the zero and identity matrices play a role in matrix addition and multiplication similar to the role of 0 and 1 in the real numbers. The determinant of a square matrix is nonzero if and only if the matrix has a multiplicative inverse.
HSN-VM.C.11 Multiply a vector (regarded as a matrix with one column) by a matrix of suitable dimensions to produce another vector. Work with matrices as transformations of vectors.
HSN-VM.C.12 Work with 2 × 2 matrices as transformations of the plane, and interpret the absolute value of the determinant in terms of area.

math-us-hs-function-notation.name: 函数记号
math-us-hs-function-notation.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.A.2 Use function notation, evaluate functions for inputs in their domains, and interpret statements that use function notation in terms of a context.

HSF-BF.A.1 Write a function that describes a relationship between two quantities. a. Determine an explicit expression, a recursive process, or steps for calculation from a context. b. Combine standard function types using arithmetic operations. For example, build a function that models the temperature of a cooling body by adding a constant function to a decaying exponential, and relate these functions to the model.

math-us-hs-transformations-fn.name: 函数变换
math-us-hs-transformations-fn.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSF-BF.B.3 Identify the effect on the graph of replacing f(x) by f(x) + k, k f(x), f(kx), and f(x + k) for specific values of k (both positive and negative); find the value of k given the graphs. Experiment with cases and illustrate an explanation of the effects on the graph using technology. Include recognizing even and odd functions from their graphs and algebraic expressions for them.
HSF-IF.C.7 Graph functions expressed symbolically and show key features of the graph, by hand in simple cases and using technology for more complicated cases.

math-us-hs-inverse-fn.name: 反函数
math-us-hs-inverse-fn.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSF-BF.B.4 Find inverse functions. a. Solve an equation of the form f(x) = c for a simple function f that has an inverse and write an expression for the inverse. For example, f(x) =2 x^3 or f(x) = (x+1)/(x-1) for x ≠ 1.
HSF-BF.B.4b Verify by composition that one function is the inverse of another.
HSF-BF.B.4c Read values of an inverse function from a graph or a table, given that the function has an inverse.
HSF-BF.B.4d Produce an invertible function from a non-invertible function by restricting the domain.

math-us-hs-piecewise-fn.name: 分段函数
math-us-hs-piecewise-fn.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSF-IF.C.7 Graph functions expressed symbolically and show key features of the graph, by hand in simple cases and using technology for more complicated cases. b. Graph piecewise-defined functions, including step functions and absolute value functions.

math-us-hs-linear-models.name: 线性模型
math-us-hs-linear-models.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSA-CED.A.2 Create equations in two or more variables to represent relationships between quantities; graph equations on coordinate axes with labels and scales.

HSF-LE.A.1 Distinguish between situations that can be modeled with linear functions and with exponential functions. a. Prove that linear functions grow by equal differences over equal intervals, and that exponential functions grow by equal factors over equal intervals. b. Recognize situations in which one quantity changes at a constant rate per unit interval relative to another.

HSF-LE.A.2 Construct linear and exponential functions, including arithmetic and geometric sequences, given a graph, a description of a relationship, or two input-output pairs (include reading these from a table).
HSF-LE.B.5 Interpret the parameters in a linear or exponential function in terms of a context.

math-us-hs-quadratic-models.name: 二次模型
math-us-hs-quadratic-models.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSA-CED.A.2 Create equations in two or more variables to represent relationships between quantities; graph equations on coordinate axes with labels and scales.

HSF-IF.B.4 For a function that models a relationship between two quantities, interpret key features of graphs and tables in terms of the quantities, and sketch graphs showing key features given a verbal description of the relationship. Key features include: intercepts; intervals where the function is increasing, decreasing, positive, or negative; relative maximums and minimums; symmetries; end behavior; and periodicity.
HSF-IF.B.6 Calculate and interpret the average rate of change of a function (presented symbolically or as a table) over a specified interval. Estimate the rate of change from a graph.

math-us-hs-exp-models-hs.name: 指数增长模型
math-us-hs-exp-models-hs.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSF-LE.A.1 Distinguish between situations that can be modeled with linear functions and with exponential functions. a. Prove that linear functions grow by equal differences over equal intervals, and that exponential functions grow by equal factors over equal intervals. b. Recognize situations in which one quantity changes at a constant rate per unit interval relative to another. c. Recognize situations in which a quantity grows or decays by a constant percent rate per unit interval relative to another.

HSF-LE.A.2 Construct linear and exponential functions, including arithmetic and geometric sequences, given a graph, a description of a relationship, or two input-output pairs (include reading these from a table).
HSF-LE.A.3 Observe using graphs and tables that a quantity increasing exponentially eventually exceeds a quantity increasing linearly, quadratically, or (more generally) as a polynomial function.
HSF-LE.B.5 Interpret the parameters in a linear or exponential function in terms of a context.

math-us-hs-regression-hs.name: 回归分析
math-us-hs-regression-hs.subject: math
curriculum_points: HSS-ID.B.6 Represent data on two quantitative variables on a scatter plot, and describe how the variables are related. a. Fit a function to the data; use functions fitted to data to solve problems in the context of the data. Use given functions or choose a function suggested by the context. Emphasize linear, quadratic, and exponential models. b. Informally assess the fit of a function by plotting and analyzing residuals. c. Fit a linear function for a scatter plot that suggests a linear association.
HSS-ID.C.7 Interpret the slope (rate of change) and the intercept (constant term) of a linear model in the context of the data.
HSS-ID.C.8 Compute (using technology) and interpret the correlation coefficient of a linear fit.
HSS-ID.C.9 Distinguish between correlation and causation.

english-us-hs-literary-analysis-hs.name: 高级文学分析
english-us-hs-literary-analysis-hs.subject: english
english-us-hs-literary-analysis-hs.domain: 文学阅读 / Reading Literature / Reading Literature
curriculum_points: RL.11-12.1: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

RL.11-12.2: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.

RL.11-12.3: Analyze the impact of the author’s choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters are introduced and developed).

RL.11-12.5: Analyze how an author’s choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
RL.11-12.6: Analyze a case in which grasping a point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).

english-us-hs-american-lit.name: 美国文学

english-us-hs-american-lit.domain: 文学阅读 / Reading Literature / Reading Literature

english-us-hs-british-lit.name: 英国文学

curriculum_points: Analyze how British authors from different literary periods use structure, rhetoric, and figurative language to develop complex themes and critique societal norms.
Evaluate the historical and cultural contexts of seminal works of British literature to understand their influence on subsequent literary movements and contemporary perspectives.
