CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RL.11-12.4
CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RI.6.4
CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RI.7.4
CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RI.8.4

english-us-ms-conventions-ms.name: 标点与机械规范
english-us-ms-conventions-ms.subject: english
curriculum_points: L.6.2: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing. a. Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.* b. Spell correctly.

math-us-ms-ratios-ms.name: 比率
math-us-ms-ratios-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-ratios-ms.domain: 比率与比例 / Ratios & Proportions / Ratios & Proportions
curriculum_points: 6.RP.1 Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.
6.RP.2 Understand the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b with b ≠ 0, and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
6.RP.3 Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems, e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations.

math-us-ms-unit-rates.name: 单位率
math-us-ms-unit-rates.subject: math
math-us-ms-unit-rates.domain: 比率与比例 / Ratios & Proportions / Ratios & Proportions
curriculum_points: 6.RP.2 Understand the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b with b ≠ 0, and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
6.RP.3.b Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed.

math-us-ms-proportional-rel.name: 比例关系
math-us-ms-proportional-rel.subject: math
math-us-ms-proportional-rel.domain: 比率与比例 / Ratios & Proportions / Ratios & Proportions
curriculum_points: 6.RP.3 Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems, e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations.

6.RP.3.a Make tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole-number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.

math-us-ms-percent-ms.name: 百分比应用
math-us-ms-percent-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-percent-ms.domain: 比率与比例 / Ratios & Proportions / Ratios & Proportions
curriculum_points: 6.RP.3.c Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity); solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent.

math-us-ms-rational-ms.name: 有理数
math-us-ms-rational-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-rational-ms.domain: 数系 / Number System / Number System
curriculum_points: 6.NS.5 Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values; use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.

6.NS.6 Understand a rational number as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes familiar from previous grades to represent points on the line and in the plane with negative number coordinates.
6.NS.7 Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.

math-us-ms-negative-ms.name: 负数运算
math-us-ms-negative-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-negative-ms.domain: 数系 / Number System / Number System
curriculum_points: 6.NS.5 Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values; use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.
6.NS.6.c Find and position integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram; find and position pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
6.NS.7 Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.

math-us-ms-irrationals.name: 无理数
math-us-ms-irrationals.subject: math
math-us-ms-irrationals.domain: 数系 / Number System / Number System
curriculum_points: Define irrational numbers and distinguish them from rational numbers by their non-terminating, non-repeating decimal expansions.
Approximate the value of irrational numbers (e.g., π, √2) on a number line and use them to solve problems involving the Pythagorean Theorem.

math-us-ms-absolute-value.name: 绝对值
math-us-ms-absolute-value.subject: math
math-us-ms-absolute-value.domain: 数系 / Number System / Number System
curriculum_points: 6.NS.7.c Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world situation.
6.NS.7.d Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.

6.NS.8 Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.

math-us-ms-algebraic-expr-ms.name: 代数表达式
math-us-ms-algebraic-expr-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-algebraic-expr-ms.domain: 表达式与方程 / Expressions & Equations / Expressions & Equations
curriculum_points: 6.EE.1 Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.
6.EE.2 Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
6.EE.3 Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
6.EE.4 Identify when two expressions are equivalent (i.e., when the two expressions name the same number regardless of which value is substituted into them).

math-us-ms-one-var-eq.name: 一元方程
math-us-ms-one-var-eq.subject: math
math-us-ms-one-var-eq.domain: 表达式与方程 / Expressions & Equations / Expressions & Equations
curriculum_points: 6.EE.5 Understand solving an equation or inequality as a process of answering a question: which values from a specified set, if any, make the equation or inequality true? Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.

6.EE.6 Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving a real-world or mathematical problem; understand that a variable can represent an unknown number, or, depending on the purpose at hand, any number in a specified set.
6.EE.7 Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p, q and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.

math-us-ms-inequalities-ms.name: 不等式
math-us-ms-inequalities-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-inequalities-ms.domain: 表达式与方程 / Expressions & Equations / Expressions & Equations
curriculum_points: 6.EE.5 Understand solving an equation or inequality as a process of answering a question: which values from a specified set, if any, make the equation or inequality true? Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.

6.EE.8 Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities of the form x > c or x < c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.

math-us-ms-linear-eq-ms.name: 线性方程
math-us-ms-linear-eq-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-linear-eq-ms.domain: 表达式与方程 / Expressions & Equations / Expressions & Equations
curriculum_points: 6.EE.7 Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p, q and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.

math-us-ms-systems-ms.name: 方程组
math-us-ms-systems-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-systems-ms.domain: 表达式与方程 / Expressions & Equations / Expressions & Equations
curriculum_points: Solve systems of two linear equations in two variables algebraically (substitution, elimination) and graphically, interpreting the solution as a point of intersection.
Construct and analyze a simple system of equations to model and solve real-world mathematical problems.

math-us-ms-exponents-ms.name: 幂与指数
math-us-ms-exponents-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-exponents-ms.domain: 表达式与方程 / Expressions & Equations / Expressions & Equations
curriculum_points: 6.EE.1 Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.

math-us-ms-area-volume-ms.name: 面积与体积
math-us-ms-area-volume-ms.subject: math
curriculum_points: 6.G.1 Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

6.G.2 Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Apply the formulas V = l w h and V = b h to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

6.G.4 Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

math-us-ms-pythagoras-ms.name: 勾股定理
math-us-ms-pythagoras-ms.subject: math
curriculum_points: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in real-world and mathematical problems in two and three dimensions.
Use the converse of the Pythagorean Theorem to determine if a triangle is a right triangle.

math-us-ms-transformations-ms.name: 变换
math-us-ms-transformations-ms.subject: math
curriculum_points: Describe the effect of translations, rotations, reflections, and dilations on two-dimensional figures using coordinates, preserving congruence or similarity as appropriate.
Given a geometric figure, perform a sequence of transformations and describe a sequence that exhibits the congruence or similarity between two figures.

math-us-ms-similar-triangles.name: 相似三角形
math-us-ms-similar-triangles.subject: math
curriculum_points: Use the properties of similarity transformations to establish the Angle-Angle (AA) criterion for two triangles to be similar.
Apply similarity criteria for triangles to solve problems involving missing side lengths and to prove relationships in geometric figures.

math-us-ms-angles-ms.name: 角与平行线
math-us-ms-angles-ms.subject: math
curriculum_points: Use informal arguments to establish facts about the angle sum of triangles, exterior angles of triangles, and angles created when parallel lines are cut by a transversal.
Apply theorems about angles (e.g., vertical, adjacent, complementary, supplementary) to write and solve simple equations for an unknown angle in a figure.

math-us-ms-statistical-questions.name: 统计问题
math-us-ms-statistical-questions.subject: math
math-us-ms-statistical-questions.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Probability
curriculum_points: 6.SP.1 Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers. For example, “How old am I?” is not a statistical question, but “How old are the students in my school?” is a statistical question because one anticipates variability in students’ ages.

math-us-ms-data-distributions.name: 数据分布
math-us-ms-data-distributions.subject: math
math-us-ms-data-distributions.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Probability
curriculum_points: 6.SP.2 Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution which can be described by its center, spread, and overall shape.
6.SP.3 Recognize that a measure of center for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation describes how its values vary with a single number.
6.SP.4 Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots.

6.SP.5 Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context, such as by: a. Reporting the number of observations. b. Describing the nature of the attribute under investigation, including how it was measured and its units of measurement. c. Giving quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (interquartile range and/or mean absolute deviation), as well as describing any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered. d. Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context.

math-us-ms-sampling.name: 抽样
math-us-ms-sampling.subject: math
math-us-ms-sampling.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Probability
curriculum_points: Understand that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a sample of the population, and that generalizations from a sample are valid only if the sample is representative.
Generate and use random sampling to produce representative samples and support valid inferences about a population.

math-us-ms-probability-ms.name: 概率
math-us-ms-probability-ms.subject: math
math-us-ms-probability-ms.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Probability
curriculum_points: Understand that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1 that expresses the likelihood of the event occurring, with larger numbers indicating greater likelihood.
Approximate the probability of a simple event by collecting data and observing its long-run relative frequency, and predict the approximate relative frequency given the probability.

math-us-ms-compound-prob.name: 复合概率
math-us-ms-compound-prob.subject: math
math-us-ms-compound-prob.domain: 统计与概率 / Stats & Probability / Stats & Probability
curriculum_points: Represent sample spaces for compound events using methods such as organized lists, tables, and tree diagrams, and use the space to calculate probabilities.
Find probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation, distinguishing between independent and dependent events.

math-us-ms-function-concept.name: 函数概念
math-us-ms-function-concept.subject: math
curriculum_points: 6.EE.9 Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation. For example, in a problem involving motion at constant speed, list and graph ordered pairs of distances and times, and write the equation d = 65t to represent the relationship between distance and time.

math-us-ms-linear-functions.name: 线性函数
math-us-ms-linear-functions.subject: math
curriculum_points: 6.EE.9 Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation. For example, in a problem involving motion at constant speed, list and graph ordered pairs of distances and times, and write the equation d = 65t to represent the relationship between distance and time.

math-us-ms-function-models.name: 函数建模
math-us-ms-function-models.subject: math
curriculum_points: 6.EE.9 Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation. For example, in a problem involving motion at constant speed, list and graph ordered pairs of distances and times, and write the equation d = 65t to represent the relationship between distance and time.

science-us-ms-structure-matter.name: 物质结构
science-us-ms-structure-matter.subject: science
science-us-ms-structure-matter.domain: 物质科学 / Physical Sciences / Physical Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-PS1-1. Develop models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules and extended structures.
MS-PS1-3. Gather and make sense of information to describe that synthetic materials come from natural resources and impact society.
MS-PS1-4. Develop a model that predicts and describes changes in particle motion, temperature, and state of a pure substance when thermal energy is added or removed.

science-us-ms-chemical-reactions-ms.name: 化学反应
science-us-ms-chemical-reactions-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-chemical-reactions-ms.domain: 物质科学 / Physical Sciences / Physical Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-PS1-2. Analyze and interpret data on the properties of substances before and after the substances interact to determine if a chemical reaction has occurred.
MS-PS1-5. Develop and use a model to describe how the total number of atoms does not change in a chemical reaction and thus mass is conserved.
MS-PS1-6. Undertake a design project to construct, test, and modify a device that either releases or absorbs thermal energy by chemical processes.

science-us-ms-forces-motion-ms.name: 力与运动
science-us-ms-forces-motion-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-forces-motion-ms.domain: 物质科学 / Physical Sciences / Physical Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-PS2-1. Apply Newton’s Third Law to design a solution to a problem involving the motion of two colliding objects.
MS-PS2-2. Plan an investigation to provide evidence that the change in an object’s motion depends on the sum of the forces on the object and the mass of the object.
MS-PS2-3. Ask questions about data to determine the factors that affect the strength of electric and magnetic forces.
MS-PS2-4. Construct and present arguments using evidence to support the claim that gravitational interactions are attractive and depend on the masses of interacting objects.
MS-PS2-5. Conduct an investigation and evaluate the experimental design to provide evidence that fields exist between objects exerting forces on each other even though the objects are not in contact.

science-us-ms-energy-ms.name: 能量守恒
science-us-ms-energy-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-energy-ms.domain: 物质科学 / Physical Sciences / Physical Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-PS3-1. Construct and interpret graphical displays of data to describe the relationships of kinetic energy to the mass of an object and to the speed of an object.
MS-PS3-2. Develop a model to describe that when the arrangement of objects interacting at a distance changes, different amounts of potential energy are stored in the system.
MS-PS3-3. Apply scientific principles to design, construct, and test a device that either minimizes or maximizes thermal energy transfer.

MS-PS3-4. Plan an investigation to determine the relationships among the energy transferred, the type of matter, the mass, and the change in the average kinetic energy of the particles as measured by the temperature of the sample.
MS-PS3-5. Construct, use, and present arguments to support the claim that when the kinetic energy of an object changes, energy is transferred to or from the object.

science-us-ms-waves-ms.name: 波动
science-us-ms-waves-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-waves-ms.domain: 物质科学 / Physical Sciences / Physical Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-PS4-1. Use mathematical representations to describe a simple model for waves that includes how the amplitude of a wave is related to the energy in a wave.
MS-PS4-2. Develop and use a model to describe that waves are reflected, absorbed, or transmitted through various materials.

science-us-ms-electromagnetic.name: 电磁
science-us-ms-electromagnetic.subject: science
science-us-ms-electromagnetic.domain: 物质科学 / Physical Sciences / Physical Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-PS4-3. Integrate qualitative scientific and technical information to support the claim that digitized signals are a more reliable way to encode and transmit information than analog signals.

science-us-ms-cells-ms.name: 细胞
science-us-ms-cells-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-cells-ms.domain: 生命科学 / Life Sciences / Life Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-LS1-1. Conduct an investigation to provide evidence that living things are made of cells; either one cell or many different numbers and types of cells.
MS-LS1-2. Develop and use a model to describe the function of a cell as a whole and ways parts of cells contribute to the function.

science-us-ms-body-systems-ms.name: 人体系统
science-us-ms-body-systems-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-body-systems-ms.domain: 生命科学 / Life Sciences / Life Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-LS1-3. Use argument supported by evidence for how the body is a system of interacting subsystems composed of groups of cells.
MS-LS1-8. Gather and synthesize information that sensory receptors respond to stimuli by sending messages to the brain for immediate behavior or storage as memories.

science-us-ms-ecosystems-ms.name: 生态系统
science-us-ms-ecosystems-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-ecosystems-ms.domain: 生命科学 / Life Sciences / Life Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-LS2-1. Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence for the effects of resource availability on organisms and populations of organisms in an ecosystem.
MS-LS2-2. Construct an explanation that predicts patterns of interactions among organisms across multiple ecosystems.
MS-LS2-3. Develop a model to describe the cycling of matter and flow of energy among living and nonliving parts of an ecosystem.
MS-LS2-4. Construct an argument supported by empirical evidence that changes to physical or biological components of an ecosystem affect populations.
MS-LS2-5. Evaluate competing design solutions for maintaining biodiversity and ecosystem services.

science-us-ms-heredity-ms.name: 遗传
science-us-ms-heredity-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-heredity-ms.domain: 生命科学 / Life Sciences / Life Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-LS3-1. Develop and use a model to describe why structural changes to genes (mutations) located on chromosomes may affect proteins and may result in harmful, beneficial, or neutral effects to the structure and function of the organism.
MS-LS3-2. Develop and use a model to describe why asexual reproduction results in offspring with identical genetic information and sexual reproduction results in offspring with genetic variation.

science-us-ms-evolution-ms.name: 进化
science-us-ms-evolution-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-evolution-ms.domain: 生命科学 / Life Sciences / Life Sciences
curriculum_points: MS-LS4-1. Analyze and interpret data for patterns in the fossil record that document the existence, diversity, extinction, and change of life forms throughout the history of life on Earth under the assumption that natural laws operate today as in the past.

MS-LS4-2. Apply scientific ideas to construct an explanation for the anatomical similarities and differences among modern organisms and between modern and fossil organisms to infer evolutionary relationships.

MS-LS4-3. Analyze displays of pictorial data to compare patterns of similarities in the embryological development across multiple species to identify relationships not evident in the fully formed anatomy.

MS-LS4-4. Construct an explanation based on evidence that describes how genetic variations of traits in a population increase some individuals’ probability of surviving and reproducing in a specific environment.
MS-LS4-5. Gather and synthesize information about the technologies that have changed the way humans influence the inheritance of desired traits in organisms.
MS-LS4-6. Use mathematical representations to support explanations of how natural selection may lead to increases and decreases of specific traits in populations over time.

science-us-ms-earth-place-universe.name: 地球在宇宙中的位置
science-us-ms-earth-place-universe.subject: science
science-us-ms-earth-place-universe.domain: 地球与空间 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: MS-ESS1-1. Develop and use a model of the Earth-sun-moon system to describe the cyclic patterns of lunar phases, eclipses of the sun and moon, and seasons.
MS-ESS1-2. Develop and use a model to describe the role of gravity in the motions within galaxies and the solar system.
MS-ESS1-3. Analyze and interpret data to determine scale properties of objects in the solar system.

science-us-ms-earth-systems-ms.name: 地球系统
science-us-ms-earth-systems-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-earth-systems-ms.domain: 地球与空间 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: MS-ESS2-1. Develop a model to describe the cycling of Earth’s materials and the flow of energy that drives this process.
MS-ESS2-2. Construct an explanation based on evidence for how geoscience processes have changed Earth’s surface at varying time and spatial scales.
MS-ESS2-3. Analyze and interpret data on the distribution of fossils and rocks, continental shapes, and seafloor structures to provide evidence of past plate motions.
MS-ESS2-4. Develop a model to describe the cycling of water through Earth’s systems driven by energy from the sun and the force of gravity.

science-us-ms-weather-climate-ms.name: 天气与气候
science-us-ms-weather-climate-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-weather-climate-ms.domain: 地球与空间 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: MS-ESS2-5. Collect data to provide evidence for how the motions and complex interactions of air masses result in changes in weather conditions.
MS-ESS2-6. Develop and use a model to describe how unequal heating and rotation of the Earth cause patterns of atmospheric and oceanic circulation that determine regional climates.
MS-ESS3-5. Ask questions to clarify evidence of the factors that have caused the rise in global temperatures over the past century.

science-us-ms-human-impact-ms.name: 人类影响
science-us-ms-human-impact-ms.subject: science
science-us-ms-human-impact-ms.domain: 地球与空间 / Earth & Space / Earth & Space
curriculum_points: MS-ESS3-1. Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how the uneven distributions of Earth’s mineral, energy, and groundwater resources are the result of past and current geoscience processes.
MS-ESS3-2. Analyze and interpret data on natural hazards to forecast future catastrophic events and inform the development of technologies to mitigate their effects.
MS-ESS3-3. Apply scientific principles to design a method for monitoring and minimizing a human impact on the environment.
MS-ESS3-4. Construct an argument supported by evidence for how increases in human population and per-capita consumption of natural resources impact Earth’s systems.

science-us-ms-engineering-problem.name: 定义工程问题
science-us-ms-engineering-problem.subject: science
curriculum_points: MS-ETS1-1. Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.

science-us-ms-design-criteria.name: 设计准则
science-us-ms-design-criteria.subject: science
curriculum_points: MS-ETS1-2. Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.

science-us-ms-optimization.name: 优化设计
science-us-ms-optimization.subject: science
curriculum_points: MS-ETS1-3. Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
MS-ETS1-4. Develop a model to generate data for iterative testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.

humanities-us-ms-ancient-civilizations-m.name: 古代文明
humanities-us-ms-ancient-civilizations-m.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-ancient-civilizations-m.domain: 世界历史 / World History / World History
curriculum_points: Analyze the influence of geography on the development of political, economic, and social systems in river valley civilizations (e.g., Mesopotamia, Egypt, Indus Valley, China).
Evaluate primary and secondary sources to construct explanations about the cultural achievements and belief systems of ancient civilizations and their enduring legacies.

humanities-us-ms-classical-era.name: 古典时代
humanities-us-ms-classical-era.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-classical-era.domain: 世界历史 / World History / World History
curriculum_points: Compare and contrast the governmental structures, legal systems, and civic ideals of classical Greece and Rome, and assess their influence on later political thought.

humanities-us-ms-medieval-world-ms.name: 中世纪世界
humanities-us-ms-medieval-world-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-medieval-world-ms.domain: 世界历史 / World History / World History
curriculum_points: Explain how the interaction of religious institutions, feudal systems, and trade networks (e.g., Silk Roads, Trans-Saharan) shaped societies in Europe, Asia, and Africa during the medieval period.

humanities-us-ms-early-modern-ms.name: 近代早期
humanities-us-ms-early-modern-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-early-modern-ms.domain: 世界历史 / World History / World History
curriculum_points: Analyze the causes and global consequences of European exploration, including the Columbian Exchange and the development of the Atlantic World.

humanities-us-ms-modern-era-ms.name: 现代史
humanities-us-ms-modern-era-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-modern-era-ms.domain: 世界历史 / World History / World History
curriculum_points: Evaluate the causes and effects of major political revolutions (e.g., American, French, Haitian) and the rise of nationalism in shaping the modern world.

humanities-us-ms-colonial-america.name: 殖民地时期
humanities-us-ms-colonial-america.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-colonial-america.domain: 美国历史 / US History / US History
curriculum_points: Construct an argument, using evidence, about the development of self-government and representative institutions in the English colonies, and assess the impact of colonial interactions with Indigenous peoples.

humanities-us-ms-revolution-founding.name: 独立战争与建国
humanities-us-ms-revolution-founding.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-revolution-founding.domain: 美国历史 / US History / US History
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.8.6-8. Analyze ideas and principles contained in the founding documents of the United States, and explain how they influence the social and political system.

humanities-us-ms-civil-war-ms.name: 内战与重建
humanities-us-ms-civil-war-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-civil-war-ms.domain: 美国历史 / US History / US History
curriculum_points: Analyze the complex interplay of social, economic, and political factors that led to the Civil War, and evaluate the successes and failures of Reconstruction in achieving its goals.

humanities-us-ms-industrial-era-ms.name: 工业化时代
humanities-us-ms-industrial-era-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-industrial-era-ms.domain: 美国历史 / US History / US History
curriculum_points: Assess the social, economic, and environmental impacts of industrialization and urbanization, and analyze the resulting reform movements and labor organizations.

humanities-us-ms-constitution-ms.name: 宪法
humanities-us-ms-constitution-ms.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.3.6-8. Examine the origins, purposes, and impact of constitutions, laws, treaties, and international agreements.

humanities-us-ms-three-branches.name: 三权分立
humanities-us-ms-three-branches.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.4.6-8. Explain the powers and limits of the three branches of government, public officials, and bureaucracies at different levels in the United States and in other countries.

humanities-us-ms-bill-of-rights.name: 权利法案
humanities-us-ms-bill-of-rights.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.8.6-8. Analyze ideas and principles contained in the founding documents of the United States, and explain how they influence the social and political system.

humanities-us-ms-citizenship-ms.name: 公民权利与责任
humanities-us-ms-citizenship-ms.subject: humanities
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.2.6-8. Explain specific roles played by citizens (such as voters, jurors, taxpayers, members of the armed forces, petitioners, protesters, and office-holders).

humanities-us-ms-world-regions-ms.name: 世界区域
humanities-us-ms-world-regions-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-world-regions-ms.domain: 地理与经济 / Geography & Economics / Geography & Economics
curriculum_points: Apply the Five Themes of Geography (location, place, human-environment interaction, movement, region) to compare the physical and human characteristics of major world regions.

humanities-us-ms-cultural-geography.name: 文化地理
humanities-us-ms-cultural-geography.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-cultural-geography.domain: 地理与经济 / Geography & Economics / Geography & Economics
curriculum_points: Investigate how cultural diffusion, migration, and globalization shape and transform cultural landscapes, identities, and patterns of interaction.

humanities-us-ms-market-economy-ms.name: 市场经济
humanities-us-ms-market-economy-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-market-economy-ms.domain: 地理与经济 / Geography & Economics / Geography & Economics
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.4.6-8. Describe the role of competition in the determination of prices and wages in a market economy.

humanities-us-ms-global-economy-ms.name: 全球经济
humanities-us-ms-global-economy-ms.subject: humanities
humanities-us-ms-global-economy-ms.domain: 地理与经济 / Geography & Economics / Geography & Economics
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.6.6-8. Explain how changes in supply and demand cause changes in prices and quantities of goods and services, labor, credit, and foreign currencies.
